title: Main process crashes with SQLITE_BUSY on new APPLICATION_SHARED storage (.vscode-shared\sharedStorage\state.vscdb) when two instances run concurrently
document:
Main process crashes with SQLITE_BUSY on new APPLICATION_SHARED storage (.vscode-shared\sharedStorage\state.vscdb) when two instances run concurrently

## Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?
Yes (root-caused in core, not extension-related — see analysis below)

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open VS Code (stable, v1.127.0) normally.
2. While it's running, open a second, independent VS Code window/instance shortly after (e.g. a second `Code.exe` process launching close in time to the first).
3. Within a few minutes, one or both windows crash with an uncaught main-process exception.

## Observed behavior
`main.log` shows the application starts up fine and successfully creates/initializes the new application-shared storage database:



The failure happens ~4 minutes after successful startup, during an ordinary write **transaction** (not the initial DB open/creation), which strongly suggests a second, independent OS process was concurrently writing to the same `state.vscdb` file at that moment.

## Root cause analysis (from source, commit `4fe60c8b1c`, v1.127.0)

- `C:\Users\\.vscode-shared\sharedStorage\state.vscdb` is the new `APPLICATION_SHARED` storage scope added in #311317 ("Add `APPLICATION_SHARED` storage scope for cross-app state sharing"), implemented by `ApplicationSharedStorageMain` in [`src/vs/platform/storage/electron-main/storageMain.ts`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/platform/storage/electron-main/storageMain.ts). It is explicitly designed to be shared **across separate processes** (VS Code and a companion "Agents" app).
- The underlying `SQLiteStorageDatabase` in [`src/vs/base/parts/storage/node/storage.ts`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/base/parts/storage/node/storage.ts) only retries once on `SQLITE_BUSY` when **opening** the DB connection (`connect()`, 2s `BUSY_OPEN_TIMEOUT`), with this comment:

 > "SQLITE_BUSY should only arise if another process is locking the same DB we want to open at that time. This typically never happens because a DB connection is limited per window."

 That assumption no longer holds for `ApplicationSharedStorageMain`, since it is intentionally opened by multiple independent processes.
- Crucially, the ongoing `transaction()` method (used for every subsequent read/write after the DB is open) has **no busy-retry/backoff logic at all** — a `SQLITE_BUSY` there just calls `handleSQLiteError()` and rejects the promise. That rejection appears to end up as an unhandled rejection reaching `process.on('unhandledRejection')` in [`src/vs/platform/telemetry/electron-main/errorTelemetry.ts`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/platform/telemetry/electron-main/errorTelemetry.ts), logged as `[uncaught exception in main]`, and the process/window subsequently crashes.
- Additionally, `ApplicationSharedStorageMain.doCreate()` constructs `SQLiteStorageDatabase` without a `busyTimeout` option, so `PRAGMA busy_timeout` is never set for this shared DB — SQLite has no chance to wait/retry internally before surfacing `SQLITE_BUSY` on lock contention between the two processes.

## Suggested fix
- Set a `busyTimeout` (`PRAGMA busy_timeout=`) when opening `ApplicationSharedStorageMain`'s `SQLiteStorageDatabase`, since it is the one scope explicitly meant for multi-process access.
- Add retry-on-`SQLITE_BUSY` handling to `transaction()` (not just the initial `connect()`), at least for the `APPLICATION_SHARED` scope.
- Ensure a `SQLITE_BUSY` in this path can't escalate to an uncaught/unhandled exception that crashes the process.

## Environment
- VS Code version: 1.127.0 (stable), commit `4fe60c8b1cdac1c4c174f2fb180d0d758272d713`
- OS: Windows
- `sharedDataFolderName`: `.vscode-shared` (from `product.json`)

I searched existing issues for `SQLITE_BUSY`, `"database is locked"`, and `ApplicationSharedStorage` and didn't find an existing report of this specific crash — apologies in advance if this duplicates something I missed.
canonical: Concurrent access to the shared SQLite storage database can cause a busy lock error during a write transaction and crash the main process because the shared database lacks sufficient retry or timeout handling.

title: Enterprise-managed plugin (extraKnownMarketplaces/enabledPlugins) marked installed but plugin files never synced to cache_path
document:
Enterprise-managed plugin (extraKnownMarketplaces/enabledPlugins) marked installed but plugin files never synced to cache_path

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: Built-in with VS Code (bundles the Copilot CLI; no separate marketplace extension entry under installed extensions on this build)
- VS Code Version: 1.127.0 (commit 4fe60c8b1cdac1c4c174f2fb180d0d758272d713, arm64)
- OS Version: macOS 26.5.2 (Darwin 25.5.0, arm64)
- Feature: Enterprise-managed plugin/agent marketplace (`managed-settings.json` plugin standards)
- Selected model: N/A — not model related

## Summary

An enterprise-managed plugin registered through a custom marketplace (`extraKnownMarketplaces` + `enabledPlugins` in `managed-settings.json`) is reported as `installed: true` / `enabled: true` in `~/.copilot/config.json`, but its content is **never synced to disk**. The `cache_path` directory referenced in config does not exist, so none of the plugin's agents/skills ever load — and no error is surfaced to the user.

## Config structures (redacted)

**Enterprise `managed-settings.json`** — registers a private marketplace and auto-enables one of its plugins:

The same enablement is also reflected in VS Code's own settings (`settings.json`):

**Resulting entry written to `~/.copilot/config.json` → `installedPlugins`** — marked installed with a `cache_path` that never gets populated:

## Steps to Reproduce

1. As an enterprise owner, configure `managed-settings.json` with an `extraKnownMarketplaces` entry pointing to a private GitHub-hosted marketplace repo, and set `enabledPlugins` to auto-install one of that marketplace's plugins (`"@": true`).
2. Sign in to VS Code (whose Copilot Chat bundles the Copilot CLI) with an account under that enterprise, and wait for the client to pull the managed settings (docs say this happens ~once per hour).
3. The plugin auto-installs per policy. Inspect `~/.copilot/config.json`: the plugin appears under `installedPlugins` with `enabled: true`, a version string, and a `cache_path` (see structure above).
4. Inspect the filesystem: the `cache_path` directory referenced in config **does not exist** (`~/.copilot/installed-plugins//` contains only `.DS_Store`), and the mirrored `~/.copilot/plugin-data///` directory exists but is **empty** (0 files, stale mtime from a much earlier registration attempt).
5. Try to remove the plugin. Shortly after, `config.json` re-adds it with a *new* `installed_at` timestamp (re-enforced by the enterprise policy) — but the cache directory is still never populated.
6. For comparison, the default `github-awesome-copilot` marketplace *does* fully populate `~/.copilot/marketplace-cache/github-awesome-copilot/` with hundreds of agents/skills/instructions files, confirming the general marketplace-sync mechanism works. The failure appears specific to marketplaces registered via `extraKnownMarketplaces` in enterprise managed settings.

**Expected:** The plugin's content should be fully downloaded before it is marked `installed: true`, or the install status should reflect a failed/incomplete sync with a visible error/retry to the user.

**Actual:** Config silently reports the plugin as installed and enabled while zero files exist on disk for it, so none of its agents/skills ever become available in chat — with no error surfaced anywhere in the UI.

## Note on the MCP log below (this is a *symptom*, not the cause)

The plugin declares a couple of MCP servers. Copilot still tries to launch them, using the (nonexistent) plugin dir as `cwd`. `npx` tolerates the missing `cwd`, so the MCP servers actually start and connect successfully — which confirms the MCP spawn is **not** the failure. The `[ERROR]` prefix here is simply how the Copilot CLI logs all MCP lifecycle events (including successful `connected` / `Started` lines). The real problem remains that the plugin's own bundled content (agents/skills) is never downloaded to `cache_path`.
canonical: Enterprise-managed marketplace plugins are marked installed and enabled in the local config even though their package contents are never downloaded to the cache directory, so the plugin stays empty and unusable without any visible failure.

canonical: The plus action button is taller than the tabs in the Agents interface, making the tab bar height inconsistent.

title: Show some tooltip about why the setting can't be edited
document:
Show some tooltip about why the setting can't be edited

Testing #324133

When hovering over a grayed out input, it might be nice to indicate why it's grayed out in case people don't see the "Managed by organization" text above
canonical: Grayed-out settings inputs do not explain why they are disabled on hover, leaving users without a tooltip indicating the setting is managed by an organization.

title: Agents window quick chat ends up in unexpected folder name
document:
Agents window quick chat ends up in unexpected folder name

Testing #324359

- Create a new quick chat
- Unset `sessions.layout.singlePaneDetailPanel` and reload window
- ❓ My quick chat is now in an unexpected long folder name
canonical: Quick chat sessions are being saved or displayed in the wrong folder when the single-pane detail panel setting is unset and the window reloads.

title: Setting input should fail earlier
document:
Setting input should fail earlier

Testing #324133

It waits all the way until I've entered and submitted a value to tell me that the setting can't be updated. Why not tell me earlier so I don't enter the values in vain?

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/54cbd02d-e101-4b2f-a4c2-3232fce71bee
canonical: The settings form only reports that a setting cannot be updated after the user has already entered and submitted a value instead of validating the setting earlier.

title: Agents window detail panel: Reload doesn't preserve `Toggle Details` enabled
document:
Agents window detail panel: Reload doesn't preserve `Toggle Details` enabled

Testing #324359

- Select an existing session
- `Toggle Details` to get into this state:

- Reload
- ❓ `Toggle Details` is no longer enabled
canonical: Reloading an existing session resets the agents detail view state so the toggle details control becomes disabled instead of staying available as it was before the reload.

title: Agents window detail panel: Reload doesn't preserve sidebar width
document:
Agents window detail panel: Reload doesn't preserve sidebar width

Testing #324359

The secondary sidebar on the right becomes narrower after reload.

Before reload:

After reload:
canonical: Reloading the agents window detail panel causes the right sidebar to lose its previous width and shrink.

title: Inconsistent wording of org policy enforcement
document:
Inconsistent wording of org policy enforcement

Testing #324133

The wording of the error message is inconsistent. Please use the same wording in both places.

---

`This setting is managed by your organization and its actual value cannot be changed.`

---

`This setting cannot be applied because it is configured in the system policy.`
canonical: The policy enforcement message is inconsistent because the same setting is described with different wording in different places, likely due to two separate message strings being used for the same organization-managed restriction.

title: Agents window detail panel: Files list missing in existing folder sessions
document:
Agents window detail panel: Files list missing in existing folder sessions

Testing #324359

- Start on new session screen
- Select an existing folder (non-worktree) session
- Session populates as expected from its previous state I left it in

- I decide to switch over to the `Files` tab
- ❓ No files listed, and I'm unclear how to get back to them

- If I select previous worktree sessions instead, their files populate:
canonical: The session detail panel fails to show the saved files list for existing non-worktree folder sessions when switching to the Files tab, likely because that state is not being loaded or rendered correctly.

title: Agents window detail panel: Not prompted about migrating local changes
document:
Agents window detail panel: Not prompted about migrating local changes

Testing #324359

Similar to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/324566 - I'm working on a repo with local uncommitted changes. When I kick off a new session to it, the `Changes` tab is empty:

- I thought I'd be prompted about if I'd like to copy over my local changes onto the worktree - did this behavior change recently?
- Should the `Changes` tab then be populated with the local changes, if they've been migrated to the worktree?
canonical: The agents window does not prompt to migrate local uncommitted changes into the worktree when starting a new session, leaving the Changes view empty instead of showing those modifications.

title: Copilot completion feedback
document:
Copilot completion feedback

## Copilot Completion Feedback
### Describe the issue, feedback, or steps to reproduce it:

### Completion text:
canonical: Copilot is sometimes showing no completion text in the feedback prompt where a completion should appear.

title: Copilot OTel Enterprise Policy UX papercuts
document:
Copilot OTel Enterprise Policy UX papercuts

Testing #324133

None of these are editable, but the UI makes it look editable. I think we can improve this rendering for managed settings
canonical: The enterprise policy settings are displayed as editable even though they are locked and cannot be changed, causing a misleading and confusing user interface.

title: Agents window detail panel: Why switch to Changes if no changes generated?
document:
Agents window detail panel: Why switch to Changes if no changes generated?

Testing #324359

- `+` a new session
- Choose a repo that doesn't have any local uncommitted changes
- `Toggle Side Panel` to view the files
- Send a message to start a new session in this repo
- The side panel stays open, as expected from the TPI
- The side panel switches to the `Changes` tab, as expected from the TPI
- ❓ I'm unclear why I'd want the panel to switch to the `Changes` tab though, since no changes have been generated, so now I'm just focusing on an empty panel view (screenshot below). Could we instead only dynamically switch to the `Changes` tab if changes have been generated in this session?
canonical: The side panel incorrectly switches to the Changes tab even when the session has not generated any changes, leaving the user on an empty view because the tab switch is triggered too broadly.

title: When sessions.list.showEmptyDefaultGroups is disabled, unclear how to launch chats in agent window
document:
When sessions.list.showEmptyDefaultGroups is disabled, unclear how to launch chats in agent window

Testing #324360

I disabled `sessions.list.showEmptyDefaultGroups`, this correctly hides the chat section when there are no active chats. Unclear how to start a chat, tried to use shorcuts` ctrl+n `, seems like they are not working.

Repro steps
1. Create a chat, after done select `Done`
2. Update the `sessions.list.showEmptyDefaultGroups` setting to false.
3. After this setting update, unable to start a new chat.
canonical: Disabling the setting that hides empty default chat groups also removes the visible way to start a new chat in the agent window, leaving no accessible chat launcher.

title: Agents window detail panel: Should changes populate while on new session screen?
document:
Agents window detail panel: Should changes populate while on new session screen?

Testing #324359

- `+` a new session
- Choose a repo that has some local changes (in my case, some modifications to existing files and an entirely new file)
- `Toggle Side Panel` so that I can view the `Files` tab
- I can see the markers next to files in the `Files` tab indicating which ones have changes
- ❓ But based on this from the TPI, should I also see a `Changes` section? I only have `Files`: `Open the Agents window with a workspace/session that has some file changes, so the Changes view has content.`
canonical: The new session detail panel does not populate the changes view for a workspace with local file modifications, likely because the session screen only shows the files tab and never loads the changes section.

title: Agents window detail panel: Unclear why I can't drag to resize sidebar
document:
Agents window detail panel: Unclear why I can't drag to resize sidebar

Testing #324359

- `+` a new session
- `Toggle Side Panel` so that I can view the `Files` tab
- ❓ Why can't I resize the side panel (make it wider or narrower)? My cursor changes to a split arrow and the border becomes blue highlighted so that it appears I should be able to resize (shown in screenshot below), but it doesn't work (dragging has no effect)
canonical: The side panel appears resizable but the drag handle does not actually change its width because resizing is not enabled or is broken for that panel.

title: Agents window detail panel: Unclear about empty Files view
document:
Agents window detail panel: Unclear about empty Files view

Testing #324359

- `+` a new session
- `Toggle Side Panel` so that I can view the `Files` tab
- Files populate as expected

- Select a specific file from the list `features.html`
- `features.html` file opens
- I notice there's a separate `Files` tab and select it
- ❓ `Files` tab is empty
canonical: Selecting a file opens a separate file detail view and leaves the Files tab empty instead of showing the file list or keeping the selected file visible.

title: some terminal tools are rendered in multiple parts?
document:
some terminal tools are rendered in multiple parts?

Testing #324419

Note the 3 tools that have been rendered outside of the "thinking" block... you can see that they started to render _inside_ the "thinking" block but then got cut off or something? Very odd.

Here's my prompt:

> ok now i wanna do something other than `ls`... here's what I was asked to do:
> 
> 
> 
> I will then note, based on what I see:
> - a successful command shows an error decoration
> - a failing command shows a success decoration
> - the decoration is missing when it should reflect a known exit status
> - the decoration state differs from what the command actually returned
canonical: Some terminal tool outputs are being split and rendered partly inside the thinking area and partly outside it instead of appearing as a single contiguous block.

title: terminal tool resize doesn't resize like it does in the actual terminal
document:
terminal tool resize doesn't resize like it does in the actual terminal

Testing #324419

I was expecting the text to reflow nicely like it does in the regular terminal view
canonical: The terminal tool does not reflow or resize its displayed output correctly when the panel size changes, unlike the regular terminal view.

title: terminal tool rendering bug - wrapped text pushes status code out of view
document:
terminal tool rendering bug - wrapped text pushes status code out of view

Testing #324419

I can't scroll but there's clearly text further down in the terminal
canonical: Wrapped terminal output is pushing the status indicator out of view so the user cannot scroll to or see content further down in the terminal.

title: Navigation shortcut conflicts between the sessions list and the chat tabs list
document:
Navigation shortcut conflicts between the sessions list and the chat tabs list

Testing #324363

1. Open a session without multiple chats
2. Press `Cmd/Ctrl`+`Shift`+`[` or `]` to navigate sessions
3. Navigate to a session that does have multiple tabs
4. 🐛 You are now stuck in this session and cannot use the shortcuts to navigate to a different session
canonical: Keyboard shortcuts for switching sessions are being intercepted by the chat tab navigation when a session has multiple tabs, preventing session navigation from working.

title: Background todo agent experiment silently disables `manage_todo_list`, breaking custom instructions and workflows
document:
Background todo agent experiment silently disables `manage_todo_list`, breaking custom instructions and workflows

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 1.127.0
- VS Code Version: 1.127.0
- OS Version: macOS 15.7.7
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): agent mode
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude Opus 4.6
- Logs:
-

ElectronBuildId: 14159160

1. Add an instruction to your global `copilot-instructions` that encourages the agent to use `manage_todo_list` for complex tasks
2. Be enrolled in the `chat.agent.backgroundTodoAgent.enabled` experiment (this happens silently, without user consent or notification)
3. Start an agent session with a multi-step task
4. Observe the agent responding with "there is no such tool called manage_todo_list"

## Problem

The `BackgroundTodoAgentEnabled` experiment (`github.copilot.chat.agent.backgroundTodoAgent.enabled` introduced in https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/313911) **silently removes the `manage_todo_list` tool from the main agent's available tools** and replaces it with a background `copilot-utility-small` model that autonomously manages the todo list. This happens with no user notification, no changelog mention, and no graceful degradation.

### Why this is problematic

1. **Breaks custom instructions**: Users who have written `copilot-instructions` files referencing `manage_todo_list` (a tool that has been publicly available and documented) will see the agent claim the tool doesn't exist while the tool UI specifically says it's still there as before.

2. **Silent enrollment**: Because the setting is `ExperimentBased` with the `"onExp"` tag, users are enrolled without any indication. There's no notification, no UI badge, nothing in the release notes to explain why a previously-working tool stopped being available.

3. **Wasted debugging time**: I spent significant time researching why the agent was refusing to use a tool that clearly exists in the documentation and had been working for me previously. There is no error message, log entry, or hint that an experiment disabled it.

4. **Breaks demos and shared workflows**: If you've built workflows or demos around the todo list feature and share them with colleagues, whether the workflow works depends on each person's invisible experiment assignment.

## Expected behavior

- If an experiment disables a user-facing tool, users should be notified (e.g., a notification, a note in the Copilot output channel, or at minimum the agent should acknowledge the tool exists but is currently managed differently)
- Custom instructions referencing `manage_todo_list` should not result in the agent claiming the tool doesn't exist — either the tool should remain available, or the agent should gracefully explain it's being handled in the background
- Experiment-gated features that remove existing functionality should have an explicit opt-in, not a silent opt-in

## Workaround

Explicitly set in `settings.json`:

This overrides the experiment assignment and restores the original behavior.
canonical: A background experiment silently removes the todo-list tool from the agent, causing custom instructions and existing workflows that reference it to fail without any notification or graceful fallback.

title: Add option for manual handling of collapsibleState similar to manageCheckboxStateManually
document:
Add option for manual handling of collapsibleState similar to manageCheckboxStateManually

# Proposal
- Let user manually handle `collapsibleState` of `vscode.TreeItems`, similar to `checkboxState`.
- Add additional option to `vscode.window.createTreeView`, like `manageCollapsibleStateManually`.
 - User must subscribe to `onDidExpandElement` and `onDidCollapseElement` to received changes.
- The `collapsibleState` of the `vscode.TreeItem` is used (provided by the `vscode.TreeDataProvider.getElement` method) just like the first time an item is drawn.

# Why
I need to programatically change the collapsible state of my tree items. Sometimes, I want them to collapse and sometimes I want them to expand. 

# Related issues
My feature request is similar to #55879, which led to the implementation of `vscode.TreeView.reveal(element, options)`. However, `reveal` doesn't solve the actual problem and brings other problems. 
1. #92176 The collapsible counter part (something like hide), is still missing and in backlog.
2. Reveal also keeps the revealed item in the view. In my case, I just need to expand the parent. I do not care and do not want the view to scroll to the item reveal. I don't want the view to scroll at all. This is related to #323192, where a scroll alignment is proposed. 
3. #286332 This is a problem I also already encountered. Would be solved by the proposal. `vscode.TreeView.onDidExpandElement` would fire and user could decide to elapsed element but he could also keep the item collapsed and do nothing.

# Questions I couldn't directly answer
- What exactly is shown by the view after items are collapsed/expanded?
 - Main idea is to keep the view as is and do not scroll up or down
 - What happens when we basically collapse all? The view has to scroll up to the parent items still shown
 - What happens when we expand all? What does "do not scroll" mean in this context? 
 - => What exactly is the anker the view should return on a redraw?
- It would make sense to have `onDidExpandElement`/`onDidCollapseElement` only get invoked if the user directly, or indirectly changes the collapsible state *OR*, `vscode.TreeView.reveal` is called. Though, `reveal` looses a bit of purpose, if one doesn't need to select, focus, or expand an element.
- Maybe it would make more sense, to fuse `onDidExpandElement` and `onDidCollapseElement`, similar to `onDidChangeCheckboxState` event. The Event would then contain the (potential) new `collapseState`. 

# Workaround how this already kinda works
We can already refresh the tree view with an empty array, thus drawing nothing. Then we draw the same view, just with updated collapsible states. However, this takes time and is ugly.
canonical: Tree views do not provide a way to manage item expand and collapse state manually, so an extension cannot reliably programmatically change a tree item’s collapsible state without forcing a full refresh or using reveal behavior.

title: Add a setting to disable the integrated Git worktrees UI (Source Control listing + branch checkout prompts)
document:
Add a setting to disable the integrated Git worktrees UI (Source Control listing + branch checkout prompts)

- VS Code Version: 1.122.1 (commit 8761a5560cfd65fdd19ce7e2bd18dab5c0a4d84e, x64)
- OS Version: Linux x64 (Arch, kernel 7.0.10)

### The request

Please add a setting to fully opt out of the integrated Git worktrees UI. Concretely, a way to disable:

1. The **worktrees listing** in the Source Control view, and
2. The **worktree-aware prompts** on branch checkout.

Some of us deliberately keep each worktree as an independent, self-contained window and never want cross-worktree UI. Today there is no setting that turns this off. This setting was still available until a recent update.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a repo with two linked worktrees, e.g. `repo/` (primary) and `repo-secondary/` (a `git worktree` of it on a different branch).
2. Set `"git.detectWorktrees": false` (and `"git.detectWorktreesLimit": 0`).
3. Open only `repo/` in a window.

Observed:
- The other worktree still shows up in the Source Control view (worktrees are listed regardless of `git.detectWorktrees`).
- Switching to a branch that is checked out in the other worktree triggers a worktree prompt/conflict dialog instead of just performing (or refusing) the checkout in the current worktree.

Expected (with an opt-out setting enabled):
- No worktree listing in Source Control for the current window.
- No worktree prompts; branch operations act only on the worktree of the current window.

### Suggestion

A single opt-out, e.g. `"git.worktrees.enabled": false` (or `"git.showWorktrees": false`), that suppresses the worktrees artifact group in Source Control and skips the worktree-aware branch prompts, so each worktree window behaves as a standalone repository.

Related: #278774 (closed; only flipped the default), #298496 (about the Copilot agent creating worktrees, not the SCM UI).
canonical: The integrated Git worktrees UI ignores the existing worktree detection settings, still showing other worktrees in Source Control and prompting on branch checkout because worktree-aware UI is always enabled with no opt-out setting.

title: macOS: show recent workspaces (incl. remote-SSH) in the dock menu, for parity with Windows JumpList
document:
macOS: show recent workspaces (incl. remote-SSH) in the dock menu, for parity with Windows JumpList

### Context

Right-clicking the VSCode dock icon on macOS shows only a single "New Window" entry plus the macOS-generated "Recent Documents" list. Remote-SSH (and other remote — WSL, Dev Containers, Codespaces, Tunnels) workspaces never appear in either.

### Problem

Two code paths keep remote workspaces out of the macOS dock:

- **macOS recent documents** — `updateMacOSRecentDocuments()` in `src/vs/platform/workspaces/electron-main/workspacesHistoryMainService.ts` filters to `file://` only (`if (loc.scheme === Schemas.file)`, ~L470), so `vscode-remote://ssh-remote+...` entries are dropped before reaching `app.addRecentDocument`. This is also a macOS API constraint — `NSDocumentController` only retains local file URLs here, so the filter is correct and should stay.
- **App dock menu** — `src/vs/platform/menubar/electron-main/menubar.ts` (~L294-297) hardcodes the dock menu to a single "New Window" item.

On Windows there is no such gap: `updateWindowsJumpList()` (~L377, same file) iterates `getRecentlyOpened().workspaces` with no scheme filter and emits `--folder-uri "vscode-remote://..."` entries, so remote hosts are one click away in the taskbar JumpList.

### Suggestion

Populate the macOS dock menu (`app.dock.setMenu`) with recent workspaces — including remote — to match the Windows JumpList. Clicking a remote entry would open the window with the remote folder URI, the same way the in-app "Open Recent" list and the Command Palette already handle remote entries today.

The in-app paths (Remote Explorer, `Remote-SSH: Connect to Host...`, `~/.ssh/config`) all work, but none are reachable from the dock — which is the whole point of the parity gap with Windows.
canonical: The macOS dock menu only shows a new window action and omits recent workspaces, so remote workspaces cannot be opened from the dock because the dock menu is not populated with recent entries.

title: Markdown headings are not recognized after fenced code block containing "kotlin" in VS Code 1.126.0 (macOS)
document:
Markdown headings are not recognized after fenced code block containing "kotlin" in VS Code 1.126.0 (macOS)

---

* VS Code Version: 1.126.0
* OS Version: macOS

---

# Steps to Reproduce:

1. Create a new Markdown file
2. Paste the following content:

kotlin
"kotlin"
`

3. Open Outline view or observe Markdown heading parsing in the editor

---

# Expected Behavior:

* Both `# A` and `# B` should be recognized as Markdown headings
* Outline view should display both headings
* Markdown structure should remain intact regardless of fenced code block content

---

# Actual Behavior:

* `# A` is recognized correctly
* `# B` is NOT recognized as a heading
* Markdown parsing appears to break after a fenced code block containing the string `"kotlin"`
* The issue affects editor-level Markdown structure (e.g. Outline / symbol navigation), while plain text rendering remains visible

---

# Additional Information:

The issue is reproducible even with all extensions disabled.

It occurs with multiple fenced code block languages:

text
"kotlin"
`

foobar
"kotlin"
`

However, the issue only appears when the exact string `"kotlin"` is present inside the fenced code block. Other strings (e.g. `"java"`, `"abc"`, `"hello"`) do not trigger the issue.

This suggests a Markdown editor tokenization/state machine regression in VS Code 1.126.0 where specific string content inside fenced code blocks causes incorrect parsing of subsequent Markdown structure.
canonical: Markdown heading parsing breaks after a fenced code block that contains the exact string `"kotlin"`, causing subsequent headings and outline structure to be ignored because the editor’s Markdown tokenizer or state machine enters an incorrect state.

title: Test: Switching update mode without restart (macOS)
document:
Test: Switching update mode without restart (macOS)

Refs: #289144

- [x] macOS @aiday-mar 
- [x] macOS @Yoyokrazy

Complexity: 4

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%289144%0A%0A&assignees=dmitrivms)

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23324438%0A%0A&assignees=dmitrivMS)

---

Scenarios can probably be split between different people.

Fixes #289144. Enables changing `update.mode` **without a restart**, and lets a
runtime disable (setting → `none` or the `UpdateMode` policy) **cancel an
in-progress update** via a transient `Cancelling…` state before `Disabled`.

## What changed (context for the tester)
- `update.mode` no longer requires a restart (the relaunch prompt was removed from `relauncher`).
- Switching to `none` (or a disabling policy) while a check/download/pending update is in flight now:
 - shows a transient **Cancelling…** indicator (title bar / tooltip / menu bar / activity badge), then
 - lands on **Disabled**, tearing down in-flight work.
- Re-enabling (`none` → `manual` / `start` / `default`) resumes update behavior without a restart.
- macOS uses the Electron/Squirrel.Mac auto-updater: states progress
 **Checking for Updates → Downloading → Downloaded → Ready (Restart to Update)**, with an
 **Overwriting** state when a newer update replaces a pending one.

## Environment / Prerequisites
- macOS machine (Apple Silicon and/or Intel; test at least one, ideally both).
- **Install an older VS Code Insiders build** so a real update is available when checking:
 - Download an older Insiders `.zip`/`.app` from the update server or a saved copy.
 - Move `Visual Studio Code - Insiders.app` to `/Applications` (Squirrel.Mac requires the app to be
 in a writable, non-quarantined location and **not** running from the read-only DMG).
 - Do **not** let it auto-update before the test starts.
- View logs: **Help → Toggle Developer Tools → Console**, and `Developer: Open Log File… → Main`.
 Look for `update#setState`, `update#reconfigure`, `update#disable`.
- Optional (policy scenario): configure the `UpdateMode` policy via a configuration profile / MDM.

---

## Scenario 1 — Change update mode without restart
1. Launch the older Insiders build from `/Applications`; confirm `Update: Mode` = `default`.
2. Wait ~30s; confirm an automatic check runs and an update becomes available
 (title-bar indicator, then "Downloading…", ending at **Restart to Update**).
3. Set `Update: Mode` → `manual`.
 - ✅ **No** "restart required" prompt.
 - ✅ Automatic background checks stop; **Check for Updates…** still works manually.
4. Set `Update: Mode` → `start`.
 - ✅ No restart prompt; only a single startup check (verify on next launch).
5. Set `Update: Mode` → `default`.
 - ✅ No restart prompt; automatic checks resume (a check runs within ~30s).
6. Set `Update: Mode` → `none`.
 - ✅ No restart prompt; state → **Disabled**; no further checks.
7. Set `Update: Mode` back to `default`.
 - ✅ Updates re-enable **without restart**; a check runs and the update reappears.

## Scenario 2 — Disable while an update is in flight → Cancelling → Disabled
1. Start from `update.mode` = `default` on the older build.
2. Trigger a check (auto or **Check for Updates…**). While the state is one of
 **Checking for Updates / Downloading / Downloaded / Ready (Restart to Update) / Overwriting**:
3. Set `Update: Mode` → `none`.
 - ✅ A transient **"Cancelling…"** indicator appears (title-bar label "Cancelling...",
 tooltip "Cancelling update, please wait...", activity badge, and menu entry "Cancelling Update...").
 - ✅ State then settles on **Disabled**
 (log: `update#disable - updates are disabled by user preference`).
 - ✅ The pending Squirrel update is not applied.
4. Re-enable `update.mode` = `default`.
 - ✅ A fresh check starts; the previously-cancelled update does not silently apply.

> `Cancelling…` is transient and may pass quickly; treat the log entries and the final
> **Disabled** state as the reliable assertions.

## Scenario 3 — Overwriting path (newer update replaces a pending one)
1. With `update.mode` = `default`, let an update reach **Ready (Restart to Update)**.
2. If a newer update is published, confirm the periodic overwrite check (~5 min) picks it up and
 the state transitions through **Overwriting** back to **Ready** for the newer version.
3. While in **Overwriting**, set `Update: Mode` → `none`.
 - ✅ Shows transient **Cancelling…**, then **Disabled**; no update is applied.

## Scenario 4 — Restart to Update completes normally (regression)
1. With `update.mode` = `default`, reach **Restart to Update** and choose to restart.
 - ✅ The app relaunches on the newer version; version is updated in **Code → About**.

## Scenario 5 — Policy: `UpdateMode` (optional, requires MDM/profile)
1. With the app running and an update in flight, apply a `UpdateMode` policy that disables updates.
 - ✅ In-flight update is cancelled (transient **Cancelling…**) → **Disabled**
 (log: `update#disable - updates are disabled by policy`).
 - ✅ `Update: Mode` becomes policy-controlled (not user-editable) and applies **without restart**.
2. Relax the policy.
 - ✅ Updates re-enable without restart; a check runs.

---

## Regression checks
- [ ] Full update path (default) completes end-to-end and relaunches on the new version.
- [ ] Toggling modes repeatedly/rapidly (default→none→default) settles on the latest value, no stuck state.
- [ ] No unexpected repeated checks after switching to `manual` / `start` / `none`.
- [ ] Update indicator / tooltip / menu-bar / activity-badge all reflect `Cancelling` correctly.
- [ ] Overwriting → Ready flow still works when not cancelled.
canonical: Changing the update mode should take effect immediately without requiring a restart, and disabling updates during an active update should cancel the in-flight update and leave the app in a disabled state.

title: Test: Switching update mode without restart (Windows)
document:
Test: Switching update mode without restart (Windows)

Refs: #289144

- [x] Windows @benvillalobos 
- [x] Windows @amunger 

Complexity: 4

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%289144%0A%0A&assignees=dmitrivms)

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23324437%0A%0A&assignees=dmitrivMS)

---

Scenarios can probably be split between different people.

Fixes #289144. Enables changing `update.mode` **without a restart**, and lets a
runtime disable (setting → `none` or the `UpdateMode` policy) **cancel an
in-progress update** via a transient `Cancelling…` state before `Disabled`.

## What changed (context for the tester)
- `update.mode` no longer requires a restart (the relaunch prompt was removed from `relauncher`).
- Switching to `none` (or a disabling policy) while a check/download/pending update is in flight now:
 - shows a transient **Cancelling…** indicator (title bar / tooltip / menu bar / activity badge), then
 - lands on **Disabled**, tearing down the in-flight check/download chain and any pending package.
- Re-enabling (`none` → `manual` / `start` / `default`) resumes update behavior without a restart.
- Windows specifics still apply: `update.enableWindowsBackgroundUpdates` (user setup only), system vs. user setup, and *user setup running as Admin is disabled*.

## Environment / Prerequisites
- Windows 10/11 machine (ideally two: one for **User setup**, one for **System setup**; or use separate VMs).
- **Install an older VS Code Insiders build** so a real update is available when checking:
 - Grab an older Insiders installer from the update server or a saved installer.
 - Do **not** let it auto-update before the test starts (launch, then immediately proceed).
- Ability to view logs: **Help → Toggle Developer Tools → Console**, and the main log via
 `Developer: Open Log File… → Main`. Look for `update#setState`, `update#reconfigure`,
 `update#disable` entries.
- Optional (for policy scenarios): access to Group Policy / registry to set the `UpdateMode` policy.

> Update state can be inspected via the title-bar update indicator, the gear/manage menu.

---

## Matrix to cover
| # | Setup type | Elevation | `update.enableWindowsBackgroundUpdates` | Focus |
|---|-----------|-----------|------------------------------------------|-------|
| A | User setup | Normal user | `true` (default, background updates ON) | Change mode w/o restart; cancel in-flight background download |
| B | User setup | Normal user | `false` (background updates OFF) | Change mode w/o restart; cancel manual/"Downloaded" flow |
| C | System setup | Normal user (elevates for install) | `true` | Mode change w/o restart; cancel; GC via inno_setup |
| D | System setup | Normal user | `false` | Mode change w/o restart; cancel |
| E | **User setup running as Admin** | Elevated | any | Updates are **Disabled (RunningAsAdmin)** — mode changes ignored |

---

## Scenario 1 — Change update mode without restart (all rows A–D)
1. Launch the older Insiders build; open **Settings** and confirm `Update: Mode` = `default`.
2. Wait ~30s; confirm an automatic check occurs and an update becomes available
 (title bar shows update indicator / "Downloading…" or "Restart to Update").
3. Set `Update: Mode` → `manual`.
 - ✅ **No** "restart required" prompt appears.
 - ✅ Automatic background checks stop; no periodic re-check occurs.
 - ✅ Manually running **Check for Updates…** still works.
4. Set `Update: Mode` → `start`.
 - ✅ No restart prompt; only a single startup check would occur (verify on next launch).
5. Set `Update: Mode` → `default`.
 - ✅ No restart prompt; automatic checks resume (a check runs within ~30s).
6. Set `Update: Mode` → `none`.
 - ✅ No restart prompt; state moves to **Disabled** (see Scenario 2 for in-flight case).
 - ✅ No further checks occur.
7. Set `Update: Mode` back to `default`.
 - ✅ Updates re-enable **without restart**; a check runs and the update reappears.

## Scenario 2 — Disable while an update is in flight → Cancelling → Disabled
Run once with background updates ON (rows A, C) and once OFF (rows B, D).
1. Start from `update.mode` = `default` on the older build.
2. Trigger a check (wait for auto, or **Check for Updates…**). While the state is one of
 **Checking for Updates / Downloading / Downloaded / Updating / Ready (Restart to Update)**:
3. Set `Update: Mode` → `none`.
 - ✅ A transient **"Cancelling…"** indicator is shown (title bar label "Cancelling...",
 tooltip "Cancelling update, please wait...", activity badge "Cancelling … update...",
 and Help/menu entry "Cancelling Update...").
 - ✅ State then settles on **Disabled** (log: `update#disable - updates are disabled by user preference`).
 - ✅ Any in-flight request/download is torn down; the pending installer/package is not applied.
4. Re-enable `update.mode` = `default`.
 - ✅ A fresh check starts; previously-cancelled work does not silently resume.
 - ✅ (Background updates ON) a new background download proceeds normally.

> Note: `Cancelling…` is transient and may pass quickly; the log entries and the final
> `Disabled` state are the reliable assertions. If the update had already reached a state that
> can't be torn down mid-flight, verify no update is applied on next restart.

## Scenario 3 — Background updates toggle (rows A/C vs B/D)
1. With `update.enableWindowsBackgroundUpdates` = `true` (user setup): confirm an available update
 downloads and installs in the background, ending at **Restart to Update**.
2. Cancel per Scenario 2 while **Downloaded/Ready** and confirm it returns to **Disabled**
 cleanly and the staged package is not applied on relaunch.
3. Set `update.enableWindowsBackgroundUpdates` = `false`; repeat: confirm the non-background
 flow (manual apply / restart-to-update) and that Scenario 2 cancellation still works.

## Scenario 4 — System setup GC (rows C, D)
1. On a **System setup** install with background updates enabled, after a successful update,
 confirm no leftover errors and that GC (handled by inno_setup with elevation) does not block
 or crash the update flow. Check main log for update entries and absence of `collectGarbage` errors.

## Scenario 5 — User setup running as Admin (row E)
1. Launch the **User setup** Insiders build **as Administrator**.
 - ✅ Updates are **Disabled** with reason RunningAsAdmin
 (log: `update#ctor - updates are disabled due to running as Admin in user setup`).
2. Change `update.mode` to `default`/`manual`/`start`.
 - ✅ Changes are **ignored** — state stays Disabled (permanent/non-reversible disable).
 - ✅ No Cancelling/checking activity; no restart prompt.

## Scenario 6 — Policy: `UpdateMode` (optional, requires policy access)
1. With the app running and an update in flight, set the `UpdateMode` policy to a value that
 disables updates (e.g. `none`).
 - ✅ In-flight update is cancelled (transient **Cancelling…**) and state becomes **Disabled**
 (log: `update#disable - updates are disabled by policy`).
 - ✅ The `Update: Mode` setting becomes policy-controlled (not user-editable) and applies **without restart**.
2. Relax the policy so updates are allowed again.
 - ✅ Updates re-enable without restart; a check runs.

---

## Regression checks
- [ ] Normal update path (default mode) still completes end-to-end on User and System setup.
- [ ] Toggling modes repeatedly / rapidly (default→none→default) settles on the latest value with no stuck state.
- [ ] No leaked timers/schedulers (no unexpected repeated checks after switching to `manual`/`start`/`none`).
- [ ] Update indicator / tooltip / menu-bar / activity-badge all reflect the `Cancelling` state correctly.
canonical: Changing the update mode should take effect immediately without restarting, including cancelling any update already in progress when updates are disabled.

title: Copilot Plan/Agent back button creates a new chat session and automatically sends Start implementation
document:
Copilot Plan/Agent back button creates a new chat session and automatically sends Start implementation

## Description

When using the Copilot Plan / Agent workflow, after an implementation task has completed, clicking the back/return button can unexpectedly create a new chat window/session and automatically send the message:

This appears to replay or restart the previous implementation action even though the prior task has already finished.

This is similar to #323996, but in this case the behavior also creates/opens a new chat window or session before sending `Start implementation` automatically.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Copilot Chat in VS Code Insiders.
2. Use the Plan / Agent workflow to create and start an implementation task.
3. Wait until the implementation task completes.
4. Click the back/return button to go back to the previous Plan/Chat state.

## Expected Behavior

Clicking back/return after the task is completed should only restore the previous Plan/Chat view.

It should not create a new chat window/session, and it should not automatically send `Start implementation` again.

## Actual Behavior

VS Code creates/opens a new chat window/session and automatically sends:

without user confirmation.

## Impact

This can accidentally start another implementation run, consume additional Copilot quota/credits, and make it difficult to safely return to the previous planning session after a completed task.

## Environment

- VS Code Version: Code - Insiders 1.128.0-insider
- Commit: 3fbc14e67322245a87adf07c3da0c487e7715918
- Architecture: x64
- OS: Windows
- Workflow: Copilot Plan / Agent
- Trigger: after implementation completes, click back/return

## Notes

I searched for related reports before filing this. #323996 appears related, but the additional behavior here is that a new chat window/session is created before `Start implementation` is sent automatically.
canonical: Clicking back after a completed Copilot Plan/Agent run incorrectly restores the previous state by creating a new chat session and automatically resending the implementation start command instead of just returning to the prior view.

title: Japanese IME reconversion at caret (no selection) duplicates committed text with EditContext
document:
Japanese IME reconversion at caret (no selection) duplicates committed text with EditContext

- VS Code Version: 1.127.0
- OS Version: Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200

## Summary

With the EditContext-based text input (default `editor.editContext: true`), Japanese IME **reconversion of already-committed text triggered at the caret position (no selection)** inserts the reconverted text instead of replacing the original, resulting in duplicated text. Reconversion **with an explicit selection works correctly** (the selection is replaced).

Reproduces with **Microsoft IME** (built-in) and other TSF-based IMEs, so this is not specific to a particular IME. The same operations work correctly in Notepad and other standard TSF applications.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Windows, Japanese Microsoft IME active, open any text file in the editor.
2. Type `・・・` (three middle dots), convert with Space to `…`, and commit with Enter. The editor now contains the committed text `…`.
3. Place the caret immediately after `…` **without selecting it**.
4. Press the **Henkan (Convert) key** to reconvert the committed text. The IME shows reconversion candidates.
5. Commit a candidate (Enter).

**Expected**: the original `…` is replaced by the chosen candidate (this is what happens in Notepad, and in VS Code when the text is explicitly selected before pressing Henkan).

**Actual**: the chosen candidate is inserted at the caret, and the original text remains — e.g. `…` becomes `……`.

## Notes

- Selection-based reconversion (select `…`, then press Henkan) replaces correctly, so the defect seems limited to the caret-position reconversion path, where the IME sets the selection over the target range via TSF (`SetSelection`) inside its edit session before starting the composition. TSF-level instrumentation on our side shows the IME's composition range correctly covers the original text (`GetSelection`/`GetRange` both report the covering range), yet the editor applies the composition as an insertion.
- Possibly related: #238643 (reconversion itself was fixed by EditContext), #320074 (duplication when moving the caret before conversion during composition).
canonical: EditContext mishandles Japanese IME reconversion started at the caret with no selection by treating the reconverted text as a new insertion instead of replacing the committed text range, causing duplication.

document:
[TPI] BYOK suport in Agent Host

Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/322977

- [x] Any OS @aeschli

Complexity: 3

Authors: @vritant24

---

# Setup
- set `chat.agentHost.byokModels.enabled` to `true
- restart VS Code
- Ensure Copilot Agent Host is being used for chat

## For adding Built-in BYOK providers:

get a secret (apices) from [this key vault ](https://ms.portal.azure.com/#@microsoft.onmicrosoft.com/resource/subscriptions/9b16b0df-65b6-46d3-b9c9-e4fbc86b1fa7/resourceGroups/vscode-copilot-chat/providers/Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/copilot-automation/secrets)(I recommend gemini or open_ai)
Using Chat: Manage Language Models > Add Models, add the corresponding provider (Gemini or Open AI)
the UI should take you through the configuration
For adding 3rd party extension based providers, there can be many ways, but I have tried Ollama, and HuggingFace.

# Testing

## With the Setting on:

> Note: Visibility toggles in `Manage Models View` will not apply in the Agent Host model picker at this point.

- The model picker should show the BYOK models in groups in the Editor Window
- You should be able to select a BYOK model and start a chat with it
- The chat should be reflected in all client windows (Agent Window and Editor Window)

## With the setting off (needs VS Code restart):

- The model picker should not show BYOK models.
- All existing GitHub Copilot models should work with no issue.
canonical: The agent host fails to include and use Bring Your Own Key models in the chat model picker and shared chat sessions even when the feature is enabled, so BYOK providers are not available there as expected.

title: Test: Linkification in Copilot/AH
document:
Test: Linkification in Copilot/AH

Refs: #315183

- [x] Windows @bamurtaugh
- [x] anyOS @deepak1556

Complexity: 2

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23324430%0A%0A&assignees=roblourens)

---

We now support linkifying file references and symbols in agent host Copilot sessions. This is way simpler than what we had in the past. We just add instructions for the agent to use markdown link syntax for files, and links with line numbers for symbols.

Test with some different models in different scenarios. Do you reliably get links for file names and symbols as appropriate? Sometimes the model wants to do somethign like only linkify the first reference of a file in a response, the Codex system prompt apparently instructs this so we probably can't get to 100% by arguing in the system prompt.

Can you click the links and open the file?

Do symbol links go to more or less the right place? We are relying on the model to pick the line.
canonical: Copilot agent responses sometimes fail to consistently turn file and symbol references into clickable links, especially for repeated references or accurate symbol line targets.

title: 1.128.0 Endgame
document:
1.128.0 Endgame

[Endgame guide](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog/blob/main/guidelines/endgame-guide.md)

# Before Monday 6AM Zurich time

`@team`:
- Update endgame availability 
- Test plan items created 
- Milestone only has TPIs. All other issues need to be closed
- All closed issues in milestone labeled `on-testplan`, `bug`, or `verification-needed`
- Verification steps added to `bug` and `verification-needed` issues

# Monday morning (Zurich)

`@endgame-champ`:
- [x] Assign TPIs
 - [x] Create TPI's for all verification needed bugs. Visit `Testing and Verification` on tools site, and generate groupings, then create TPIs. The TPI section below will update with all of the generated items. 
- [x] Pause automatic releases (this also ensures chat-pre-release isn't released) after 10am.
- [x] Run the [Start Endgame](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/workflows/start-endgame.yml) workflow
 
 - PRs opened with auto-merge enabled — ensure they get reviewed and merged Monday morning so Insiders picks up the next milestone version:
 - [x] Version bump (`microsoft/vscode`): https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/324433
 - [x] Endgame notebook milestone bump (`microsoft/vscode`): https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/324432
 
 - If you need to run these steps manually, see the [Branching guide](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog/blob/main/guidelines/endgame-guide.md#branching).
- [x] In absence of the tech writer, follow the steps in the [website endgame issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-website/wiki#performing-a-weekly-release)

# Monday morning (Redmond and Zurich)

`@team`:
- [Test plan items](https://github.com/issues?q=org%3Amicrosoft%20milestone%3A%221.128.0%22%20is%3Aissue%20is%3Aopen%20label%3Atestplan-item). All TPIs must be finished by noon. This includes verification TPIs.
- Make sure your TPIs are being tested and ping folks if to confirm they will finish by noon.

# Monday afternoon (Redmond and Zurich)

`@team`:
- Fix essential issues. Default to pushing to next iteration
- Release notes 

# Monday EOD Redmond time

`@team`:
- All issues including late fixes, are verified ([query](https://github.com/issues?q=org%3Amicrosoft+milestone%3A%221.128.0%22+is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed+reason%3Acompleted++label%3Abug+-label%3Averified+-label%3Aon-testplan+-label%3A*duplicate+-label%3Aduplicate+-label%3Ainvalid+-label%3A*as-designed+-label%3Aerror-telemetry+-label%3Averification-steps-needed+-label%3Az-author-verified+-label%3Aunreleased+-label%3A*not-reproducible+-label%3A*out-of-scope+sort%3Aupdated-asc))
- Release notes written

`@endgame-champ`:
- [ ] Create new builds from `release/` branch so we can finish verification.

# Tuesday by noon CET

`@team`:
- Fix essential issues. Default to pushing to next iteration
- Release notes 

`@endgame-champ`:
- [x] Generate "Thank you" section of the release notes in the `vnext` branch of the `vscode-docs` repo. 
 - [x] [Acknowledge third-party pull requests](https://tools.code.visualstudio.com/acknowledgement) in release notes. 
 - [x] Acknowledge [issue trackers](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog/wiki/Community-Triage---Credits#community-triage---role-based) from the community (do the copy-paste variant) - 
- [x] Build and release insiders from release/
- [x] Build stable from release/ and release to MSFT folks
- [x] Reenable automatic releases
 - A build is scheduled for 7:00 PM Zurich time (`19:00 GMT+2`); make sure automatic releases are reenabled before then so we get an Insiders build from `main` on Tuesday evening.

# Tuesday afternoon/Wednesday morning

- In extreme cases, accept candidate changes
 - [x] Build stable from `release/`. Do *not* bump the VS Code version.
 - [x] Release stable to MSFT folks

# Wednesday
`@endgame-champ`:
- [x] Stable starts rollout to everyone (start percentage: `20%`, end time UTC: `6AM next Wednesday`)
- [x] Deploy vscode.dev
 - [x] Verify the [release candidate](https://dev.azure.com/monacotools/Monaco/_build?definitionId=276) `release/x.y` passed the sanity tests in the last step
 - [x] [Kick off deploy](https://dev.azure.com/monacotools/Monaco/_build?definitionId=276) (Branch `release/x.y`, Deployment Target `prod`, needs peer approval)
- [x] [Publish @vscode/chat-lib](https://dev.azure.com/monacotools/Monaco/_build?definitionId=683) (Branch `release/x.y`, Release Version `auto`, needs peer approval)
- [x] Publish website
- [x] Publish socials (ping Cynthia)
- [x] [Publish @types/vscode](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Publish-vscode-types) if you find type diffs after running the steps
- [x] Create an official release
 - [x] Create a tag (make sure you pull the release branch first): `git tag `
 - [x] Push the tag: `git push origin `
 - [x] Create a GitHub release: [Open the GitHub tags](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/tags), and click far right ... > Create Release. Use the version as title and use the release link `https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v` for the description
- [x] Close milestone (**do not** delete it)
- [x] Move all remaining issues to "On Deck"
- [x] Ensure that the latest Insiders release from main has succeeded

title: [PERFORMANCE] Critical performance issue when typing fast in the address bar of the integrated browser (7000ms+ INP)
document:
[PERFORMANCE] Critical performance issue when typing fast in the address bar of the integrated browser (7000ms+ INP)

# Hardware
* CPU: i3-4160T
* Memory: 8GB DDR3

# Version
VSCode Insiders 1.128

# Core Issue
When typing fast in the address bar of the integrated browser, the INP (Interaction-to-Next-Paint) is very high, and even reaches 5000ms or higher (in my test, the maximum INP was 7648ms).

# Reproduction
Open the integrated browser, type fast (for example, long press the A key), and the issue reproduces.

# Basic Analysis
## The Direct Cause
I tried to analyze the cause of the issue per Chromium Profiler, the core high-cost task is style re-calculation, for more detail, it is getPropertyValue called by getDimension function in dom.ts.

## RCA
Based on my personal analysis of profile results, I think the RCA of the issue is:
Due to the doSetValue method of the QuickInput component triggered style re-calculation many times, each keypress or input event costs \~500ms, and Chromium merged 5~10 events into a single paint cycle, raising the high INP.

# Attachments
[Trace-20260706T105244.json.gz](https://file.uhsea.com/2607/2f21c42f1e24ae3970694a55b89cb39eHA.gz)
canonical: Typing quickly in the integrated browser address bar causes severe input latency because QuickInput repeatedly triggers expensive style recalculation and dimension measurements on each keystroke.

title: Copilot changes diff do not honor word wrap
document:
Copilot changes diff do not honor word wrap

Visual Studio Code editor allows word-wrap, which automatically breaks visually long lines so they fit in the visible panel. While not typical for code, this is usual for prose text, such as markdown or latex files.

When using Copilot agent for editing the file, the changes show the typical diff view with red/green showing the changes. However, while the green section honors the word wrap, the red section do not, and extends horizontally as if the word wrap is disabled.

The expected behavior is that both red and green sections should honor word wrap, if it is enabled.
canonical: Copilot edit diffs do not wrap removed lines in the editor when word wrap is enabled, likely because the diff view only applies wrapping to added content and not deleted content.

title: Pseudoterminal plugins do not have a way to get the users cwd picked in a multi-root workspace
document:
Pseudoterminal plugins do not have a way to get the users cwd picked in a multi-root workspace

- Entry point: window.registerTerminalProfileProvider(id, { provideTerminalProfile(token) }) — the callback only receives a CancellationToken, never the cwd.
- Where the info exists but is dropped: VS Code's multi-root picker result flows internally as ICreateContributedTerminalProfileOptions.cwd into extHostTerminalService.$createContributedProfileTerminal(id, options). It's used to build the internal shellLaunchConfig/DTO, but it is not forwarded to provideTerminalProfile, and it is not surfaced on Terminal.creationOptions for extension terminals (that stays the ExtensionTerminalOptions the provider returned, which has no cwd field).
- Reasonable feature request: either (a) pass the resolved cwd/WorkspaceFolder into provideTerminalProfile (e.g. a context/options argument), or (b) expose it on Terminal.creationOptions / a new field for extension terminals so a Pseudoterminal can read it in onDidOpenTerminal.
canonical: Pseudoterminal terminal profile providers in multi-root workspaces cannot access the user-selected working directory because the resolved cwd is dropped before the provider is called or exposed to the created terminal.

title: TPI) Agent Host Copilot terminal command decorations reflect shell exit status
document:
TPI) Agent Host Copilot terminal command decorations reflect shell exit status

Refs: #322163, #323922

- [x] macOS @TylerLeonhardt
- [x] linux @rzhao271
- [x] windows @hediet

Complexity: 2

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23324419%0A%0A&assignees=anthonykim1)

---

Verify that terminal command decorations in Agent Host Copilot chat reflect the real exit status of shell commands when the custom terminal tool is disabled (SDK shell tool path).

Background: This only reproduced with the custom terminal tool disabled, where the agent relies on the shell tool from Copilot CLI. With that path, command decorations were previously not reflective of the command's exit status. The decoration should now be driven by the SDK shell exit code: `0` shows success, non-zero shows error, and missing exit metadata shows unknown.

Prerequisites:
- Use the latest VS Code Insiders
- Sign in to GitHub Copilot
- Enable `chat.agentHost.enabled` (this test is for Agent Host Copilot)
- Set `chat.agentHost.customTerminalTool.enabled` to `false` (custom terminal tool disabled, so the SDK shell tool is used)

Part 1: Successful command shows a success decoration
- Start an Agent Host Copilot chat
- Ask Copilot to run a command that succeeds, for example `echo hello` or `ls`
- Confirm the terminal command row in chat shows a success (non-error) decoration marker
- Confirm the decoration matches the actual `0` exit code

Part 2: Failing command shows an error decoration
- Ask Copilot to run a command that exits non-zero, for example `ls /path/that/does/not/exist` or a script that exits with a non-zero code
- Confirm the terminal command row shows an error decoration marker
- Confirm the decoration reflects the non-zero exit status (not shown as success)

Part 3: Mixed sequence
- Ask Copilot to run several commands in a row that mix success and failure
- Confirm each command's decoration independently reflects its own exit status
- Confirm a failing command does not cause a following successful command to show as failed, and vice versa

Part 4: Exploratory
- Try a variety of commands (long-running, quoted args, commands writing files, commands with stderr output) and note any cases where:
 - a successful command shows an error decoration
 - a failing command shows a success decoration
 - the decoration is missing when it should reflect a known exit status
 - the decoration state differs from what the command actually returned
canonical: Terminal command decorations in Agent Host Copilot chat can show the wrong status because they are not correctly driven by the shell command’s actual exit code when the custom terminal tool is disabled.

title: TPI) Agent Host Copilot session attachments from Copilot CLI trajectories
document:
TPI) Agent Host Copilot session attachments from Copilot CLI trajectories

Refs: #323773, #315193

- [x] macOS @Yoyokrazy
- [x] linux @TylerLeonhardt
- [x] windows @alexr00

Complexity: 3

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23324418%0A%0A&assignees=anthonykim1)

---

Verify that Agent Host Copilot chat can attach prior Copilot agent host sessions, and extension host Copilot CLI sessions as context.

Background: Non-Copilot sessions including Claude/local/other chat sessions are not supported here.

Prerequisites:
- Use the latest VS Code Insiders
- Sign in to GitHub Copilot
- Enable `chat.agentHost.enabled` (this test is for Agent Host Copilot)
- Have at least one prior Copilot CLI session available to attach (create one first if needed), whether it's an agent host Copilot session or an extension host Copilot session previously

Part 1: Attach an agent host or extension host Copilot session in Agent Host chat
- Start an Agent Host Copilot chat
- Open the `Sessions...` picker (or type `#session:`) and confirm prior agent host or extension host Copilot sessions are listed
- Attach one session
- Confirm a visible Sessions chip appears on the input
- Ask a question that requires context from that prior session and confirm Copilot can use the attached trajectory to answer

Part 2: Picker only shows supported sessions
- With other session types present (for example Claude / local / other chat sessions, and any non-Copilot-CLI Agent Host sessions), open the Agent Host `Sessions...` picker
- Confirm only attachable Copilot CLI sessions are listed
- Confirm non-Copilot sessions including Claude/local/other chat sessions are not offered

Part 3: Reload / resume restores only the visible chip
- After attaching a session and sending a message, reload the window (or resume the chat)
- Confirm the visible Sessions chip is restored
- Confirm no extra hidden trajectory/resource attachment is surfaced in the UI after reload

Part 4: Exploratory
- Try attaching multiple sessions, re-attaching the same session, and mixing session attachments with other context
- Note any cases where:
 - a supported Copilot CLI session is missing from the picker
 - an unsupported session type appears in the Agent Host picker
 - the visible chip or the underlying trajectory context is lost after reload/resume
 - attached session context is not actually used by Copilot
canonical: Agent Host Copilot chat fails to consistently list, attach, and restore prior Copilot CLI or Copilot agent sessions as context, sometimes omitting supported sessions, showing unsupported ones, or losing the attachment after reload.

title: chat: make maximum persisted sessions a user-configurable setting
document:
chat: make maximum persisted sessions a user-configurable setting

## Problem

The number of chat sessions retained in the history panel is hard-coded to 50 in `chatSessionStore.ts`:

`trimEntries()` runs on every shutdown and permanently deletes index entries beyond that limit (sorted by `lastMessageDate`). There is no way for a user to recover deleted sessions — even if the underlying JSONL files still exist on disk — because the index entry is gone.

50 is far too low for anyone who uses Copilot Chat heavily. Sessions fall off the list within days.

## Proposed Solution

Expose `maxPersistedSessions` as a VS Code setting, for example:

`trimEntries()` would read from the settings service instead of the hard-coded constant, keeping the default behaviour unchanged.

## Workaround

Users currently have to patch `workbench.desktop.main.js` directly to raise the limit, which is wiped on every VS Code update.
canonical: Chat session history is permanently trimmed using a hard-coded limit, causing older persisted sessions to be deleted from the index with no user-configurable way to raise the retention limit.

title: Test: AH Copilot SDK network proxy
document:
Test: AH Copilot SDK network proxy

Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/324209

- [x] macOS @Giuspepe 
- [x] linux @aeschli
- [x] windows @hediet 

Complexity: 4

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23324405%0A%0A&assignees=chrmarti)

---

# Test: Agent Host respects HTTP proxy configuration

Verify that chat requests made from the **Agent Host** process honor the configured HTTP proxy, both when the proxy is set explicitly via the `http.proxy` setting and when it is resolved from the OS (PAC URL).

> **Two windows:** the test proxy runs inside a dev container in one VS Code window (the "proxy window"). The actual testing happens in a **separate** VS Code window/install (the "test window") — it is easier to use two different installs (e.g. run the dev container in VS Code Stable and test in VS Code Insiders). Steps below that mention Settings, restarting, and making chat requests apply to the **test window**.

## Setup

1. In the **proxy window**, start the test proxy from the `vscode-proxy-agent` repo:
 - Clone `https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-proxy-agent`.
 - Important: switch to branch `chrmarti/quarrelsome-gibbon`.
 - `Dev Containers: Reopen in Container` > `HTTP Proxy Test`.
 - This starts 3 log terminals in the dev container: 1 for the PAC config and 2 for the two proxies. (See `.devcontainer/http-proxy-test/README.md` for details.)
 - Keep the proxy log terminals visible so you can observe requests as they arrive.
2. In the **test window**, ensure the Agent Host is enabled: set `chat.agentHost.enabled` to `true` in Settings.

## Test 1: Explicit `http.proxy` setting

1. Set `http.proxy` in Settings to the test proxy, e.g. `http://127.0.0.1:3155`.
2. Restart VS Code (current limitation: the Agent Host does not pick up proxy changes without a restart).
3. Make a chat request that runs in the Agent Host's Copilot.
4. **Verify** in the proxy log terminal that the chat request(s) go through the proxy.

## Test 2: PAC URL configured in the OS

1. Remove the `http.proxy` setting.
2. Configure the PAC URL at the OS level, pointing at the test PAC, e.g. `http://127.0.0.1:4444`:
 - **macOS**: System Settings → Network → (active interface) → Details… → Proxies → enable "Automatic proxy configuration" and set the URL.
 - **Windows**: Settings → Network & Internet → Proxy → "Use setup script" → turn on and set the "Script address".
 - **Linux (GNOME)**: Settings → Network → Network Proxy → set Method to "Automatic" and set the "Configuration URL".
3. Restart VS Code (current limitation, as above).
4. Make a chat request that runs in the Agent Host's Copilot.
5. **Verify** in the proxy log terminal that the chat request(s) go through the proxy resolved from the PAC file.

## Cleanup

1. Remove the `http.proxy` setting if it is still set.
2. Remove the OS-level PAC URL / automatic proxy configuration.
3. Restart VS Code so it no longer routes traffic through the test proxy.
canonical: The Agent Host does not route Copilot chat requests through the configured HTTP proxy, whether the proxy is set directly or obtained from the system PAC configuration.

title: Copilot Chat loses custom agents and MCP servers after first prompt (workspace becomes `unknown:/`)
document:
Copilot Chat loses custom agents and MCP servers after first prompt (workspace becomes `unknown:/`)

**VS Code version** 
1.127.0

**Copilot Chat version** 
0.55.0

**OS** 
Windows 11

**Issue type** 
Bug

**Description** 
When I start a new chat with a custom agent selected, the agent is initially visible and selectable. After sending the first prompt, the Agents view loses my custom agent(s) and MCP server(s), and the chat continues with default behavior.

I can reproduce this consistently.

**Repro steps**
1. Open workspace folder.
2. Confirm custom agent appears in Agents view.
3. Confirm MCP servers appear (including `unraid-mcp`).
4. Start new chat with custom agent selected.
5. Send first prompt.
6. Observe custom agent and MCP servers disappear from session context and fallback occurs.

**Expected result** 
Selected custom agent remains active and MCP servers remain available for the chat session.

**Actual result** 
After first prompt:
- custom agent disappears from active set
- MCP server count drops
- chat runs with default agent behavior

**What I already tried**
- Enabled `security.workspace.trust.enabled = true`
- Reloaded window
- Started new chats
- Issue still reproduces

**Key evidence from logs**
- Initially loads correctly:
 - customizations log shows `agents = 1` and `MCP Servers = 2`
- Then workspace resolution fails:
 - renderer log repeatedly shows `unknown:/...` and `ENOPRO`
 - examples:
 - `Failed to get installed servers from unknown:/.vscode/mcp.json`
 - `Failed to resolve files at location: unknown:/.github/agents`
- After that, customizations drop:
 - customizations log shows `agents = 0` and `MCP Servers = 1`
- MCP then fails:
 - Copilot Chat log:
 - `Failed to load tools for MCP server unraid-mcp`
 - `Gateway not found`
 - SSE reconnect failures / Not Found

**Relevant log files attached**
[customizationsDebug.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29680325/customizationsDebug.log)
[GitHub Copilot Chat.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29680328/GitHub.Copilot.Chat.log)
[mcpGateway.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29680327/mcpGateway.log)
[renderer.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29680326/renderer.log)
canonical: After the first prompt, the chat loses custom agents and MCP servers because workspace resolution falls back to an unknown location, causing the client to stop loading the workspace-specific customizations and tools.
