title: Copilot Chat frequently fails with "Response contained no choices" using OpenAI Responses API BYOK model
document:
Copilot Chat frequently fails with "Response contained no choices" using OpenAI Responses API BYOK model

1.125.1

Copilot Chat Version:
0.53.1

Windows 11

Description:
Copilot Chat/Agent mode repeatedly fails during normal conversations with:

This happens multiple times per conversation. There does not appear to be a 100% reliable way to reproduce the problem but it inevitably happens the longer the conversation goes.

Model configuration:
The failing model is configured through `chatLanguageModels.json`, not selected from the standard built-in Copilot model list.

The relevant configuration is an OpenAI provider entry using the Responses API:

This configuration is used so model requests can use zero data retention mode.

Local log correlation:
In local VS Code logs, each visible `Response contained no choices` error lines up with a Copilot Chat request summary marked `unknown` for the same configured `gpt-5.5` model via `copilotLanguageModelWrapper`.

Redacted examples:

This pattern was found repeatedly in the same VS Code log session. The unredacted request IDs are available locally, but are redacted here because they may be uniquely trackable.

Relevant source-code path:
In current `microsoft/vscode`, this appears to flow through:

`languageModelAccess.ts` uses `debugName: 'copilotLanguageModelWrapper'` for the language model wrapper request.

`chatMLFetcher.ts` returns `ChatFetchResponseType.Unknown` with `RESPONSE_CONTAINED_NO_CHOICES` when no successful completion is produced from the completion stream.

`commonTypes.ts` defines:

Impact:
This is user-visible and interrupts normal chat/agent workflows repeatedly. It does not appear to be prompt-specific. The failure appears to happen when the OpenAI Responses API configured model stream produces no usable completion choice, after which Copilot Chat classifies the request as `unknown` and surfaces the generic `Response contained no choices` error.

Expected:
Copilot Chat should either produce a response or surface a more actionable provider/model/backend error. If this is a valid upstream “no output” or malformed Responses API stream condition, the extension should handle it gracefully and show enough information to identify whether the issue is model output, Responses API parsing, streaming, tool-calling, reasoning output, or zero data retention mode.

Possible related issues:
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/318235
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/313215
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/316360
canonical: Copilot Chat intermittently fails during longer conversations with a generic “response contained no choices” error because the configured OpenAI Responses API model sometimes returns no usable completion choice, causing the request to be classified as unknown.

title: Integrated Browser docked DevTools disappear and Browser View incorrectly resizes during editor interactions
document:
Integrated Browser docked DevTools disappear and Browser View incorrectly resizes during editor interactions

Description

When using Live Preview with docked browser DevTools, normal editor interactions cause the browser layout to break.

The browser view temporarily expands to fill the area occupied by the docked DevTools, causing the DevTools to disappear. Once the interaction ends, the layout returns to normal.

This only affects docked browser DevTools. If the browser DevTools are undocked into a separate window, the problem disappears.

Open Live Preview (Show Preview).
Open Browser DevTools docked inside the browser.
Interact with the editor by:
switching editor tabs,
typing (triggering IntelliSense/autocomplete),
changing focus between editors,
open command palette.
Observe the browser view.

Expected behaviour

The browser and docked DevTools remain visible and maintain their layout.

Actual behaviour

The browser temporarily expands over the docked DevTools area, making the DevTools disappear until the interaction completes. The layout then snaps back.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5ef9222d-20ee-49fc-b479-160e5ed21b82

Additional information

Reproduces with Live Preview pages and ordinary websites (e.g. https://google.com), so it is not specific to Live Preview or my application.
Does not reproduce when Browser DevTools are undocked.
Developer: Toggle Developer Tools continues to inspect the VS Code workbench correctly; the issue is specific to the Integrated Browser + docked Browser DevTools.
The issue began immediately after updating both macOS and VS Code to the versions listed below:

VS Code: 1.124.2 (Stable)

macOS: 15.5 (Darwin 25.5.0)
canonical: The integrated browser incorrectly resizes and hides docked developer tools during editor focus or content changes, likely because the browser panel is recalculating its layout as if the docked tools were not present.

title: Feature Request/Regression: Extension update lifecycle causes aggressive cache invalidation and hardware resource exhaustion
document:
Feature Request/Regression: Extension update lifecycle causes aggressive cache invalidation and hardware resource exhaustion

Dear VS Code Development Team,I am writing to submit an issue regarding how VS Code's extension update mechanism interacts with local, resource-intensive extensions (specifically local AI/LLM indexing tools like Kilo Code).Whenever VS Code updates itself or automatically updates an AI indexing extension, the extension's local storage and cache state are routinely orphaned or invalidated. Because I rely on local LLMs for code embeddings, this forced cache loss triggers an aggressive, automatic re-indexing loop on startup. This pegs my laptop’s CPU/GPU at 100% compute for an extended period, destroying battery life and severely disrupting my active development workflow.The Technical IssueWhile I understand that VS Code isolates extension versions into clean directories (e.g., migrating from ~/.vscode/extensions/publisher.extension-1.1.0 to 1.2.0), the platform's current management of global/workspace storage caches (ExtensionContext.globalStorageUri and ExtensionContext.storageUri) seems to trigger aggressive purges or path disconnections during specific update lifecycles.When the extension boots up post-update, the underlying platform state fails to maintain the pointer to the previously computed database. Because VS Code handles these updates aggressively and silently in the background, users have no way to defer or prevent these destructive cache resets.Requested Enhancements / SafeguardsTo protect hardware performance and stop silent resource exhaustion, please consider the following platform safeguards:Persistent State Bridging: Ensure that updates across minor extension versions guarantee strict continuity and preservation of data stored inside globalStorageUri. Extensions should not lose access to their heavy local databases due to platform migration quirks.Resource-Aware Update Throttling: Provide a clear, explicit setting or API that allows extensions to flag themselves as "High Compute / Local Model Reliant." When flagged, VS Code should block silent background updates and instead prompt the user with a warning: "Updating this extension will require re-indexing your workspace. Proceed now?"Workspace Integrity Check: Provide a more robust way for the platform to preserve local index mappings so extensions can seamlessly reconnect to external/global caches post-migration without assuming a blank-slate state.The current "silent update followed by massive background compute spike" behavior makes developing with local AI tools incredibly disruptive. I look forward to seeing the VS Code platform evolve to better handle the heavy, stateful storage needs of modern local AI tools.Sincerely, Magnus
canonical: Updating an extension causes its persisted local storage or cache to be lost or disconnected, forcing the extension to rebuild heavy indexes and consume excessive CPU or GPU resources on startup.

title: Add a Directory of Free AI Coding Assistants and Alternatives
document:
Add a Directory of Free AI Coding Assistants and Alternatives

Many students and beginner developers rely on AI coding assistants such as GitHub Copilot. However, when free credits expire, users often need to search for alternative tools manually.

It would be helpful if VS Code provided a dedicated section or marketplace category that highlights free AI coding assistants and their available plans. This could include information about:

**Free AI coding tools compatible with VS Code
Student-friendly offers and GitHub Student Developer Pack benefits
Free trial availability and usage limits
Recommended alternatives when credits expire**

This feature would help developers discover useful coding assistants more easily and improve the overall VS Code experience, especially for students and new programmers.

**Benefits for VS Code:**
This feature can improve user experience, help students discover useful AI coding assistants, increase engagement with the VS Code Marketplace, and encourage more developers to use VS Code as their primary development environment.
canonical: VS Code lacks a dedicated marketplace section for discovering free or student-friendly AI coding assistants and alternative tools, making it harder for users to find options when paid credits run out.

title: Smoke test Electron crash (SIGSEGV) on relaunch — fontconfig/expat font init in browser process
document:
Smoke test Electron crash (SIGSEGV) on relaunch — fontconfig/expat font init in browser process

🤖 generated based on a failing smoke test in CI

---

## Summary

A Linux Electron smoke test failed because the **relaunched** Electron **browser (main) process crashed with SIGSEGV during startup**, before the test driver could attach over the DevTools WebSocket. The minidump shows the top of the crashing stack is in **libexpat + libfontconfig**, i.e. fontconfig parsing its XML config via expat during font initialization.

This is almost certainly **infrastructure flakiness, not a product bug** — and it's unrelated to the PR it failed on (#322170 only touches Agents Window smoke tests). The CI already carries a workaround for expat 2.6.1 NULL-deref CVEs in fontconfig parsing, yet the crash still occurred.

## Links

- **Failing job:** https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/actions/runs/27850813533/job/82429344900
- **Workflow run:** https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/actions/runs/27850813533
- **Crash-dump artifact (minidump):** https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/actions/runs/27850813533/artifacts/7759317546
- **Logs artifact:** https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/actions/runs/27850813533/artifacts/7759333287
- **Minidump path inside artifact:**
 `smoke-tests-electron/4_suite_test_verifies_that_hot_exit_works_for_dirty_files_with_delay/pending/430a921f-8492-43e3-b347-df0a0fa978e5.dmp`

## Failing test

(46 passing, 63 pending, **1 failing**; step exited 255)

The first app instance shut down cleanly (`main.log` shows `app.quit()` at `22:19:05`). The failure is on `Application.restart()` → the **relaunched** process at `22:19:06`:

## Minidump analysis

Parsed from the minidump (timestamp `Fri Jun 19 22:19:06 2026`, matching the relaunch):

- **Signal:** `EXCEPTION_SIGSEGV` (0xb), `ExceptionAddress: 0x0`
- **Crashing process:** browser/main process (`code-oss`, with `libgtk-3`, `libffmpeg`, `libnss3`, full browser module set loaded)
- **RIP:** `0x7fe0a1048806` → `libc.so.6+0x48806`
- **RSP:** `0x7fdf7a3e9a90`, **RBP:** `0x7fdf7a3e9ac0`

**Top of the crashing-thread stack** (module+offset; no symbols available in CI build):

(Frames #1–#5 are contiguous near RSP and consistent; deeper frames are from a naive stack scan and less reliable.) The expat → fontconfig chain ending in a libc fault strongly indicates a crash while **fontconfig parses font-config XML via expat** during browser-process font init.

## Environment (from job log)

- Runner: Ubuntu 24.04 (noble), x86_64, 4 CPUs
- `libexpat1: 2.6.1-2ubuntu0.4` ← has the known NULL-deref CVEs (fixed upstream in **expat ≥ 2.7.5**)
- `fontconfig / libfontconfig1: 2.15.0-1.1ubuntu2`
- `FONTCONFIG_FILE=/tmp/fonts-minimal.conf` (the CI workaround is active)

The workflow already has a step **"Workaround expat NULL deref CVEs in fontconfig parsing"** that writes a minimal `fonts.conf` with no `` directives and clears the fontconfig cache, precisely because expat 2.6.1 NULL-derefs when fontconfig parses `` via `XML_ExternalEntityParserCreate`. **The crash happened despite this workaround.**

## Hypothesis

The relaunched Electron browser process **SIGSEGVs inside fontconfig's expat-based XML config parsing** at startup. Even with the minimal `FONTCONFIG_FILE` workaround, the buggy `libexpat 2.6.1` is still being exercised (e.g. fontconfig rebuilding its cache after the CI cache-clear, or a parse path the minimal config doesn't avoid), hitting one of the expat NULL-deref bugs. Because it's a font-init crash in the browser process, it surfaces intermittently and only on app relaunch, presenting to Playwright as `electron.launch: WebSocket error: socket hang up`.

## Suggested next steps

- Symbolicate the dump against this build's `code-oss` + bundled libs to confirm the exact expat/fontconfig frames.
- Consider hardening the CI workaround: pin/upgrade `libexpat1` to ≥ 2.7.5 on the runner, or ensure fontconfig never parses the system config (verify the minimal conf fully avoids `` and pre-warm the cache so no rebuild happens mid-run).
- Treat the `Data Loss / hot exit (with delay)` failure as a flaky native crash on relaunch, not a regression in #322170.

---

Want me to also extract the renderer/main logs of the crashing window into the issue, or symbolicate the dump if you can point me at the matching symbol server/build?
canonical: The relaunched Electron browser process crashed during startup because fontconfig, while initializing fonts, hit a null-dereference bug in expat while parsing XML configuration.

title: Test: Cloud Agent - create a pull request on demand
document:
Test: Cloud Agent - create a pull request on demand

# Test: Cloud Agent (v2) — create a pull request on demand

- [x] anyOS @meganrogge
- [x] anyOS @vritant24 

Complexity: 3

Author: @osortega

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23322177%0A%0A&assignees=osortega)

---

## Context

Cloud Agent sessions on the v2 (Task API) backend are now PR-less by default, a session no longer auto-creates a pull request. Instead, you create the PR on demand from the session toolbar once the task has settled, and then open it. This TPI validates that flow.

## Setup

1. Latest VS Code Insiders.
2. Sign in to GitHub Copilot.
3. Set `"github.copilot.chat.cloudAgentBackend.version": "v2"` and reload the window.
4. Open a repo enrolled for Copilot coding agent.

## What to test

1. Start a new cloud task that makes code changes and let it run to completion. Confirm the settled session is PR-less (no PR badge/link, no PR auto-created on GitHub).
2. On the settled, PR-less session, confirm the chat input toolbar shows Create Pull Request button.
3. Invoke Create Pull Request → a PR is created against the correct repo/branch. The toolbar updates and shows Open Pull Request icon appears
4. Verify the changed-files view for the settled task is populated and matches the diff in the created PR.
5. Click Open Pull Request → the correct PR opens in the browser. Confirm it resolves whether the session is from v1 or v2 backend.
6. Reload the window and reopen the same session → it still resolves to the PR, and Open Pull Request is shown.
canonical: The cloud agent session does not reliably create or retain the linked pull request on demand for a settled task, so the session can remain PR-less or fail to show or open the correct pull request afterward.

title: Test: browser isolation in agents window
document:
Test: browser isolation in agents window

Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/321517

- [x] anyOS @sandy081

Complexity: 3

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23322171%0A%0A&assignees=kycutler)

---

Integrated browser tabs in the agents window should be treated as specific to one session.

Each session should have its own set of browser tabs that don't interfere with other sessions.

## Setup

- Ensure the setting `workbench.browser.agentHostChatToolsEnabled` is enabled
- Open two sessions using either Local or Agent Host-based agents

> [!NOTE]
> There is currently an issue with agent host that causes agents to not automatically "see" browser pages that have been shared with them.
> 
> **For scenario 3 below, be sure you test using the Local agent.**

## Scenarios

1. If an agent opens a browser tab while it is **not** the active session, the tab should not be opened in the editor.
 - The tab should still be usable by the agent
 - The tab can be opened by clicking on its label in the tool calls within the chat.

2. If an agent tries to open a browser tab with the same URL that is open in another session, it should open a new tab with no prompting.
 - But if a tab with the same URL is already open in this session, it will either prompt to share the tab or just tell the agent it is already open.

3. Agents should only "see" the browser tabs that are part of their session.
 - Be sure to test this using a Local agent (see above note)

4. Browser commands should operate on the tabs for the current active session.
 - Specifically, pressing `ctrl+alt+/` (`command+option+/` on Mac) should always open a new browser tab if there are none in the current session, or list the open ones for that session if there are any.
canonical: Browser tabs opened by one agent session should be isolated to that session so they do not appear in or affect other sessions, but they are currently being shared or reused across sessions.

title: Test: Voice Mode
document:
Test: Voice Mode

Refs: meganrogge/voice-mode-integration branch

- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Windows
- [ ] Linux

Complexity: 5

Authors: @meganrogge

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+voice+mode%0A%0A&assignees=meganrogge)

---

Voice Mode enables hands-free interaction with chat sessions via push-to-talk, speech recognition, and text-to-speech responses.

## Prerequisites
- Enable `agents.voice.enabled` in settings
- Microphone access available

## Connection & Basic Flow

- [ ] Connect to voice mode via the mic icon in chat input
- [ ] Verify "Connecting..." state shows, then transitions to idle with PTT keybinding hint (e.g. "Press Ctrl+Shift+Space to talk")
- [ ] Hold the PTT keybinding (Ctrl+Shift+Space) and speak — verify transcription appears in real-time
- [ ] Release PTT — verify request is sent and response is read aloud (TTS)
- [ ] Verify mic icon is colored blue while listening, purple while speaking
- [ ] Disconnect via the disconnect button — verify green disconnect icon in chat input

## Aux Window

- [ ] Open voice mode aux window (floating pill)
- [ ] Verify it appears centered above the VS Code window bottom
- [ ] Verify the window can be dragged/moved
- [ ] Verify PTT works in the aux window with same keybinding (Ctrl+Shift+Space)
- [ ] Verify transcript shows in aux window (user input + assistant response)
- [ ] Verify processing animation (comet border beam) shows while waiting for response
- [ ] Verify the window does not move/jump when toggling session list expand/collapse
- [ ] Verify session list is scrollable when many sessions exist (max-height constraint)
- [ ] Verify toolbar and transcript always remain visible below session list

## Session Targeting (Aux Window)

- [ ] Expand session list in aux window — verify sessions are listed
- [ ] Click a session — verify it becomes the voice target AND is revealed in the chat panel
- [ ] Speak with a non-focused session targeted — verify request goes to correct session
- [ ] Verify response is read aloud even when targeting a non-focused session
- [ ] Verify transcript in chat input is suppressed when aux window targets a different session

## Confirmations via Voice

- [ ] When a session has a pending confirmation, say "approve" or "reject"
- [ ] Verify the confirmation is handled correctly even if the session is not focused
- [ ] Verify voice backend narrates the result after approval/rejection

## Error & Edge Cases

- [ ] Disconnect network mid-session — verify reconnection behavior
- [ ] Deny microphone permission — verify appropriate error state
- [ ] Rapidly toggle PTT — verify no crashes or stuck states
- [ ] Close aux window while speaking — verify graceful cleanup
canonical: Voice mode has a bug where the floating voice window or push-to-talk flow does not reliably stay synced with the active session, causing input, output, or session targeting to go to the wrong place or get interrupted when the window or session focus changes.

title: Copilot Chat: Session debug logs empty (1 byte) - no chat history titles generated
document:
Copilot Chat: Session debug logs empty (1 byte) - no chat history titles generated

## Bug Description
Chat session history titles are not being generated. All session debug logs are empty (1 byte), resulting in no session titles appearing in the chat history UI.

## Expected Behavior
- Session debug logs should capture chat content
- Session titles should auto-generate within ~1 minute
- Chat history should display sessions with generated titles

## Actual Behavior
- All 88 indexed sessions have empty summaries/titles
- Session debug log files are created but contain only 1 byte
- No metadata captured (cwd, repository, agent_name all NULL)
- Session history UI shows no sessions

## Diagnostic Data

**VS Code Version:** 1.125.1 (fcf604774b) 
**Platform:** Windows x64 
**Affected Sessions:** 88 (all sessions since 2026-06-18)

**Session Database Query Results:**


**Session Debug Log Status:**
- Current session ID: `497fa7a8-11af-49b7-88d1-406459f5ffd0`
- Log file exists: ✅ Yes
- Log file size: ❌ 1 byte (empty)
- Path: `C:\Users\jdickson\AppData\Roaming\Code\User\workspaceStorage\...\GitHub.copilot-chat\debug-logs\`

**Reindex Attempt:**
- Ran `session_store_sql` reindex operation
- Result: +2 sessions indexed, but still 0 titles generated
- Issue persists after reindex

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start any chat session in VS Code
2. Wait ~1-5 minutes
3. Check chat history UI
4. Observe: No session titles appear
5. Query session database: All summary fields are empty/NULL

## Impact
- **Critical:** Users cannot navigate chat history
- Cannot resume previous conversations from UI
- Session context is lost between restarts
- Work product must be manually saved to files

## Workaround
Manually save important chat outputs to files (the saved files are preserved).

## Timeline
- **Issue started:** ~2026-06-18 (all sessions since then affected)
- **Likely trigger:** Recent VS Code or Copilot extension update around this date

## Additional Context
- Session database is successfully tracking session IDs and timestamps
- The session metadata capture/logging mechanism appears to be silently failing
- No visible errors in VS Code or Copilot extension
- Permissions appear intact (files are created, just not written to)

## Request
Please investigate why session content logging stopped working, resulting in empty debug logs and no session title generation.
canonical: The Copilot Chat session logger is creating empty debug log files and failing to capture session metadata, which prevents automatic chat history title generation and leaves session history blank.

title: Instead of:
document:
Instead of:

Instead of:

> "The application shall sanitize all untrusted inputs before processing."

Perhaps:

> "The application shall implement controls to ensure that processing untrusted inputs does not compromise the application's security boundaries."

This is technique-neutral. A desktop email client satisfies it by sanitizing HTML in email bodies. A browser satisfies it by pointing to sandboxing, site isolation, and same-origin  enforcement. A PDF reader satisfies it by validating document structure before parsing. Each application demonstrates compliance through whatever mechanism is appropriate to its threat  model.

The tradeoff: Outcome-based language is harder to audit than prescriptive language. "Did you sanitize inputs?" is a yes/no question a lab can test. "Did you implement controls to protect security boundaries?" requires the applicant to articulate their threat model and the assessor to evaluate whether the controls are adequate. If we need a concrete checklist, we might need to add guidance like:

"Acceptable controls include, but are not limited to: input validation and sanitization, sandboxing, process isolation, and privilege separation."

_Originally posted by @alex941115 in https://github.com/appdefensealliance/ASA-WG/pull/306#discussion_r3175301472_
canonical: The issue is that a security requirement was rewritten from a concrete, testable input-sanitization rule into vague outcome-based wording that is harder to audit and enforce consistently.

VS Code now surfaces **session-level token usage**, similar to the Copilot CLI's `/usage`. This supplements the existing per-turn credit consumption and context-window indicators to give a fuller picture of what a chat session has consumed.

# Task

**Validate that session-level token usage is reported and is accurate.**

- Start a new agent/chat session and run several turns.
- Open the session token usage view and confirm:
 - [ ] Total session token consumption is shown and increases as turns are added
 - [ ] The session-level numbers are consistent with the per-turn credit / context-window info already shown
- Confirm the data resets / starts fresh for a new session.
- Try across model configurations (different context sizes / reasoning efforts) and verify the usage view still behaves correctly.
canonical: Session-level token usage is not being reported correctly, with the session totals failing to update accurately or reset properly across chat sessions and model configurations.

title: VS Code allows incompatible local extension installation into Linux Codespace, leading to platform mismatch and "bad image format" errors
document:
VS Code allows incompatible local extension installation into Linux Codespace, leading to platform mismatch and "bad image format" errors

The issue appears to be related to installation of a platform-specific extension into a remote Codespace environment.

- VS Code Version: 1.125.1
- OS Version: Host: Windows; Remote: GitHub Codespace (Linux/Ubuntu)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start a Linux GitHub Codespace from a Windows machine.
2. Connect to the Codespace from an environment where Extension Marketplace access is unavailable or restricted.
3. Attempt to install C# Dev Kit using the local installation workflow ("Install from Local" / local VSIX installation).
4. Open a .NET project in the Codespace.
5. Attempt to use C# Dev Kit functionality (debugging, project operations, etc.).

## Expected Behavior

When connected to a Linux Codespace, VS Code should either:

- Install the Linux-compatible version of the extension, or
- Detect that the locally installed extension targets a different platform and block the installation (or provide a clear warning).

## Actual Behavior

VS Code appeared to accept the local installation, but the resulting environment appeared to contain a mix of Windows and Linux components. The extension subsequently produced errors consistent with a platform mismatch, including "bad image format" failures when attempting to use C# Dev Kit functionality. This made the root cause difficult to diagnose.

The failure occurred at runtime rather than during installation. Had VS Code detected and blocked the platform mismatch during the installation workflow, the root cause would have been immediately obvious.

## Additional Context

The following screenshot shows the BadImageFormatException observed within the Linux Codespace environment after the local extension installation workflow was used.

While troubleshooting with members of the C# Dev Kit team, the symptoms were determined to be consistent with a Windows-targeted extension being used within a Linux Codespace environment.

The issue was ultimately resolved by creating a fresh Codespace from an environment with access to the Extension Marketplace and installing the extensions normally. After doing so, C# Dev Kit functioned as expected.

One team member suggested that VS Code should not allow local installation when a platform-specific extension package is incompatible with the target environment, as the current behavior can lead users into a difficult-to-diagnose configuration.

## Suggested Improvement

During local extension installation, validate the extension's target platform against the remote execution environment (Codespaces, SSH, Dev Containers, etc.).

If the extension is incompatible:

- Block installation, or

title: Codex panel stuck on loading spinner — IpcClient Initialize timeout with multiple VSCode windows
document:
Codex panel stuck on loading spinner — IpcClient Initialize timeout with multiple VSCode windows

### Summary
The Codex panel hangs forever on the loading spinner (logo only, chat never loads).

### Environment
- Codex extension: openai.chatgpt-26.616.31447 (linux-x64)
- codex-cli: 0.142.0-alpha.1
- OS: Linux 6.17 (x86_64)
- VSCode: desktop

### What happened
With two VSCode windows open, each window spawned its own `codex app-server`
process. The extension's IPC initialize timed out and the CODEX panel stayed
on the loading spinner indefinitely, with no error shown in the UI.

### Relevant log lines (exthost/openai.chatgpt/Codex.log)
[info] [CodexMcpConnection] Spawning codex app-server (x2, one per window)
[warning] [IpcClient] Initialize failed errorMessage=timeout
 at Timeout._onTimeout (.../out/extension.js:481:18725)
[warning] [IpcClient] Received broadcast but no handler is configured method=client-status-changed
[warning] cli: ... featured plugin request ... failed with status 401 Unauthorized

### Workaround that fixed it
1. Killed both stale `codex app-server` processes (`pkill -f 'codex app-server'`)
2. Reloaded the window (Developer: Reload Window), keeping a single VSCode window
3. Signed in to Codex

### Suspected issues
1. IpcRouter contention when multiple VSCode windows run the extension → duplicate
 app-servers and an Initialize timeout.
2. On Initialize timeout the UI shows only a spinner with no error/retry/sign-in
 prompt, so the user has no indication of what's wrong.
canonical: The Codex panel can get stuck on a loading spinner because multiple editor windows start separate background server processes, causing the IPC initialization to time out and leaving the extension with no recovery or error handling in the UI.

Hi,

I have an public extension template in https://dev.azure.com/softera-baltic/ExternalTools/_git/SofteraBalticPSTools?path=/Files/ExtensionTemplate and a skill file in https://dev.azure.com/softera-baltic/ExternalTools/_git/SofteraBalticPSTools?path=/Files/ExtensionTemplate/.agents/skills/external/ExtensionTemplateMigration.SKILL.md. title: VS Code symbol auto-complete is disabled by Copilot Ghost text suggestions
document:
VS Code symbol auto-complete is disabled by Copilot Ghost text suggestions

# Steps needed to reliably reproduce the problem

1. Enable Copilot / Inline Suggestions / Ghost text suggestions.
2. Focus on an editor window labelled with a language for which Copilot Suggestions are enabled.
3. Type text in an area for which VS Code provides auto-completion of symbols and snippets (_e.g._, not in a comment block).
4. Whether or not Copilot in fact produces a Ghost text suggestion, VS Code will not display auto-complete options.

# An exception that is probably not an exception

1. Enable Copilot / Inline Suggestions / Ghost text suggestions.
2. Enable Copilot Suggestions for Python language.
3. Enable ms-python.python Python language server.
4. Alternatively, enable charliermarsh.ruff Python language server. 
5. Focus on an editor window labelled as Python language.
6. Type "import" in an area the language server will interpret "import" as the Python keyword "import".
7. Alternatively, type "from" in an area the language server will interpret "from" as the Python keyword "from" that will be coupled with the Python keyword "import" (_i.e._ `ast.ImportFrom`).
8. Type the identifier (_i.e._ `ast.Name`) of a package for which the language server has an index of symbols, followed by ".".
9. Alternatively, type "." if the namespace of the focused window resolves to a package for which the language server has an index of symbols.
10. The language server(s) will display auto-complete options: limited to the language server's knowledge of the namespace and the subset of attributes of the namespace the language server is configured to display (the language server might only display, for example, attributes defined in a `.pyi` file).

# Troubleshooting details

In addition to troubleshooting implied by the above description:

- Onset: 2-3 months ago.
- Consistent, not intermittent, since onset.
- Did not occur before onset.
- No change of any kind is known to be associated with the onset.
- No other problems are known to be associated with the onset.
- No other problems are known to be mitigated by disabling Ghost text suggestions.
- OS, VS Code, and extensions are up to date and have been updated regularly since onset.
- Searched for, but did not find, VS Code settings related to the problem.

# See also

#308464
#316305

---
canonical: Copilot inline ghost text suggestions are suppressing VS Code’s normal symbol and snippet autocomplete popup in the editor, preventing completion options from appearing.

title: editor.insertSnippet() still destroys active snippet session tabstops (regression from #214757, misclosed)
document:
editor.insertSnippet() still destroys active snippet session tabstops (regression from #214757, misclosed)

- VS Code Version: 1.86.0+ through current (regression introduced in 1.86, works correctly in 1.85.2)
- OS Version: All platforms

## Summary

`editor.insertSnippet()` called from an extension while a snippet session is active destroys the outer session's remaining tabstops. This is a regression from 1.86.

This was previously reported as #214757, which was closed as a duplicate of #63129. However, **these are unrelated issues**: #63129 is about snippet auto-indentation (fixed in #234858 by exposing `keepWhitespace`), not tabstop destruction. Since #214757 is locked, I'm opening a new issue.

## Root cause

In 1.86, `mainThreadEditor.ts` was refactored to route extension API `insertSnippet()` calls through `snippetController.apply(edits[])` instead of `snippetController.insert(template)`.

In `snippetController2.ts`, `_doInsert()` has this guard:

The `apply()` path passes an array, hitting `this.cancel()` and destroying the active session. The `insert()` path passes a string, hitting `this._session.merge()` which properly nests the new snippet. The merge infrastructure (`OneSnippet.merge()`, `_nestingLevel`, `_renormalizePlaceholderIndices()`) is fully implemented and works — the API bridge just stopped using it.

## Impact

This breaks any extension that programmatically inserts snippets during editing:
- [HyperSnips](https://github.com/draivin/hsnips/issues/189) (77 comments, open since Feb 2024)
- [HyperSnips #157](https://github.com/draivin/hsnips/issues/157)
- [LTeX](https://github.com/valentjn/vscode-ltex) and other language extensions

## Fix

I have a PR ready: #322008

The fix is ~5 lines: route single-edit `insertSnippet()` calls back through `snippetController.insert()` (the merge path), falling back to `apply()` only for multi-range edits that cannot be merged.

cc @jrieken 

---

1. Install any extension that calls `editor.insertSnippet()` in response to typing (e.g. [HyperSnips](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=draivin.hsnips))
2. Trigger a snippet with multiple tabstops (e.g. a snippet that expands to `\int_{$1}^{$2} $3 \,d$4`)
3. While the cursor is at `$1`, type text that triggers the extension to call `editor.insertSnippet()` with a simple replacement
4. Press Tab — expected: cursor moves to `$2`. Actual: snippet session is destroyed, Tab inserts a tab character.

This works correctly in VS Code 1.85.2 and earlier.

---

This issue was researched and analysed with the assistance of [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code) (Claude Opus 4.6).
canonical: Calling the extension snippet insertion API while a snippet session is active incorrectly cancels the outer snippet session and destroys its remaining tabstops because the edit path no longer merges into the active snippet.

Chats in history might change order and seem to (or truly) disappear and reappear

- Copilot Chat Extension Version:"The GitHub Copilot Chat extension is now bundled into VS Code itself (starting with VS Code 1.100+). It's no longer a separate installable extension — it ships as a built-in component. Your built-in Copilot Chat version matches your VS Code 1.124.2 release."
- VS Code Version: 1.124.2 (x64), commit 6928394f
- OS Version: Windows 11 (NT 10.0.26200.0)
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude Opus 4.6
- Logs: Sorry didn't have logs on and it doesn't always reproduce. Have logs on now, though.

Might be partially the same issue here with a different part to it, or maybe a different issue.
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/319416

I haven't been able to get a screen shot but with a longer list of history filling the screen, the chat seems to disappear and later pop back up (reappear). I believe I have seen it actually show up at the top of the list, in my case under "OLDER". Even if it was a 2 month old chat it might show up at the top of the list before all the 1 week ones. But then it will pop back down later in its regular order later. My guess is that these popping out of order are running and doing something with the status that makes them change sort for a sec. If you were at the top of the list already you might not even see it or care. But if you have scrolled down enough, it will seem to disappear and reappear. 

My expectation would be that if the time the chat was created is what is displayed, like "1 wk ago", even if the chat is doing something else, it was still created 1 wk ago and shouldn't reorder in the list...and then go back to where it was. Maybe some field for how old it is that is being used for sorting is being used that makes it resort. ??? HTH

So working on Root cause and it disappears. I scroll up and think it is at the top of the list but then a sec later it is not.

This part of it might be what the other bug is about because I noticed the number change like in theirs...

then later

But it does come back.

Incidentally, the box eventually comes back for me and I don't have a permanent loss of chat history like some other bugs mention. Just an individual chat box moving around on the right history side
UPDATE: Well, I lied. Just was clicking around and had one go away and it has not come back. Using same example as screen shot above, the "Bluebird" one came back and then "Azure SQL" one disappears and has not come back yet as I click around

UPDATE 2: clicking around and/or about 10 mins later #4 showed back up. So I lied about lying. It did still eventually come back but that was the longest time I have seen where I really thought this time it was gone for good.

1. Sorry, not 100% sure but probably clicking around to different tabs sending in a prompt for 1 or more of the chats to work on.
2. Stare at chats and click around and hope you see one disappear, reappear and/or change sort order.
canonical: Chat history entries are being sorted by a mutable status field tied to activity instead of staying fixed by creation time, causing items to jump, disappear, and reappear in the list.

title: Global, local-device search across Copilot Chat conversations
document:
Global, local-device search across Copilot Chat conversations

**- Summary:** I frequently use Copilot Chat across multiple VS Code windows for the same projects. I often remember topics I discussed but can’t recall which window or which chat contains the relevant thread. There’s no built-in way to search across all saved Copilot conversations on my device, making it hard to rediscover past decisions or follow-ups.

**- Problem:** Currently I must manually export or copy transcripts from each window, save them to a folder, and then search with external tools. This is error-prone, inconvenient, and not something I can always predict to do ahead of time.

**- Proposed solution:** Add a local, per-device indexed search for Copilot Chats:
 - A global search box in the Copilot pane that searches all conversations stored locally (across VS Code windows/workspaces) and shows the matching conversation + message excerpt.
 - An indexed, local-only storage (opt-in) that persists chat metadata (title, tags, workspace, timestamp) in extension globalStorage so search is fast.
 **- Optional features:** per-chat tags/bookmarks, star/favorite, export all, and filters (workspace, date, starred).
 **- Privacy guarantee:** default to local-only storage; any cloud sync should be opt-in and explicit.
 
**- Benefits:** Faster context recovery, fewer duplicate questions, better continuity across windows and days, and improved developer productivity.

**- Notes for implementers:** Conversations appear to already be stored by the extension in VS Code storage; an indexer + small UI in the Copilot pane could surface results quickly. Consider making indexing opt-in and allow users to exclude certain workspaces.
 
**- Environment:** macOS, VS Code (latest)
canonical: Copilot Chat lacks a built-in local search and indexing feature to find past conversations across all saved chats and windows on a device.

title: policy: surface managed-settings projection warnings in diagnostics
document:
policy: surface managed-settings projection warnings in diagnostics

When `adaptManagedSettings` processes the raw `/copilot_internal/managed_settings` response, several steps silently drop or transform data:

- `flattenManagedSettings` silently drops non-scalar, non-object values (arrays, nulls) — no feedback when a key is ignored
- `normalizeExtraKnownMarketplaces` validates the marketplace structure and calls `onWarn` for some issues, but the warnings are only logged at `warn` level — not surfaced in diagnostics
- Unknown top-level keys pass through flattening without any indication they will be dropped by the policy pipeline downstream

### Proposal

1. Thread a warnings collector through `flattenManagedSettings` and `normalizeExtraKnownMarketplaces`
2. Store the collected warnings alongside the raw response in the provider (similar to `_managedSettingsRawResponse` added in #321871)
3. Surface them in the **Developer: Policy Diagnostics** report as a "Warnings" section between the raw response and the processed output
4. Include the key path and reason for each dropped/transformed value (e.g. "strictKnownMarketplaces: dropped (array, expected boolean)", "extraKnownMarketplaces.my-marketplace.source: missing required field repo")

This would make the mock policy server → Code OSS feedback loop much tighter — right now you have to cross-reference the raw vs processed output manually to figure out what went wrong.

Related PR: #321871 (mock policy server GUI + raw response in diagnostics)
canonical: Managed settings diagnostics currently hide warnings from validation and flattening, so dropped or transformed values and malformed marketplace entries are not surfaced in the policy report even though they are silently discarded by the processing pipeline.

title: Improve contrast and discoverability of Activity Bar / Side Bar icons (UX)
document:
Improve contrast and discoverability of Activity Bar / Side Bar icons (UX)

Problem

Some Activity Bar and Side Bar icons have low contrast and poor discoverability, making it hard for users—especially those with visual impairments—to find and understand available views and extensions. This leads to confusion and slows down workflow.

Proposal

Increase visual contrast and improve affordances for Activity Bar and Side Bar icons by:
- Adjusting icon color/contrast (for both active and inactive states) to meet WCAG AA where possible.
- Adding clearer hover/focus states (outline, subtle background, or tooltip timing improvements).
- Improving visual separation between groups of icons (e.g., file explorer vs extensions) to make scanning faster.

1. Open VS Code with the default theme and a project loaded.
2. Look at the Activity Bar and Side Bar area and try to quickly locate the Extensions and Source Control icons.
3. Toggle high-contrast OS theme or enable a low-contrast custom theme to observe discoverability regressions.

Expected behavior

- Icons and their interactive states should be clearly visible and discoverable across themes, including high-contrast and popular custom themes.
- Hover and focus states should make it obvious that the icon is interactive without being visually jarring.

Acceptance criteria

- Updated icon color and state styles that improve measured contrast (WCAG AA where feasible).
- Clearer hover/focus affordances implemented and tested across built-in themes.
- No regressions introduced to layout or accessibility; manual verification steps documented.

Notes

- This is a UX improvement request; consider consulting the accessibility guidelines and running quick contrast checks on key themes.
- Assigning to @eli-w-king for initial investigation and a small design proposal.

Environment

- Repository: microsoft/vscode
- Labels: ux

[View original Slack conversation](https://vscodeteam.slack.com/archives/C09E1AKMB1P/p1781742745130709?thread_ts=1781742745.130709&cid=C09E1AKMB1P)
canonical: Activity Bar and Side Bar icons are too low-contrast and have weak hover/focus affordances, making them hard to discover and distinguish across themes, especially for visually impaired users.

title: Improve chat input UX for multiple sessions
document:
Improve chat input UX for multiple sessions

Summary
Users working with multiple chat sessions have many small input boxes which are hard to use. This proposal improves the input UX so the focused session’s input is clear and easier to navigate with the keyboard.

Problem
- When many chat sessions are open each has a small input area, making typing and focus management difficult.
- A single large shared input can feel disconnected from the conversation for some users.
- It’s currently unclear which input belongs to which pane when many are present.

Proposed ideas to explore
- Focused-only input: show the input box only for the focused session and visually de-emphasize or “push aside” adjacent sessions (a curtain/slide effect) to reduce clutter and make focus obvious.
- Shared input (alternative): a single shared input that clearly indicates the active session and supports keyboard navigation between sessions. This has some detractors who find it disconnected.
- Keyboard navigation: provide keyboard shortcuts to cycle focus between sessions and explicitly select the active session; make the focused input visually larger and clearly labeled (e.g., “Changes”, “Browser”).

Benefits
- Reduces visual clutter when working with many sessions.
- Makes typing and focus management easier and faster.
- Preserves context by keeping input visually attached to the active conversation.

Acceptance criteria (examples)
- Design prototypes for at least the “focused-only input” and the “curtain” behavior.
- Keyboard navigation between sessions (cycle focus, explicit select).
- Usability feedback gathered from a small set of users before broader rollout.

Context
This came from a Slack thread discussing alternatives (single shared input vs. showing only the focused input and a curtain effect). Multiple participants preferred showing input only for the focused session.

[View original Slack conversation](https://vscodeteam.slack.com/archives/C09E1AKMB1P/p1781654502211019?thread_ts=1781654502.211019&cid=C09E1AKMB1P)
canonical: The chat interface becomes cluttered and hard to use when multiple sessions each show their own small input, making it unclear which pane is active and difficult to manage focus from the keyboard.

1. Open VSCODE
2. Select Extension
3. Run accessibility checker on it

**Screenshots for views showing this error:**

Outline default view showing same issue:

**Rationale:**
This requirement ensures that Assistive Technologies (AT) can gather information about, activate, set, and receive status of all user interface components.

**Note:** This requirement is primarily for developers who develop or script their own custom user interface components. Standard HTML controls already meet this requirement when used according to specification.

Custom interactive user interface components often do not expose proper role, state, and property information needed for assistive technologies. As a result, people with disabilities have difficulty identifying and interacting with the components. Common examples are custom menus, trees, data grids, accordions, and dialog boxes. Ensuring proper markup and keyboard operation enables people with disabilities to identify and interact with the custom components in the same manner they identify and interact with standard components.
canonical: A custom view menu item is exposing an invalid accessibility label on an element with a generic implicit role, causing assistive technologies to fail to identify its name and role properly.

title: Gemma models return reasoning in `<thought>` tags – UI does not format/hide them
document:
Gemma models return reasoning in `<thought>` tags – UI does not format/hide them

When using Gemma models (e.g., `gemma-4-27b-a4b-it`, `gemma-4-21b-it`) via the Google AI API , the model includes its reasoning/chain-of-thought inside `...` tags. The extension currently only strips or formats tags like `` or ``, leaving `` content rendered as plain text, which clutters the chat UI and breaks the intended clean display.

**Environment:** 
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.54.0
- VS Code Version: 
- OS Version: windows 11
- Feature: Ask/Edit mode (both) 
- Selected model: Gemma 4 31B and 27B (via custom endpoint)

**Steps to Reproduce:** 
1. Configure the extension to use the Google AI API with a Gemma model. 
2. Send a prompt that triggers reasoning (e.g., a math problem or coding puzzle). 
3. Observe the response – the model outputs a `` block containing its internal reasoning, followed by the final answer. 
4. The `` content appears as raw text in the chat panel, not collapsed or styled.

**Expected Behavior:** 
The extension should treat `` tags similarly to ``/`` – i.e., either hide them entirely (since they are internal reasoning) or render them in a collapsible/separate section with a distinct style (e.g., grayed out, collapsed by default). This would keep the final answer clean and readable.

**Actual Behavior:** 
The entire `` block is displayed verbatim, making the response messy and harder to parse, especially for lengthy reasoning.

**Workaround (for now):** 
I cloned the copilot repo and applied support for . 

Here is before 

Here is after

**Additional Context:** 
This affects all Gemma models served by Google . The model’s official documentation confirms it uses `` for internal reasoning. Many other tools (e.g., Ollama, LM Studio) already support stripping these tags, so it would be great if this extension could do the same.
canonical: The chat UI fails to recognize and hide or specially render model reasoning enclosed in thought tags, so that internal reasoning is shown as plain text instead of being collapsed or omitted.

title: Corporate Controls: Whitelist/Block Extensions by Tag
document:
Corporate Controls: Whitelist/Block Extensions by Tag

The release of 1.125 introduces third party model providers tag:"language-models", this causes a big problem for corporate companies because secure controlled GitHub Copilot now becomes the wild west of users sending sensitive data to anyone who claims to be a model provider.

I realise through InTune/Group policy it's possible to whitelist the extensions but this is a massive job in a large organisation in order to meet all the users needs. There doesn't seem to be a way to turn off installing language models so I propose that extensions should be able to be blacklisted and/or whitelisted by tag which would go a long way to regaining control.
canonical: Users can still install third-party language model extensions in managed environments because there is no way to block or allow extensions by tag, which makes it hard to control access to external model providers.

title: Chat implicit "active selection" context stays stuck after its source document is closed/deleted
document:
Chat implicit "active selection" context stays stuck after its source document is closed/deleted

Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.53.0

Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent mode (applies to all chat modes)
Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): All models (model-independent)
Logs: na (no related entries in Copilot Chat log — see Debug data)

1. Open a document and select some text → it attaches as the implicit "active selection" context chip in the Chat input.
2. Delete or close that document while its editor stays focused.
3. Submit a chat turn → the stale selection is still attached.
4. Try to remove it via "unpin" in the chat window → it only suppresses for one send and re-attaches next turn.
( Symbol pin is still "not attached" though, but visible in Chat window and also llm agrees to see it fresh again. )
5. Focus another editor chat window → the chip frequently persists. Cannot get rid of it.
6. Make a fresh text selection / copy text in another editor (or open a real file with a selection) → only now does the chip clear.

##### Remark: _WriteUp via Opus 4.8 of the situation was performend from here on

**Summary**

The Chat input's implicit "active selection" context is bound to the last active text selection and is only invalidated on the next selection-change event. Closing or deleting the backing document emits no such event, so the chip remains attached and keeps being sent on every turn. "Unpin" does not remove it permanently, and merely focusing another editor is often insufficient.

**Current Behaviour**
After the source document is closed/deleted, the active-selection chip lingers in the input and re-attaches each turn. It is only cleared when a new selection-change fires elsewhere (selecting/copying text in another editor, or opening a real file with a selection). Reproduced with both a memory:-scheme virtual document and a regular workspace file.

**Expected Behaviour**
When the backing document of an implicit active-selection context is closed or deleted, the context should be invalidated immediately. Unpinning the chip should also remove it permanently rather than for a single submission.

**Workaround**
Trigger a fresh selection-change — select or copy text in another editor, or open a different file — which replaces the stale reference. Alternatively start a New Chat or reload the window.

**Impact**
Stale, unremovable context silently pollutes every subsequent turn, wasting tokens and skewing the model's grounding toward a deleted document, with no reliable UI affordance to clear it.

**Debug data**
Reviewed GitHub.copilot-chat/GitHub Copilot Chat.log across all windows for the session — no related error/warning is logged, consistent with a renderer-side chat-widget state issue rather than an extension fault. Only unrelated entries were present ([CopilotCLIModels] 401 Unauthorized: Bad credentials at startup, and a benign Error compiling input schema for tool run_in_terminal: unknown keyword "enumDescriptions").
canonical: The chat input keeps holding onto a stale implicit active-selection context after its source document is closed or deleted because that selection is only cleared by a later selection-change event, so it keeps reattaching on subsequent turns.

title: Intermittent 20–50 second unresponsive startup on macOS; reproduces with extensions disabled and fresh user-data directory
document:
Intermittent 20–50 second unresponsive startup on macOS; reproduces with extensions disabled and fresh user-data directory

## Description

VS Code intermittently opens its window quickly but remains slow or unresponsive for approximately 20–50 seconds before becoming usable.

The problem is intermittent. Reopening the same repository is often fast, so a single successful startup does not demonstrate that a configuration has fixed the issue. It occurs across different repositories rather than one specific large workspace.

In some slow runs, `Developer: Startup Performance` records a 35–52 second delay in extension registration. In other visibly slow runs, including one captured with `--prof-startup`, the startup performance marks report only approximately 1–2 seconds and do not contain the full user-visible delay.

## Environment

* VS Code: 1.124.2
* Commit: 6928394f91b684055b873eecb8bc281365131f1c
* OS reported by VS Code: Darwin 25.5.0
* Hardware: Apple M3 Pro, 36 GB RAM

The problem was also seen with VS Code 1.123.0.

## Isolation already performed

The problem still occurs after:

* Disabling all marketplace extensions

* Starting with `--disable-extensions`

* Starting with a new temporary user-data directory

* Combining both:

 `code -n --disable-extensions --user-data-dir /tmp/vscode-test-userdata `

* Reinstalling VS Code

* Testing multiple repositories

One clean-user-data and extensions-disabled run recorded:

* `shared process connection ready`: approximately 35 seconds
* `register extensions & spawn extension host`: approximately 36 seconds
* `extensions registered`: approximately 37 seconds

Another run with marketplace extensions disabled recorded:

* `shared process connection ready`: 24 ms
* `register extensions & spawn extension host`: approximately 52 seconds
* `extensions registered`: approximately 52 seconds

In that second run, the extension host appeared to be waiting for initialization data rather than executing a slow marketplace extension.

## Latest profiling run

I fully quit VS Code and launched it using:

`code --prof-startup `

The application was noticeably slow to become responsive, but the startup performance marks reported:

* `workbench ready`: 1531 ms
* `register extensions & spawn extension host`: 180 ms
* `extensions registered`: 1663 ms

The generated CPU profiles cover only approximately 4–5 seconds each, so they do not appear to include the complete perceived delay.

I have attached:

* Main-process CPU profile
* Renderer CPU profile
* Extension-host CPU profile
* Startup Performance reports from multiple slow runs
* macOS process samples captured during a slow run

## Expected behaviour

The window should become responsive shortly after appearing, and the performance diagnostics should account for the period during which the UI cannot be used normally.

## Actual behaviour

The window appears, but interaction can remain delayed or unresponsive for tens of seconds. Sometimes this corresponds to extension-registration timing, while other times the user-visible delay is not represented in Startup Performance or the automatically generated startup CPU profiles.

[Archive.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29087702/Archive.zip)

[2026-06-18-prof-startup.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29087713/2026-06-18-prof-startup.txt)
[2026-06-16-Sample of Code Helper.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29087714/2026-06-16-Sample.of.Code.Helper.txt)
[2026-06-16-Startup performance.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29087716/2026-06-16-Startup.performance.md)
[2026-06-16-Sample of Code Helper (Renderer).txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29087717/2026-06-16-Sample.of.Code.Helper.Renderer.txt)
[2026-06-17-startup-performance.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29087715/2026-06-17-startup-performance.md)
canonical: VS Code sometimes becomes unresponsive for tens of seconds during startup on macOS because extension host initialization or shared-process startup is intermittently blocked, even when extensions are disabled and a fresh user data directory is used.

title: Copilot CLI AH not picking up the platform-specific hooks
document:
Copilot CLI AH not picking up the platform-specific hooks

- VS Code Version: 
- OS Version: 

In the microsoft/vscode-docs repo we have [pre- and post-tool use hooks](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-docs/blob/main/.github/hooks/docs-instructions.json) that have a platform-specific overrides (use PowerShell on Windows).

When running a Copilot CLI [Agent Host] session on Windows, the PowerShell script is not being invoked and the default (bash script) is called, which doesn't work on Windows.

Using latest Insiders.

**Steps to repro**

1. Open the vscode-docs repo in Insiders on Windows
2. Start a new Copilot CLI [Agent Host] session
3. Send a prompt that invokes a tool
4. Notice that the `.sh` hook scripts are opened instead of running the PowerShell scripts
canonical: Copilot CLI Agent Host ignores platform-specific hook overrides on Windows and runs the default shell scripts instead of the intended PowerShell hooks.

title: Gemini models fail on tool calling in arrays of objects
document:
Gemini models fail on tool calling in arrays of objects

- VS Code Version: 1.125.0
- OS Version: Darwin arm64 25.5.0

Ask a gemini model to perform a task that involves calling a tool with an argument of an array of objects like the task tool, gemini will answer with a flatten tool call and the tool call will fail, the model will be looping that tool calling attempt until it decides it can't use the tool.

Example of internal call tool from the debug view

Response
canonical: Gemini tool calls fail when a tool argument is an array of objects because the model flattens the nested structure into an invalid call and then keeps retrying instead of completing the action.

title: Ctrl+Shift+I always activates GitHub Copilot tab instead of last active chat extension (no Copilot license)
document:
Ctrl+Shift+I always activates GitHub Copilot tab instead of last active chat extension (no Copilot license)

## Problem

When pressing `Ctrl+Shift+I` ("Open Chat (Agent)"), VS Code always activates the GitHub Copilot Chat tab — even when:
- the user has no GitHub Copilot license
- another chat extension (e.g. Claude Code) is installed and was previously active

After the Copilot tab opens, clicking the Claude Code tab immediately causes the Copilot tab to disappear (likely because Copilot hides itself on focus-loss when there is no valid license). This results in a jarring flash every time the shortcut is used.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Install the Claude Code VS Code extension
2. Do **not** have a GitHub Copilot subscription/license
3. Press `Ctrl+Shift+I`
4. Observe: the GitHub Copilot Chat tab opens/activates instead of Claude Code
5. Click the Claude Code tab
6. Observe: the Copilot tab immediately disappears

## Expected Behavior

`Ctrl+Shift+I` should activate the last-used chat extension, or VS Code should not default to an extension the user is not licensed for.

## Workaround Attempted

Setting `chat.disableAIFeatures: true` (suggested in #295690) disables the **entire** chat sidebar, making all AI chat extensions (including Claude Code) inaccessible. This is not a viable workaround.

## Environment

- **VS Code Version:** 1.125.0 (x64)
- **OS Version:** Windows 11 Enterprise 10.0.26200
canonical: The chat shortcut always opens the built-in Copilot chat view instead of the previously used third-party chat extension when Copilot is unavailable or unlicensed, causing Copilot to briefly appear and then disappear on focus change because it is being chosen as the default chat provider.

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: insider build from 2026-06-16
- VS Code Version: 1.126.0-insider
- OS Version: Windows 11
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agents window
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Local qwen
- Logs:

1. Open the Agents window
2. Change the type from Copilot CLI to Local
3. Ask the model what tools it has available. It will have between none and 3, notably not having any way to read files.
4. In the same conversation, ask the model a second time what tools it has available, and it will provide the full list.

title: VS Code can become stuck reopening the Agent login window and prevent access to the normal editor
document:
VS Code can become stuck reopening the Agent login window and prevent access to the normal editor

- VS Code Version: 1.124.2 (User Setup)
- OS Version: Windows 11 Version 25H2 (OS Build 26200.8655)

1. Launch VS Code while not signed in to any account.
2. Trigger the built-in "Open in Agents" feature
3. An Agent window opens and prompts for sign-in
4. Two windows are now open:
- Normal VS Code editor window
- Agent login window
6. Close the normal VS Code editor window first.
7. Close the Agent login window.
8. Launch VS Code again.

Expected Behavior:

VS Code should open the normal editor window or provide a clear way to return to the editor.

Actual Behavior:

Only the Agent login window reopens.
The normal editor window does not appear.

Additional observations:

-Opening a folder with VS Code does not restore the normal editor window.
-Ending VS Code processes does not resolve the issue.
-There is no obvious way to exit the Agent login experience and return to the editor.

Workaround:

Running:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e55cdd1f-3fc9-4953-a0d9-b9ba6e9c2d21

code --disable-extensions

allows recovery, but only when the Agent login window is already open.
Running the command while no VS Code window is open does not appear to resolve the issue.

Impact:

This can leave users effectively locked out of the normal VS Code interface and may be particularly confusing for new users who are not familiar with command-line recovery steps.

Notes:

The issue occurs with the built-in "Open in Agents" feature and is not related to a third-party extension.
canonical: Launching the editor after using the built-in agents sign-in flow can get stuck reopening the agent login window instead of restoring the normal editor, likely because the previous agent session state is being persisted and prioritized over the main window.

title: Agents: Need a way to heal broken worktree paths
document:
Agents: Need a way to heal broken worktree paths

The agents window needs a way to "heal" the scenario when the path to a worktree for an active session is changed / broken. 

To recreate:

1. Make a new workspace at `~/projects-a/workspace-1`
2. Start a new worktree-based chat session using `workspace-1`
3. Go back to new chat screen
4. Rename the parent "projects-a" folder to "projects-b"
5. Select the chat session you kicked off a minute ago
6. See error notification

Core problem: The original chat session is backed by a worktree located at `~/projects-a/workspace-1.worktrees/name-of-session/`. When we renamed projects-a to projects-b we broke that path and now agents window sees the worktree as not existing anymore. This in turn ruins your ability to keep working on that chat session.

We need a way to tell the agents window "oops, the worktree for this session is now located at `~/projects-b/workspace-1.worktrees/name-of-session/`"
canonical: Active chat sessions become unusable when their underlying worktree path changes because the agents window still points to the old location and has no way to update it.

title: Internal Stop hook forces agent to continue indefinitely ("Keep working autonomously") — no setting to disable
document:
Internal Stop hook forces agent to continue indefinitely ("Keep working autonomously") — no setting to disable

The Copilot Chat runtime has an internal Stop hook that fires when the agent tries to end its response. The runtime injects this message:

> *"Keep working autonomously until the original request is fully satisfied. DO NOT repeat or restate your previous response — pick up where you left off. If you were planning, stop planning and start implementing, and resolve any errors or remaining steps before you stop."*

This happens even when the agent has genuinely finished the task and the user is satisfied with the answer. There is no setting or workaround to control this.

- VS Code Version: 1.101.x (latest stable, June 2026)
- OS Version: Linux (WSL2)

## How I found this

I was having a research/discussion conversation with the agent (model: Xiaomi MiMo) — no code to write, no files to edit, just questions and answers. Every time the agent tried to wrap up, the hook fired and forced it to keep going. This happened repeatedly across multiple sessions, always on non-implementation conversations.

## What I tested

**First hypothesis: triggered by `task_complete` tool**

I told the agent to respond and end *without* calling `task_complete`. The hook still fired. Not about the tool call.

**Second hypothesis: about response patterns**

I asked the agent to just say one sentence and stop. The hook fired anyway. The runtime seems to detect "conclusive" language in the response text and blocks the stop regardless of what tools were or weren't called.

**Third hypothesis: a user hook I configured**

I checked everything:
- `.github/hooks/` — empty in all workspace folders
- `~/.copilot/ide/` — no hook config files, only a lock file
- `.claude/settings.json` — doesn't exist
- No workspace-level hooks of any kind

The hook is internal to the Copilot Chat runtime. No external hooks are involved.

**How many times it fires**

In a single conversation turn, the hook fired **7 times** without me sending any new messages. Each time the agent tried to stop, the runtime blocked it. The agent was forced to produce filler content just to satisfy the hook.

## Why this is a problem

1. **Burns credits for nothing** — each forced continuation is another LLM call with no value to the user
2. **Makes the agent worse** — forced to keep going, it repeats itself or produces low-quality filler
3. **Ignores the user** — the user's task was done, but the runtime overrides the agent's judgment
4. **Worse with some models** — concise models get punished because the runtime interprets brevity as "giving up early"

## What would help

A setting to control this, something like:

or even just:

The [Agent Hooks docs](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/agent-customization/hooks) already describe how `Stop` hooks can block the agent from stopping with `decision: "block"`. The runtime uses this same mechanism internally but gives users no way to configure it.
canonical: An internal stop hook in the Copilot Chat runtime is preventing the agent from ending a response and forcing it to continue indefinitely, even after the task is complete, with no user setting to disable or control this behavior.

title: Remote-WSL: GUI-triggered npm scripts and Git hooks fail with "command not found" for fnm-managed Node.js (npm/pnpm)
document:
Remote-WSL: GUI-triggered npm scripts and Git hooks fail with "command not found" for fnm-managed Node.js (npm/pnpm)

- VS Code Version: 1.124.1 / 1.124.2 / 1.125.0
- OS Version: Windows 11 25H2 (WSL 2 with Debian 13)

1. Launch VS Code on Windows 11 via desktop shortcut or Start Menu.
2. Connect to a WSL 2 Debian 13 environment using the Remote-WSL extension.
3. Open a node.js project, containing a `package.json` file with any script defined (e.g., `"test": "pnpm --version"` or `"build": "echo hello"`), but crucially the script invokes the `npm` or `pnpm` binary itself (i.e., the script content calls these executables).
4. Click the "Run" button for that script in the "NPM Scripts" view on the left sidebar (or use the "Run Script" button in the `package.json` editor).
5. Observe the output in the terminal.

**Actual Result:**
The terminal outputs `pnpm: command not found` (or `npm: command not found`) and the task fails. Example:

(If the script invokes `npm`, the error will be `npm: command not found`.)

**Expected Result:**
The command should execute successfully, just like when typed manually in VS Code's integrated terminal (e.g., manually entering `pnpm run test` or `npm run test` works fine).

**Additional Information:**

1. **Environment Setup**:
 - Node.js 24.16.0 is managed by `fnm` (v1.39.0) and initialized **only** in `~/.bashrc` via `eval "$(fnm env --use-on-cd --shell bash)"`. `fnm` dynamically sets `PATH` based on the current directory, meaning the paths to Node.js and its associated binaries are not static—they are injected at shell runtime.
 - `pnpm@10` is installed and activated via Node.js's built-in `corepack` (and `npm` is also provided by Node.js).
 - Git hooks (e.g., `pre-commit`) that rely on `pnpm` for linting/formatting also fail with the same `command not found` error.
2. **Behavior Comparison**:
 - **Broken**: Clicking the GUI "Run npm script" button or triggering a Git hook fails with `command not found` (for both `pnpm` and `npm`).
 - **Working**: Manually typing the same command (e.g., `pnpm run test` or `npm run test`) in VS Code's integrated WSL terminal works perfectly.
3. **Version Regression**: This issue **was not occurred in earlier VS Code versions**. It appears to have started with recent updates (around 1.124.x). Due to the frequent release cycle, we cannot precisely pinpoint the exact introducing version.
4. **Related Setting**:
 - `terminal.integrated.shellIntegration.enabled` is set to `true` (default).

**Root Cause Analysis:**
This issue likely stems from VS Code's GUI processes (like the NPM Scripts view or Git hook triggers) not correctly inheriting the `PATH` environment variable that is dynamically set in WSL via non‑interactive shell configuration files like `~/.bashrc`. These GUI actions may spawn a non‑interactive shell, which does not source `~/.bashrc`, thus omitting the `fnm`‑dynamically‑managed Node.js binaries (including `pnpm` and `npm`) from the `PATH`. This behavior is inconsistent with the integrated terminal, which typically sources interactive shell configurations. Notably, because `fnm` paths are dynamic, there is no simple static workaround like hardcoding paths.
canonical: GUI-triggered npm scripts and Git hooks in Remote-WSL are launched without the shell initialization needed for fnm to add Node.js binaries to PATH, so npm and pnpm are not found.

title: Copilot Chat: auto-generated title corrupted with wrong-language characters ("原因" appended)
document:
Copilot Chat: auto-generated title corrupted with wrong-language characters ("原因" appended)

ElectronBuildId: 14159160

Open Copilot Chat and start a new conversation
Ask a question (e.g., "why microsoft is down")
Observe the auto-generated chat title in the chat tab
Expected Behavior
The title should be generated in the same language as the conversation (English), e.g. "Microsoft service outage".

Actual Behavior
The title appears as "Microsoft service outage原因" — Chinese characters (原因, meaning "reason") were unexpectedly appended to an otherwise English title. The user did not rename the chat.

Frequency
Happened once; difficult to reproduce on demand (suspected data corruption / streaming glitch).

Possible Cause (Hypothesis)
The chat title is auto-generated server-side by an AI model. If the model receives partially corrupted input due to a truncated stream, broken packet, or buffer overrun during a chat response, it may emit unexpected tokens from its multilingual vocabulary — in this case, Chinese characters appended to the end of the title.

This may be related to the existing chat title persistence issues:

[#316994]
[#316393]
[#312948]

Additional Context
The vscode-copilot-chat repo has been archived. The user's VS Code display language is set to English, so this is not a locale issue.
canonical: Automatically generated Copilot chat titles can become corrupted and append stray foreign-language characters due to a title-generation or persistence glitch, likely from malformed or partially corrupted input during generation.

title: 1.126.0 Endgame
document:
1.126.0 Endgame

[Endgame guide](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog/blob/main/guidelines/endgame-guide.md)

# Before Monday 6AM Zurich time

`@team`:
- Update endgame availability 
- Test plan items created 
- Milestone only has TPIs. All other issues need to be closed
- All closed issues in milestone labeled `on-testplan`, `bug`, or `verification-needed`
- Verification steps added to `bug` and `verification-needed` issues

# Monday morning (Zurich)

`@endgame-champ`:
- [x] Assign TPIs
 - [x] Create TPI's for all verification needed bugs. Visit `Testing and Verification` on tools site, and generate groupings, then create TPIs. The TPI section below will update with all of the generated items. 
- [x] Pause automatic releases (this also ensures chat-pre-release isn't released) after 10am.
- [x] Run the [Start Endgame](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/workflows/start-endgame.yml) workflow
 
 - PRs opened with auto-merge enabled — ensure they get reviewed and merged Monday morning so Insiders picks up the next milestone version:
 - [x] Version bump (`microsoft/vscode`): https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/322339
 - [x] Endgame notebook milestone bump (`microsoft/vscode`): https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/322338
 
 - If you need to run these steps manually, see the [Branching guide](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog/blob/main/guidelines/endgame-guide.md#branching).

# Monday morning (Redmond and Zurich)

`@team`:
- [Test plan items](https://github.com/issues?q=org%3Amicrosoft%20milestone%3A%221.126.0%22%20is%3Aissue%20is%3Aopen%20label%3Atestplan-item). All TPIs must be finished by noon. This includes verification TPIs.
- Make sure your TPIs are being tested and ping folks if to confirm they will finish by noon.

# Monday afternoon (Redmond and Zurich)

`@team`:
- Fix essential issues. Default to pushing to next iteration
- Release notes 

# Monday EOD Redmond time

`@team`:
- All issues including late fixes, are verified ([query](https://github.com/issues?q=org%3Amicrosoft+milestone%3A%221.126.0%22+is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed+reason%3Acompleted++label%3Abug+-label%3Averified+-label%3Aon-testplan+-label%3A*duplicate+-label%3Aduplicate+-label%3Ainvalid+-label%3A*as-designed+-label%3Aerror-telemetry+-label%3Averification-steps-needed+-label%3Az-author-verified+-label%3Aunreleased+-label%3A*not-reproducible+-label%3A*out-of-scope+sort%3Aupdated-asc))
- Release notes written

`@endgame-champ`:
- [x] Create new builds from `release/` branch so we can finish verification. Not required to contain OSS Tool changes.
- [x] Run the [OSS Tool Workflow](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/workflows/oss-tool.yml) to update `ThirdPartyNotices.txt` for release. ([detailed docs / manual fallback](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-build-tools/tree/main/distro-tools))
 - [ ] On Failure: an agentic workflow auto-dispatches to fix missing licenses. PR link is in the `safe_outputs summary`

 - [ ] Trigger the OSS Tool Workflow again.
