title: Hide "Run and Debug" panel title when Activity Bar width is narrow
document:
Hide "Run and Debug" panel title when Activity Bar width is narrow

# Bug: "Start Debugging" dropdown menu is inaccessible or cut off when Activity Bar is narrow

## Issue Type
Bug / UI Glitch

## Description
When the VS Code window is resized to be narrower, or when the Side Bar width is reduced, the dropdown menu triggered by the arrow next to the "Start Debugging" (Green Play) button in the "Run and Debug" view becomes unusable.

Specifically, the dropdown content either:
1. Does not appear at all when clicked.
2. Is cut off by the window edge or the sidebar boundary.
3. Is visually obscured by other UI elements due to lack of horizontal space.

This makes it impossible to switch between debug configurations (launch.json profiles) without resizing the window to be wider first.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open the "Run and Debug" view (`Ctrl+Shift+D`).
2. Ensure you have multiple debug configurations in your `launch.json` so the dropdown arrow is active.
3. Resize the VS Code window to make it very narrow, or drag the sidebar divider to make the Run and Debug panel very thin.
4. Click the small arrow (dropdown trigger) next to the Green Play button.
5. Observe that the list of configurations fails to render correctly or is inaccessible.

## Expected Behavior
The dropdown menu should adapt to the available space (e.g., anchor to the left side of the button instead of the right, or float above the UI) so that users can still select a configuration even in a compact layout. Alternatively, the UI should hide the text label "RUN AND DEBUG" earlier to preserve space for the controls.

## System Info
- VS Code version: Code 1.123.2 (3c631b164c239e7aeaaae7c626b46c527b361af2, 2026-06-09T15:06:40-07:00)
- OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
canonical: The Run and Debug dropdown becomes inaccessible when the sidebar or window is too narrow because the panel does not adapt its layout or reposition the menu to fit the available space.

title: False CSS diagnostics for Laravel Blade directives (@if, @else, @endif) inside <style> blocks
document:
False CSS diagnostics for Laravel Blade directives (@if, @else, @endif) inside <style> blocks

## Description

### Environment

* VS Code: 1.123.2 (user setup)
* OS: Windows 11
* File type: `.blade.php`
* Laravel Blade template

### Problem

When Laravel Blade directives are used inside a `` block, VS Code's CSS parser reports false diagnostics such as:

* `Unknown at rule @if`
* `Unknown at rule @else`
* `Unknown at rule @endif`

These diagnostics appear even when:

* `CSS: Lint Unknown At Rules` is set to `ignore`
* `css.validate` is disabled
* Blade files are correctly associated with Laravel Blade syntax

### Reproduction

Example Blade file:

### Actual Result

VS Code reports:

### Screenshots

Settings for fixing but not work:

### Expected Result

Laravel Blade directives inside `.blade.php` files should not generate CSS diagnostics, or CSS validation should recognize and ignore Blade directives embedded within `` blocks.

### Additional Notes

This appears to be a false positive caused by the CSS language service parsing Blade directives as CSS at-rules. The issue is especially noticeable in Laravel projects where conditional styles are commonly generated using Blade directives.
canonical: VS Code’s CSS language service incorrectly treats Laravel Blade directives inside style blocks as CSS at-rules, causing false unknown-at-rule diagnostics even when CSS validation is disabled.

title: Voice Mode: Follow-up improvements from PR review
document:
Voice Mode: Follow-up improvements from PR review

Follow-up items deferred from #320785 based on review feedback from @dmitrivMS.

## 1. Replace polling with observables/events in `agentsVoiceWindowService`
**Comment:** [agentsVoiceWindowService.ts:275](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/320785#discussion_r3399360305)

The session polling (`setInterval` every 3s to call `agentSessionsService.model.resolve()`) is a workaround. We should subscribe to proper events/observables for session state changes instead of polling.

## 2. Make BroadcastChannel window-scoped
**Comments:** [agentsVoiceWidget.ts:176](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/320785#discussion_r3399367501), [chatViewPane.ts:398](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/320785#discussion_r3399377032)

The `BroadcastChannel` used for communication between the voice controller and the chat widget is global. If two chat panes or windows race on commands like `getCurrentSession`, the last one wins. This should be scoped per-window to prevent conflicts.

cc @meganrogge
canonical: The voice chat implementation relies on polling for session updates and uses a globally shared broadcast channel, causing stale state handling and cross-window command conflicts when multiple chat panes or windows are open.

title: Copilot Chat does not respond and fails to retrieve token (ERR_CONNECTION_TIMED_OUT) from Brazil.
document:
Copilot Chat does not respond and fails to retrieve token (ERR_CONNECTION_TIMED_OUT) from Brazil.

# Copilot Chat does not respond and fails to retrieve token (ERR_CONNECTION_TIMED_OUT) from Brazil

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: Latest available version (June 2026)
- VS Code Version: 1.124.0
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Ask mode (also affects other chat modes)
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Any model (GPT 4.1, Claude Sonnet, etc.)
- Logs:

## Description

GitHub Copilot Chat is not responding when sending prompts. After selecting any model and submitting a question, nothing happens in the chat interface.

Authentication appears to succeed, but the extension fails to retrieve the Copilot token due to repeated connection timeouts.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code.
2. Sign in to GitHub Copilot.
3. Open Copilot Chat.
4. Select any available model.
5. Send a prompt in Ask mode.
6. Observe that no response is generated.

## Expected Behavior

The selected model should process the prompt and return a response.

## Actual Behavior

No response is returned in the chat. Logs show repeated timeout errors while attempting to retrieve the Copilot token.

## Additional Context

- Location: Brazil
- Authentication succeeds (`Logged in as hugo-autotrac`)
- The issue occurs regardless of the selected model.
- Multiple timeout errors occur while connecting to Copilot services.
- The logs repeatedly reference `4.228.31.149:443`.
- This may indicate a regional networking, routing, CDN, or service availability issue affecting Copilot users in Brazil.
canonical: Copilot Chat fails to answer because it cannot reliably reach Copilot services to fetch the authentication token, causing repeated network timeouts that prevent any prompt from being processed.

title: Feature Request: Add `TurnEnd` / `ResponseComplete` hook lifecycle event
document:
Feature Request: Add `TurnEnd` / `ResponseComplete` hook lifecycle event

## Problem
The current agent hook lifecycle events don't include one that fires when the agent finishes responding to a user prompt (i.e., when a "turn" is complete). This is the most natural point to trigger user notifications — especially audio cues, desktop notifications, or status updates — since it's when the user needs to re-engage.

## Current workarounds
- `PostToolUse` fires too frequently (every tool call, not just the final one)
- `Stop` only fires when the entire session ends
- `SubagentStop` only covers delegated work, not direct responses

## Proposed solution
Add a new hook event — e.g., `TurnEnd`, `ResponseComplete`, or `AssistantResponseEnd` — that fires once after the agent has finished generating its response and is waiting for user input.

### Use cases
- Play a notification sound when Copilot finishes a long task
- Send a desktop notification ("Copilot is ready for your review")
- Log turn durations for productivity tracking
- Trigger external integrations (update a dashboard, post to Slack, etc.)

## Environment
- VS Code with GitHub Copilot Chat (agent mode)
- Hook system as documented in the Preview agent hooks feature
canonical: The hook system is missing a lifecycle event that fires once when an assistant turn has finished and the agent is waiting for the next user input, forcing users to rely on less precise hooks instead.

title: Agents Window Tabs: Editor Tab settings no longer work
document:
Agents Window Tabs: Editor Tab settings no longer work

In VS Code, we have around 15 settings that control how editor tabs are rendered. Many of these settings no longer work in the Agents Window. As a result, users who have customized their tab settings may encounter broken tab layouts or behavior that differs from their expectations when they switch to the Agents Window.

Please review these settings and decide which ones should be supported in the Agents Window and which ones should not. For settings that are not supported, it is possible to force a specific value and make the setting read-only within the Agents Window.

Keep in mind that the fewer tab-related settings are supported, the less familiar the Agents Window will feel to users who have customized their VS Code Editor experience.

I tested some of the settings, these are broken:
- `workbench.editor.wrapTabs`: Tabs do not wrap when enabled
- `workbench.editor.showTabs`: The tab bar height is changing when switching between `multiple` and `single`. It should not move, the entire editor layout relies on the tab bar height being `35px` (without compact mode enabled)
- `workbench.editor.pinnedTabSizing` & `workbench.editor.tabSizing`: When set to `shrink` or `fixed` a weird shadow renders on the right hand side of the tab
- `window.density.editorTabHeight`: When set to `compact` the tab height does not change as expected, but the editor layout breaks because it's not expecting that it's getting more space even though it does not.
- `workbench.editor.titleScrollbarVisibility`: the tab bar no longer scrolls so this settings no longer makes sense and should be made read-only

There are more settings which are relevant which I have not tested, check in the settings editor under `workbench tab`
canonical: Several editor tab settings are not being respected in the Agents Window, causing tab layout and sizing behavior to differ from the normal editor because the window does not properly support the full tab configuration.

title: Terminal: WebGL renderer corrupts glyph-atlas cells (solid black boxes) on ANGLE GLES backend (Mesa radeonsi); Vulkan & software backends fine
document:
Terminal: WebGL renderer corrupts glyph-atlas cells (solid black boxes) on ANGLE GLES backend (Mesa radeonsi); Vulkan & software backends fine

With the WebGL terminal renderer (`terminal.integrated.gpuAcceleration` = `auto`/`on`), individual glyph cells render as **solid black rectangles** instead of the glyph. Affected cells are scattered and **independent of foreground color and font weight** — bold, non-bold, plain, and colored cells are all hit, and the same letter can box out in one weight while rendering fine in another. The boxes are solid/filled (not hollow tofu), consistent with corruption of individual cells in the renderer's glyph **texture atlas**. Most visible in glyph-dense full-screen TUIs like `fzf`.

### Steps to reproduce
1. `"terminal.integrated.gpuAcceleration": "auto"` (or `"on"`).
2. In the integrated terminal, run a glyph-dense fuzzy finder:

3. Type to filter and scroll the list (forces glyphs into the atlas and triggers rapid redraws).
4. Scattered glyph cells render as solid black boxes. (Screenshots attached.)

### Bisection
| Configuration | Result |
| --- | --- |
| `gpuAcceleration: off` (DOM renderer) | correct |
| External terminal (Ghostty), same font/machine | correct |
| `--use-angle=swiftshader` (ANGLE software) | correct |
| `--use-angle=vulkan --enable-features=Vulkan` (ANGLE Vulkan) | correct |
| Default ANGLE **GLES** backend (the `*ACTIVE*` one in the attached GPU info) | **black boxes** |

Identical xterm.js output renders correctly under the DOM, SwiftShader, and Vulkan backends and in an external terminal, so xterm.js is not implicated — the defect is in the **ANGLE OpenGL ES** path (ANGLE GLES translation or the Mesa radeonsi GLES driver).

### Workarounds
- `"terminal.integrated.gpuAcceleration": "off"` (terminal-only DOM renderer).
- Launch with `--use-angle=vulkan --enable-features=Vulkan` (keeps the hardware WebGL terminal, off the GLES backend).

---

*Drafted with assistance from Claude (Anthropic). The bisection and environment details above were run and verified by me on the affected machine.*

---
canonical: The WebGL terminal renderer corrupts glyph atlas texture cells on the ANGLE OpenGL ES backend, causing scattered glyphs to appear as solid black boxes while software and Vulkan backends render correctly.

title: Autoupdate doesn't work reliably on corporate managed macos
document:
Autoupdate doesn't work reliably on corporate managed macos

- VS Code Version: I just updated to the latest version. I don't recall what version I was using before.
- OS Version: macos Tahoe 26.5

1. settings->check for updates proceed with update process
2. 
3. settings->install now

if I'm lucky the management software doesn't kick in fast enough and the update helper is not removed allowing for a successful update. It takes about 3 attempts to update. Firefox, Brave, Chrome, iTerm2 all seem to autoupdate fine and don't install update helpers. Perhaps draw insights from their update strategy for macos for an easier experience. I prefer Firefox's strategy, prepare the update for next restart of application.

Perhaps the bug is: If vscode is being updated at the moment, provide a message "please wait" message if I attempt to open it in the mean time, and periodically check if the PID doing the update is still alive.

I won't be able to offer much help in investigating this issue, so feel free to close if this is not helpful enough to do anything on your end.
canonical: On managed macOS systems, the app’s update process is unreliable because the temporary update helper is sometimes removed by system management software before the update completes.

title: 1.124.1 Endgame
document:
1.124.1 Endgame

- [x] Create a milestone `1.124.1` **@dileepyavan**
- [x] Bump the version number & run `npm i` to bump `package-lock.json` as well. Ensure there are no other `package-lock.json` changes. **@dileepyavan**
- [x] [🔖candidate issues](https://github.com/issues?q=org%3Amicrosoft%20is%3Aissue%20milestone%3A%221.124.1%22%20label%3Acandidate)
- [x] Assign candidate issues to the recovery milestone **team**
- [x] Review the `candidate` issues, and if they pass the review assign them to the recovery milestone **team**
- [ ] All `candidate` fixes are peer reviewed and pushed to `main` and then cherry-picked into the release branch **team**
- [ ] Initiate `insiders` build from `main`
- [ ] Issues are tested in the `insiders` **team**
- [ ] Build and manually release Insider from main branch for vscode.dev (if there were vscode.dev candidates)
 - [ ] Issues are tested in the `insiders` **team**
- [x] Build `stable` for all platforms from release branch **@dileepyavan**
- [x] Issues are verified on `stable` build and the `verified` label is added **@dileepyavan**
- [x] Check `https://github.com/Microsoft/vscode/compare/release/` to ensure no other commits have been made in the release branch **@dileepyavan**
- [ ] The `insiders` build with all candidate fixes needs to be in the wild for 24 hours before recovery release, unless explicitly approved by @kieferrm
- [ ] Update the release notes and include a link to a query for the fixed issues @ntrogh
- [ ] Publish website @ntrogh
- [ ] Publish stable build **@dileepyavan**
- [ ] (if there were vscode.dev candidates) Trigger the [vscode.dev Deploy pipeline](https://dev.azure.com/monacotools/Monaco/_build?definitionId=276) for `release/x.y` for the `prod` deployment target. **@dileepyavan**
 - [ ] Request approval from another team member at the necessary step to deploy the vscode.dev build. **@dileepyavan**
- [ ] [Publish @vscode/chat-lib](https://dev.azure.com/monacotools/Monaco/_build?definitionId=683) (Branch `release/x.y`, Release Version `auto`, needs peer approval) **@dileepyavan**
- [ ] Create an official release **@dileepyavan**
 - [ ] Create a tag (make sure you pull the release branch first): `git tag release/`
 - [ ] Push the tag: `git push origin `
 - [ ] Create a GitHub release: [Open the GitHub tags](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/tags), and click far right `... > Create Release`. Use the correct title and description from our release notes. [Example](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/releases/tag/1.81.1)
- [ ] Close the milestone on [GitHub](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/milestones) **@dileepyavan**

title: Agents Window: Custom agents disappear from dropdown after sending a message
document:
Agents Window: Custom agents disappear from dropdown after sending a message

## Bug Description

Custom agents defined in `.github/agents/*.agent.md` appear in the Agents window dropdown when starting a new session, but **disappear immediately after pressing Enter** to send the first prompt. The dropdown reverts to the default agents (Copilot, Claude) while the session is running.

The same custom agents work perfectly in the **Chat view** (`@agent` mentions) — this issue is specific to the Agents window.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code with a workspace containing `.github/agents/*.agent.md` files
2. Open the Agents window (`Chat: Open Agents Window` or `code --agents`)
3. Start a new session and select the workspace folder
4. Observe: custom agents appear in the agent dropdown ✅
5. Select a custom agent from the dropdown
6. Type a prompt and press Enter
7. Observe: the agent dropdown resets to default agents — all custom agents disappear ❌

## Expected Behavior

Custom agents should remain available in the dropdown throughout the session. The selected custom agent should persist and be used for the session.

## Actual Behavior

After pressing Enter to send a message, the agent dropdown resets to the default agent list (Copilot, Claude, etc.). The custom workspace agents are no longer available in the dropdown for the running session.

## Environment

- **VS Code version**: 1.124.0
- **OS**: macOS 26.5 (arm64, Apple Silicon)
- **Agent files**: 12 custom agents in `.github/agents/` (all with valid YAML frontmatter)
- **Workspace**: Multi-repo workspace with sentinel file at root

## Agent Configuration Details

All 12 agent files have valid YAML frontmatter with `description`, `tools`, and `model` fields. File sizes range from 2.2KB to 50KB. The largest has a `tools` field with ~80 explicit tool references (3,385 bytes on that single YAML line).

Example frontmatter (working in Chat view):

## Potentially Related

- #315144 — Reports that opening a new session resets agent/model/MCP selections in a running session. This issue is different: the reset happens **within the same session** after sending the first message.

## Additional Context

- Agents work flawlessly in the Chat view (sidebar) via `@agent` mentions
- The agents appear correctly in the dropdown *before* sending — they are being discovered and parsed fine
- The issue seems to be that session initialization (on message send) clobbers the customization/agent state
- Tested with 12 agents ranging from simple (4 tools) to complex (80 tools) — all disappear simultaneously
canonical: The Agents window loses custom workspace agents after the first message because starting the chat session resets the available agent list to the default built-in agents instead of preserving the workspace-discovered agents.

title: [Feature]Stable installation path required: commit ID breaks third-party integrations
document:
[Feature]Stable installation path required: commit ID breaks third-party integrations

Third-party applications (like SumatraPDF) sometimes need to reference core files of VS Code (e.g., `cli.js`) to enable features like inverse search. Currently, the path to this file includes the commit ID (e.g., `.../Microsoft VS Code/1b50d58d73/resources/app/out/cli.js`). This internal identifier changes with every update, breaking these third-party integrations. End users should not be exposed to or need to understand these internal IDs.
canonical: The installation path for core application files changes with each update because it includes an internal commit identifier, which breaks third-party integrations that rely on a stable file location.

title: [Bug] Two cursors appear simultaneously in editor while typing in Agent Mode
document:
[Bug] Two cursors appear simultaneously in editor while typing in Agent Mode

## Bug Report

**Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?:** Yes

**VS Code Version:** 1.124.0 (user setup)
**OS Version:** Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 (Windows 11)

---

## Description

When typing in the editor while in **Agent Mode** (GitHub Copilot), two cursors appear simultaneously without any intentional multi-cursor action. This happens across all file types. The second cursor appears to be a ghost/phantom cursor and persists as long as I am actively typing.

This is reproducible consistently and is not triggered by any known multi-cursor shortcut (`Alt+Click`, `Ctrl+Alt+Up/Down`, etc.).

---

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code on Windows 11
2. Switch to **Agent Mode** 
3. Click into the editor and begin typing
4. Observe two cursors appearing in the editor simultaneously

---

## Expected Behavior

Only a single cursor should appear in the editor while typing, unless multi-cursor mode is explicitly triggered by the user.

---

## Actual Behavior

Two cursors are visible in the editor at the same time during normal typing in Agent Mode. No multi-cursor shortcut was used.

---

## Video ( Screen Recording )

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/919f3ede-8e3c-496e-8f3b-558b7cc63781

---

## Version Info

**VS Code Version:** 1.124.0 (user setup)
**OS Version:** Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 (Windows 11)
canonical: Typing in Agent Mode causes a duplicate ghost cursor to appear in the editor, likely because the agent input integration is incorrectly creating an extra caret while text is being entered.

title: Enable renaming of chat sessions in editor mode
document:
Enable renaming of chat sessions in editor mode

Currently, chat session renaming is available in the Agents window and in VSCode, but not when chat is opened in editor mode. Users expect the ability to rename chat sessions directly in the editor context as well, similar to other windows. This feature request is to support renaming chat sessions in editor mode via UI or command, to bring parity with other chat contexts and improve usability for users who prefer the editor layout.

[View original Slack conversation](https://vscodeteam.slack.com/archives/C05BHT75R6G/p1781040748665449?thread_ts=1781040748.665449&cid=C05BHT75R6G)
canonical: Chat sessions can be renamed in other chat contexts but not when opened in editor mode because that mode lacks the rename action or command.

title: Modernization: Add rounded corners + horizontal marign to all menu/list items

Modernization: Add rounded corners + horizontal marign to all menu/list items

Proposal: For awhile, we've been starting to add list/menu UI with rounded corners and horizontal margin across _both_ Agents window and VS Code workbench. I propose we make this change everywhere.

- [x] Command palette list items
- [x] Context menus
- [ ] Tree views
- [ ] Diff file lists
- [ ] Vertical terminal tabs
- [ ] Search result lists (i.e. tree views)
- [ ] Extension lists
- [ ] Generic lists (i.e. Profiles modal left sidebar or Chat Debug list)
- [ ] Collapsible views?

Examples of the direction we'd be aiming for:

title: Reduce Reliance on Arrow Keys for Code Navigation
document:
Reduce Reliance on Arrow Keys for Code Navigation

Description
I often find the placement of arrow keys on laptops quite uncomfortable, especially while coding. A typical scenario is when I’m typing a variable name and VS Code auto-completes it with a semicolon. Everything looks fine until I realise I need to move to the next line to continue typing.
At that point, I have to lift my hand, locate the awkwardly positioned arrow keys, and use them to navigate—only to repeat this process again and again. It feels like an unnecessary interruption to the typing flow and overall productivity.
I believe this is a common annoyance, and I’d be interested to know if others have experienced the same or found better solutions.
One workaround I’ve tried is using Ctrl + Enter, which inserts a new line and moves the cursor to it. However, this approach is not very effective when there is existing code on the next line that needs to be edited, so it’s not suitable in many situations.
I also attempted remapping keys on my laptop, but the alternative keys available for remapping are themselves not ergonomically placed, so this didn’t help much.
I’m reaching out to the VS Code team and the community for suggestions or best practices to address this issue. Ideally, I’d like to avoid purchasing and carrying an external keyboard, especially given my hybrid work setup.

If nothing already exist to tackle this issue then could you look at this as a feature request?
canonical: Code navigation in the editor is too dependent on arrow keys, making it awkward to move the cursor and continue editing efficiently on laptops.

title: Wrong theme colors
document:
Wrong theme colors

I use the Everforest Pro theme extension (https://github.com/AndreiLucaci/everforest-vscode). On Github, I can see which color codes are set for which color names. If I use a color picking tool to see what color is actually being applied in VSCode, it shows the wrong code. The color is very similar but it's not the one defined in the extension's code. I tried it with the One Monokai theme (https://github.com/azemoh/vscode-one-monokai) as well and same thing.

1. Set theme
2. Pick color (e.g. the editor background color)
3. Search for the color code in the extension's Github repo
4. The search returns no results, because the color code is slightly different.

title: Copilot Chat fails to authenticate – network timeout in VS Code
document:
Copilot Chat fails to authenticate – network timeout in VS Code

**Steps to Reproduce**
1. Open VS Code (latest Stable on Windows). 
2. Try to sign in with GitHub for Copilot Chat. 
3. Authentication flow starts, token exchange succeeds. 
4. When fetching user info, connection times out.

**Expected Behavior** 
Successful login and Copilot Chat session.

**Actual Behavior** 
VS Code shows connection error. Logs indicate `net::ERR_CONNECTION_TIMED_OUT` when calling `https://api.github.com/user`. Curl requests to the same endpoint succeed outside VS Code.

**Troubleshooting Already Tried**
- Reinstalled VS Code (Stable build). 
- Deleted `%APPDATA%\Code` including `globalStorage` and `workspaceStorage`. 
- Cleared Copilot extension cache. 
- Verified GitHub Copilot subscription is active. 
- Checked firewall: Code.exe allowed for both Private and Public networks. 
- Tested with extensions disabled (`code --disable-extensions`). 
- Still fails with timeout. 

**Logs (excerpt)** 

**Environment** 
- Windows 11 
- VS Code Stable (latest build) 
- Extension: Built-in `github.copilot-chat` 
- Copilot subscription active 
- Network: firewall allows Code.exe, curl requests succeed, but VS Code fails
canonical: VS Code’s GitHub sign-in for Copilot Chat is failing because its request to fetch the signed-in user from GitHub times out even though the initial token exchange succeeds, likely due to a network or proxy issue affecting the editor’s HTTP traffic.

title: Inline suggestions (Copilot ghost text) block IntelliSense and Emmet, breaking native completion flow
document:
Inline suggestions (Copilot ghost text) block IntelliSense and Emmet, breaking native completion flow

### Summary

Inline suggestions / ghost text (e.g., Copilot) are interfering with VS Code's native suggestion system (IntelliSense/Emmet) in a highly disruptive way. When ghost text appears, the suggest widget often no longer shows up. Even triggering `Trigger Suggest` manually does not reliably bring back the suggestion list, making native completions unusable. Copilot effectively takes over the interaction and suppresses the deterministic tools that users want to use.

### Problem

I rely on native completion behaviors:
- IntelliSense symbol/member suggestions
- Emmet abbreviation expansion
- Quick access to exact object/method names via fuzzy completion

Expected workflow:
1. Type a short prefix/abbreviation
2. Native suggest widget pops up
3. I select the symbol/snippet I want quickly

**After recent updates, if Copilot/inline suggestions show ghost text first:**
- Native suggestions often do not appear
- Emmet completions may not show up
- Even manual `Trigger Suggest` doesn't restore the suggest widget reliably
- The ghost text usually does not match my intent

**Result:**
Copilot/inline suggestions are “grabbing the steering wheel” from IntelliSense/Emmet, and are usually steering in the wrong direction, making the whole experience much worse for power users and precision-driven workflows.

### Why is this a problem?
- Predictable, deterministic completion is essential for many developers
- AI suggestions should not actively block or degrade core editor features
- The regression makes it much harder to use well-understood APIs, frameworks, or Emmet abbreviations
- Suggest widget should always be recoverable

### Expected behavior
- Inline suggestions (ghost text) should NOT suppress IntelliSense or Emmet
- At minimum: `Trigger Suggest` must always show the suggestion widget, no matter what state inline suggestions are in
- Ideally: A separate setting to prefer native completion over ghost text, or to independently enable/disable these two interactions

### Actual behavior
- When ghost text is showing, native suggestions/Emmet may not show
- Even after triggering suggestions, widget may not appear
- Editing experience becomes much worse than without ghost text

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open a file where IntelliSense/Emmet suggestions are normally available (e.g. .vue, .js, .ts, .css)
2. Start typing a prefix or abbreviation
3. Wait for Copilot/ghost text to appear
4. Notice that native suggest widget doesn't show
5. Attempt to manually trigger the suggest list (`Trigger Suggest` command)
6. Observe it still doesn't appear as expected

### Request
Please prioritize native completion (IntelliSense/Emmet/suggest widget) above inline ghost text, or provide an option for users to select their preferred priority. Right now, Copilot/inline suggestions feel intrusive, not assistive.

---

#### Some environment info
- VS Code Version: 1.123.2 (Universal)
- OS: macOS 14.6.1 
- Copilot or related extensions: Yes
- Vue/Emmet/TypeScript plugins: Yes
- Sample relevant settings:
 - `editor.inlineSuggest.enabled`
 - `github.copilot.enable`
 - `emmet.triggerExpansionOnTab`

我遇到的问题不是“不想要 AI”，而是 Copilot 的 ghost text 当前会严重干扰 IntelliSense/Emmet 原生补全：我本来能很快通过简写找到想要对象，但 ghost text 介入会直接压制掉建议弹窗，甚至手动唤出弹窗都不行，体验极差。希望至少能让 Trigger Suggest 始终优先，ghost text 和原生补全能够分离。
canonical: Inline suggestion ghost text is incorrectly suppressing or blocking the native suggestion widget and Emmet completion, likely because inline completions are taking priority over trigger-suggest behavior instead of coexisting with it.

Currently, VS Code prompts users to restart after updates, but there is no easy, always-available way to manually trigger a full restart when needed. title: Extensions gallery: support ~/.netrc credentials for custom serviceUrl (enterprise auth)
document:
Extensions gallery: support ~/.netrc credentials for custom serviceUrl (enterprise auth)

**Problem**

Enterprises that need to control which VS Code extensions developers can install currently have one supported path: [Private Marketplace](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/editor/private-marketplace). This requires standing up and maintaining Microsoft's dedicated Private Marketplace service, which is not ideal for organizations that already operate in-house software approval pipelines (dependency registries, allowlist services, artifact proxies).

The underlying mechanism VS Code exposes `extensionsGallery.serviceUrl` in `product.json` is sufficient to point VS Code at a custom registry that filters extensions. A lightweight proxy in front of the VS Marketplace can strip blocked extensions from `extensionquery` responses before VS Code ever sees them, giving enterprises full control without adopting a separate product.

The blocker today is authentication: VS Code provides no way to supply credentials to a custom `serviceUrl`. Credentials embedded in the URL itself crash VS Code at startup (see https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/320930). There is no setting to inject an `Authorization` header. This means any custom registry must be unauthenticated and exposed to the network without access control.

**Proposed solution**

Add a new Application scoped boolean setting `extensions.gallery.useNetrcAuth` (default `false`). When enabled, VS Code reads `~/.netrc` for the gallery hostname on every gallery request and injects a `Basic` `Authorization` header.

`~/.netrc` is already a standard credential store on developer machines, used by npm, pip, Go module proxy, and most package managers. Enterprise tooling that manages `~/.netrc` entries (credential helpers, token rotation daemons) works out of the box with no changes.

This is additive and fully compatible with Private Marketplace: users on Private Marketplace are unaffected (setting defaults to `false`). It opens a path for organizations that prefer to operate their own extension filtering service instead.

**Security considerations**

- Opt-in, default `false`
- APPLICATION scope prevents workspace-level override
- `~/.netrc` is already a trusted, user-owned credential store
- VS Code only reads credentials from it; it never writes to it
- `default` entries in `.netrc` are explicitly not matched to avoid silently injecting credentials into unintended hosts
canonical: VS Code does not support using credentials from a user’s netrc file to authenticate requests to a custom extension gallery service URL, forcing enterprise registries to be exposed without access control or use unsupported embedded credentials.

title: Extensions gallery: startup crash "Invalid port" when serviceUrl contains userinfo
document:
Extensions gallery: startup crash "Invalid port" when serviceUrl contains userinfo

**Steps to Reproduce**
1. Edit `product.json` (e.g. `/Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/product.json`) and set `extensionsGallery.serviceUrl` to a URL containing userinfo: `https://user:pass@registry.example.com/vsix`
2. Launch VS Code

**Expected:** VS Code starts normally.

**Actual:** VS Code crashes at startup with:

TypeError: Invalid url pattern https://user:pass@registry.example.com/vsix/* Invalid port

**Root cause:** `windowImpl.ts` builds the Electron URL pattern for `webRequest.onBeforeSendHeaders` using `URI.parse(serviceUrl).authority`. `URI.parse().authority` follows RFC 3986 and
 includes the userinfo component (`user:pass@host:port`) which Electron's URL pattern validator misparses as a malformed port value.

**Fix**: use WHATWG `new URL(serviceUrl).host` which returns `hostname:port` without userinfo.

Extensions disabled
canonical: The app crashes at startup when the extensions gallery service URL contains user credentials because the URL pattern builder incorrectly uses the full authority including userinfo, which Electron parses as an invalid port.

title: Webview resource cache grows unboundedly: media-preview cache-busts with Date.now(), service worker never evicts (21+ GB observed)
document:
Webview resource cache grows unboundedly: media-preview cache-busts with Date.now(), service worker never evicts (21+ GB observed)

(media-preview and the webview service worker are built-in, so `--disable-extensions` does not affect the leak.)

- VS Code Version: 1.123.2 (commit 3c631b164c239e7aeaaae7c626b46c527b361af2, x64)
- OS Version: Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200 (also observed under WSL Remote – Ubuntu)

## Summary

`%APPDATA%\Code\WebStorage\\CacheStorage` grew to **23.5 GB** on my machine.
I parsed the Chromium simple-cache entry keys to find out why. The root cause is a
combination of two pieces of built-in code:

1. **media-preview cache-busts with the current time, not the file version.**
 `extensions/media-preview/src/imagePreview/index.ts` (`getResourcePath`) appends
 `?version=${Date.now()}` on **every render**. Every time an image is opened or
 re-rendered, the URL is brand new — even if the file is unchanged.

2. **The webview service worker caches every response and never evicts.**
 `src/vs/workbench/contrib/webview/browser/pre/service-worker.js` does
 `cache.put(event.request, ...)` for every 200 response with an ETag, keyed by the
 full URL including the query string. The file contains no eviction, expiry, or
 quota logic. The `activate` handler doesn't even delete caches from previous
 `VERSION`s — my disk holds both `vscode-resource-cache-4` (2.09 GB) and
 `vscode-resource-cache-5` (21.38 GB).

Combined effect: **every view of an image permanently writes a full copy to disk**,
and each copy is dead on arrival — the `Date.now()` query string guarantees no future
request can ever hit it.

## Evidence (from parsing the cache entry keys)

My 21.38 GB cache origin contains 6,413 entries:

- They map to only **1,947 unique files** (1,013 files cached ≥ 2 times).
- The most-viewed image was cached **86 times within a single hour** (one evening,
 up to 9 copies in one minute) — per-render, not per-modification.
- In a sample of the 200 most-duplicated paths, **81% point to files that no longer
 exist** on disk.
- Zero video/audio entries: `` uses Range requests (206), which the SW marks
 `no-store` and the Cache API cannot store anyway. The leak therefore only bites
 image-heavy workflows and grows slowly enough to evade detection — which is
 presumably why earlier reports (#131226, #166632 [145 GB], #152519 [500 GB]) died
 as "cannot reproduce" / stale.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Note the size of `%APPDATA%\Code\WebStorage` (`du -sh ~/.config/Code/WebStorage` on Linux).
2. Launch `code --disable-extensions` and open a large PNG (e.g. 20 MB) in the
 built-in image preview.
3. Close the tab and reopen the same file 10 times.
4. The folder grows by ~10 × the image size. None of the new entries can ever be
 read back (each was stored under a unique `?version=` URL), and they
 are never cleaned up.

## Expected

Either of:
- media-preview keys the cache-busting parameter on the file's mtime instead of
 `Date.now()`, so unchanged files reuse one cache entry; and/or
- the service worker evicts stale versions of the same base URL when caching a new
 one, applies an LRU/size cap to `vscode-resource-cache-*`, and deletes
 previous-`VERSION` caches on activate.

## Related

#131226 (closed as stale, "needs repro"), #166632 (145 GB, closed as duplicate),
#152519 (500 GB, closed as duplicate), #310384 (open, 30 GB via Remote-SSH — same
mechanism; this report adds the root-cause analysis).
canonical: The built-in image preview appends a unique time-based query to every render and the webview service worker caches each response without eviction, causing the cache to grow unboundedly with duplicate stale copies of the same file.

title: Bring back update.titleBar: "none" - Title‑bar update button causes accidental restarts that kill active sessions
document:
Bring back update.titleBar: "none" - Title‑bar update button causes accidental restarts that kill active sessions

### Summary

Please restore a setting (previously `"update.titleBar": "none"`) that hides the
green **Update** button from the title bar while keeping the subtle update badge on
the Manage (gear) icon in the Activity Bar.

This setting used to work but is no longer recognized in recent versions
(see #308207). With it gone, there is no supported way to hide the title‑bar
update button.

### The problem

The title‑bar **Update** button renders immediately to the right of the active
file name. Visually it sits in the same place a per‑file action would, so it is
very easy to read it as "update/apply this file" rather than "update VS Code".

The consequence of a single mis‑click is severe and immediate: clicking it
restarts VS Code, which **terminates whatever in‑progress session you had** — an
active debugging session, a long‑running task, or an AI coding session (e.g. an
agent mid‑task). There is no confirmation step, so one accidental click silently
destroys work that can take a long time to rebuild context for.

Because the button:
1. is placed next to the filename (ambiguous about *what* is being updated),
2. is a prominent, colored call‑to‑action, and
3. triggers an immediate restart with no confirmation,

…it combines high click‑likelihood with a high‑cost, irreversible outcome.

### Proposed solution

Reintroduce a setting to control the title‑bar update entry, e.g.:

- `"update.titleBar": "none"` — hide the title‑bar button, keep the badge on the
 Manage/gear icon (the preferred, low‑intrusion indicator).
- `"update.titleBar": "default"` — current behavior.

### Alternatives considered

- `"update.mode": "manual"` / `"none"` removes the button, but it also disables
 background update checks and removes the badge, so you lose any indication that
 an update is available. There is currently no way to keep the badge while
 hiding only the title‑bar button.
- A confirmation prompt before restarting would also reduce accidental
 session loss, but simply restoring `update.titleBar` is the smaller, proven fix.

### Related

- #308207 — `update.titleBar` setting no longer recognized and doesn't work
- #311922 — Update button should be hidden from the title bar in Zen Mode
canonical: The update button in the title bar is easy to click by mistake and immediately restarts the editor without confirmation, which can abruptly terminate active work sessions, and the removed setting to hide that button is the missing control behind the problem.

title: GitHub Copilot: Semantic index finalize fails with 412 for non-GitHub repository even though external ingest is allowed by organization policy
document:
GitHub Copilot: Semantic index finalize fails with 412 for non-GitHub repository even though external ingest is allowed by organization policy

## Description

GitHub Copilot fails to build the codebase semantic index in VS Code for a non-GitHub repository. The external ingest upload succeeds, but the finalize request fails with HTTP 412.

The organization policy has already been configured to allow external ingest / semantic indexing for non-GitHub repositories.

## Error log

Another earlier attempt failed with the same error:

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a non-GitHub repository in VS Code.
2. Make sure the organization policy allows external ingest / semantic indexing for non-GitHub repositories.
3. Run `GitHub Copilot: Build Codebase Semantic Index`.
4. Wait for external ingest upload to finish.
5. The upload succeeds, but `external-code-ingest-finalize` fails with HTTP 412.

## Expected Behavior

The semantic index should finalize successfully after all ingestable files are uploaded.

## Actual Behavior

The upload succeeds, but the finalize request fails with:

## Additional Notes

This repository is not hosted on GitHub. It is a non-GitHub repository.

The organization policy has already been configured to allow external ingest / semantic indexing for non-GitHub repositories.

I have retried multiple times. The number of uploaded files changed from 886 to 618, but the failure still occurs at the same finalize step.

Please check the GitHub request IDs:

- `4FF1:88557:9A52E1:9A71D3:6A2A6D31`
- `309F:1D7DE6:8861C7:886F54:6A2A6AE3`
canonical: Semantic indexing for a non-GitHub repository is failing at the finalization step because the service is rejecting the ingest request as not authorized or not eligible despite policy allowing external ingest.

Implementing a synchronization mechanism would allow developers to move fluidly between general chat interactions and specialized agent-driven tasks, ensuring a seamless and continuous workflow regardless of which interface is being utilized.

document:
the issue of showing previous projects and folders on remote explorer

- VS Code Version: windows 11 
- OS Version: 26200.8655

1. create wsl project using any linux os and use that os and open some folders 
2. uninstall the os from wsl or reinstall again or install any other linux os
3. previous uninstalled os information and projects opened will be listed on remote explorer
4. it will create confusions and when opening them crashes will happens
canonical: Remote Explorer keeps showing stale entries for previously used or uninstalled remote folders because its history is not properly cleared when a WSL distribution is removed or replaced.

title: open terminal in editor area is shared between vscode instances
document:
open terminal in editor area is shared between vscode instances

open a vscode in folder A. then press ctrl+p and choose "Terminal: create new in editor Area" 

open another vscode instance in folder B. and then press ctrl+p and choose "Terminal: create new in editor Area" 

you will see the new terminal is created is opened in Folder A

close the terminal in folder A and you will see it get removed from folder B vscode instance.
canonical: Creating a terminal in the editor area is incorrectly shared across separate editor instances, so a terminal opened in one folder appears in another and closing it in one instance removes it from the other because the terminal state is not isolated per window.

title: shell-quote CVE-2026-9277 in 1.8.3, bump to 1.8.4 to resolve
document:
shell-quote CVE-2026-9277 in 1.8.3, bump to 1.8.4 to resolve

- VS Code Version: built from main at commit 05c3dde1c9f
- OS Version: Linux

1. Clone microsoft/vscode at main and run `npm ls shell-quote` in the repo root and in `remote/`
2. Both report shell-quote 1.8.3, which is affected by CVE-2026-9277, fixed in 1.8.4

Details:

shell-quote <= 1.8.3 has a command injection flaw. Fixed in shell-quote 1.8.4. 

CVSS 8.1

Advisory: https://github.com/ljharb/shell-quote/security/advisories/GHSA-w7jw-789q-3m8p

Dependency tree:

- @vscode/sandbox-runtime depends on shell-quote ^1.8.3, declared in both package.json and remote/package.json, so the vulnerable copy also ships with the server build
- npm-run-all2 depends on shell-quote ^1.7.3 (devDependency, build time only)

Since declared ranges already allow 1.8.4, this is fixable by refreshing the resolved version in package-lock.json and remote/package-lock.json; no package.json changes are needed.

I have the lockfile update prepared and will open a PR referencing this issue.
canonical: A vulnerable shell-quote version is pinned in the repository lockfiles, causing the affected command-injection flaw to ship in both the local and remote builds, and the fix is to refresh the resolved dependency to the patched release.

title: Copilot SDK mutates shared extension host process.env (GIT_CONFIG_*=safe.bareRepository=explicit), breaking git for other extensions — Flutter iOS builds fail with "xcodebuild encountered an error (74)"
document:
Copilot SDK mutates shared extension host process.env (GIT_CONFIG_*=safe.bareRepository=explicit), breaking git for other extensions — Flutter iOS builds fail with "xcodebuild encountered an error (74)"

- VS Code Version: 1.122.1 (8761a5560cfd, arm64)
- OS Version: macOS 26.4 (Apple Silicon)
- Built-in GitHub Copilot Chat: 0.50.1 / bundled @github/copilot SDK 1.0.49
- Also involved: Dart-Code + Flutter iOS build (SwiftPM), Xcode 26.5

## Summary

The `@github/copilot` SDK bundled inside the **built-in** Copilot Chat extension mutates the **shared extension host's** `process.env` to inject git hardening config (`safe.bareRepository=explicit`). Every child process spawned by *any other extension* afterwards inherits it, breaking any tool that operates on bare git repos. It broke **all Flutter iOS device builds launched from VS Code** with a misleading error, costing ~3 hours to diagnose:

Terminal `flutter run` and Xcode both work fine — only VS Code launches fail, because only the extension-host environment is poisoned.

## Root cause (from the shipped bundle)

`extensions/copilot/node_modules/@github/copilot/sdk/index.js`, de-minified:

This writes to the **global `process.env` of the shared extension host**, not to the env of Copilot's own child processes.

## Evidence of cross-extension contamination

The Flutter daemon spawned by the (unrelated) Dart extension inherits the injection:

The VS Code main process and the extension host show **no** such variables at spawn time — confirming the mutation happens at runtime, after Copilot activates. It's also racy: a Flutter daemon spawned *before* Copilot activates is clean, so the failure is intermittent across windows/sessions.

## Why it breaks SwiftPM / Flutter iOS builds

SwiftPM stores repository caches as **bare** repos (`~/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm/repositories/`). With `safe.bareRepository=explicit` injected via env, every git call xcodebuild/SwiftPM makes against those caches dies:

A user-level `git config --global safe.bareRepository all` **cannot** fix this — env-injected config outranks global config. Users have no clean way to defend themselves.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a Flutter project with SwiftPM-based iOS deps (e.g. Firebase) in VS Code with the built-in Copilot Chat active.
2. Hit Run / F5 targeting a physical iOS device (or run the minimal repro below).
3. Build fails with `xcodebuild encountered an error (74)`. Terminal `flutter run` and Xcode succeed.

Minimal repro of the poisoned git call (no Flutter needed):

## Expected behavior

Copilot's git hardening should be scoped to the env passed to **its own child processes** (`spawn(cmd, { env: { ...process.env, GIT_CONFIG_* } })`), never written into the shared extension host's global `process.env`. The host is shared by all extensions; mutating its env contaminates unrelated products.

## Workarounds (for others hitting this)

- Dart/Flutter: add `"dart.env": { "GIT_CONFIG_COUNT": "0" }` to settings, then Reload Window; or
- `"chat.disableAIFeatures": true` to disable the built-in Copilot entirely.
canonical: The built-in Copilot SDK mutates the shared extension host environment with Git hardening variables, causing unrelated extensions to inherit those settings and break Git operations on bare repositories.

title: Outline drag-and-drop and layer visibility
document:
Outline drag-and-drop and layer visibility

While it's easy to manage and sort classes, private/public methods, and functions when a project is small, it becomes very difficult to keep all the context in mind as the codebase grows.

Currently, editor-folding is one tool we have for this(though it can be buggy, as I've reported in issue #310842). However, a much more convenient option already exists conceptually: **OUTLINE** view.

Since VS Code already builds an AST to map out the document symbols, could we add some **drag-and-drop** functionality to the Outline panel? I would be incredibly powerful to rearrange classes, functions, and methods directly in the Outline view for languages that support this coding style (moving symbol in the Outline would automatically cut and paste the corresponding block in the editor).

Additionally, the Outline view currently expands fully every time a window or file loads, forcing me to manually collapse things to getan overview. It would be wonderful to introduce visibility toggles/layers, such as:

- `Classes Layer`/`Global Layer` - Collapses everything to show only top-level classes and global functions.
- `Method Layer` - Unfolds classes but keeps their internal methods/properties collapsed.

This would drastically improve code navigation and refactoring efficiency for large files.
canonical: The Outline view lacks support for dragging symbols to reorder code and for remembering or selectively collapsing symbol levels to control visibility in large files.

title: VS Code cannot initialize WebView2 on Windows 11 — Markdown preview crippled, printing impossible
document:
VS Code cannot initialize WebView2 on Windows 11 — Markdown preview crippled, printing impossible

VS Code (System Installer) — latest stable
Windows 11 Home

Problem: 
VS Code cannot initialize WebView2 on this system. As a result:

Markdown preview right‑click menu is crippled (Cut/Copy/Paste only)

Printing is impossible

WebView2 detection returns false even after full system resets

Steps taken (none of which fixed the issue):

Full uninstall of VS Code

Removal of all sandbox folders under AppData\Local\Packages

Removal of all VS Code aliases under WindowsApps

Reinstall of VS Code System Installer

Reset integrity level of VS Code folder

Verified UAC slider at top position

Verified registry ConsentPromptBehaviorAdmin = 2

Full cold boot (Shift + Shut down)

Relaunch VS Code

WebView2 still fails to initialize

Diagnostic test: 
In Developer Tools console:

Code
document.documentElement.outerHTML.includes("ms-webview")
Result: false

Expected behavior: 
WebView2 should initialize normally so Markdown preview and printing work.

Actual behavior: 
WebView2 never initializes. Markdown preview is crippled. Printing is impossible.

Impact: 
This has cost multiple days of troubleshooting. VS Code becomes significantly less useful without printing support. This appears to be a Microsoft‑on‑Microsoft failure between Windows integrity levels, WebView2, and VS Code.
canonical: VS Code is failing to initialize WebView2 on this Windows system, which breaks the Markdown preview context menu and printing, likely because the WebView2 runtime or its integration is being blocked by a system-level installation or permission issue.

title: Korean IME input is dropped or corrupted in Markdown preview Cmd+F find box
document:
Korean IME input is dropped or corrupted in Markdown preview Cmd+F find box

- VS Code Version: 1.123.2
- OS Version: macOS 26.4.1 (25E253), arm64

1. Open any Markdown file.
2. Open the built-in Markdown preview, for example with `Markdown: Open Preview to the Side`.
3. Focus the Markdown preview pane.
4. Press `Cmd+F` to open the find box for the preview.
5. Switch to the Korean input method and type Korean text into the find box, for example `안녕하세요` or another Korean search term.

Expected:

The Korean text should be entered into the Markdown preview find box correctly, with all composed characters preserved.

Actual:

Some Korean keystrokes are dropped or entered incorrectly in the Markdown preview find box. The text shown in the find box can differ from what was typed, making it unreliable to search Korean text in Markdown preview.

Notes:

I searched existing issues and PRs before filing this report. I found related Korean/IME/find/webview issues, but I could not find an exact duplicate for Korean IME keystrokes being dropped or misinterpreted specifically in the Markdown preview `Cmd+F` find box.

I also reproduced the issue with all extensions disabled using `code --disable-extensions`.

The same behavior was confirmed on a colleague's computer as well, so this does not appear to be specific to my local extension setup or user settings.
canonical: Korean IME input is sometimes dropped or corrupted when typing into the Markdown preview find box, likely because the preview’s search field mishandles composed text input events.

title: FLAC files open as binary instead of Media Preview
document:
FLAC files open as binary instead of Media Preview

- VS Code Version: 1.124.0 (1b50d58d73426c9171299ec4037d01365d995b78, arm64)
- OS Version: macOS 26.5 (25F71)

1. Open a `.flac` audio file in VS Code.
2. Observe that VS Code opens it in the text editor and shows: "The file is not displayed in the text editor because it is either binary or uses an unsupported text encoding."
3. Try `Reopen Editor With...`.

Actual:

The `.flac` file is not associated with the built-in Media Preview / Audio Preview editor, so it falls back to the text editor binary-file message.

Expected:

`.flac` files should open with the built-in Audio Preview editor, or be available from `Reopen Editor With...`, consistent with the release notes and codec support docs that list FLAC as supported:

- https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_72#_builtin-preview-for-some-audio-and-video-files
- https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_71#_ffmpeg-codecs-support

Additional info:

The bundled `media-preview` extension appears to register `vscode.audioPreview` for `mp3`, `wav`, `ogg`, and `oga`, but not `flac`.

In my installed VS Code:

The current `main` branch appears to have the same selector:

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/extensions/media-preview/package.json

I searched existing issues for FLAC/media-preview/editor-association terms and did not find an exact duplicate.
canonical: The built-in audio preview does not register FLAC files as supported, so FLAC files open in the text editor as binary instead of using the media preview editor.

Clico em "Sign In", sou redirecionado para o navegador para fazer login com minha conta do GitHub, ao acessar a conta com sucesso o navegador direciona novamente para o Visual Studio Code, neste momento o login deveria funcionar, mas simplesmente aparece "Sign in failed: Error: network error", mas estou conectado a internet, consigo acessar várias páginas na web, minha conexão está estável, tentei reinciar o Visual Studio Code, utilizei a opção "reload window", mas nada acontece, o erro persiste, até mesmo tentei entrar em outra conta, mas o resultado é o mesmo.

title: Adopt native sanitizer API instead of dompurify
document:
Adopt native sanitizer API instead of dompurify

[Once safari lands support](https://caniuse.com/mdn-api_sanitizer), we should explore moving from dompurify to the [native sanitizer api](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Sanitizer)

This has the potential to be safer, faster, and removes one more dependency
canonical: The app should switch from the external HTML sanitizer to the built-in sanitizer API once the browser support is available to reduce dependency risk and improve safety and performance.

title: Release an agentless version of VSCode
document:
Release an agentless version of VSCode

Whilst having agent support is useful, there are scenarios where VSCode due to its versatility as a lightweight editor needs to have no agents.
canonical: VSCode should support an agentless mode so it can be used as a lightweight editor without any agent processes running.

title: Consuming more memory (idle as well) in latest version
document:
Consuming more memory (idle as well) in latest version

- VS Code Version: 1.124.0
- OS Version: Ubuntu 26.04

1. Start both insiders and simple vscode with extensions disabled, it will take around a gig more than version 1.119.0, and it is also more than Cursor with same conditions.

2. With version 1.119.0, you can see how much less idle memory it is taking:

For ref purposes, I disabled AI features in code and it still consumed same amount of memory. And I used 1.119.0 as ref because this is the last version after which you shipped the agent window experience.

Is this because of the new AI features that being built inside vscode that keeps taking space? Won't it be better if you took less memory than your competitors?
canonical: A recent release of the editor appears to use significantly more idle memory than earlier builds, likely due to the newly added agent/AI window features increasing baseline resource usage even when extensions and AI features are disabled.

title: Files without extension break ${extname} in customLabels.patterns
document:
Files without extension break ${extname} in customLabels.patterns

Any files that don't have an extension (e.g. .gitignore, .env, .editorconfig) show the full expansion string as part of the file name. It can be problem when working with submodules (especially in the Open Editors panel).

For example in my setting I have:

Searching in quick open for "gitignore" shows `.gitignore` as well as `.gitignore.${extname} [design-docs] addons/test/design-docs/.gitignore`

The `${extname}` shouldn't be shown when displaying files without an extension. For completeness there should also be a way to remove the trailing dot (or only display it when an extension is present).
canonical: Files without an extension incorrectly keep the literal extension placeholder in custom label patterns because the file name expansion does not suppress the extension part when it is empty.

title: Rename chat "todos" to "tasks"
document:
Rename chat "todos" to "tasks"

The chat feature currently uses the term "todos" (e.g. the todo list / todo tool). The word "todos" is ambiguous because it is also a Spanish word ("todos" = "all/everyone") and is read/pronounced that way, which can be confusing — especially for screen reader users and non-English speakers.

### Proposal
Rename "todos" to "tasks" throughout the chat experience for clarity.

### Areas to update
- UI labels and strings (localized)
- Setting IDs / keys (with backward-compat aliases where needed)
- Command IDs and titles
- Tool name / tool reference name (preserve backward compatibility via legacy names)
- ARIA labels and accessibility help text
- Documentation

### Notes
- Tool/command/setting renames must keep backward compatibility (legacy aliases) so existing references keep working.
canonical: The chat feature should consistently use “tasks” instead of “todos” in labels, commands, tool names, settings, and accessibility text, while keeping legacy aliases for backward compatibility.

title: Editor selection rendering is off in the agents window
document:
Editor selection rendering is off in the agents window

Happens both in the new chat input and in the proper chat input:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/aba35861-f20a-438a-b038-f30795c857a7

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b3e03dc2-badd-4343-aa4e-0b24fe6baba3
canonical: Text selection in the agents chat editor is rendered in the wrong position, causing the highlight to appear misaligned in both the new chat input and the regular chat input.

title: Update Button Modal Flickers During Update
document:
Update Button Modal Flickers During Update

When VS code is downloading an update, hovering over the Update button next to the command palette, causes the modal to flicker as update progress is increased likely from re-rendering on every progress increment.
canonical: The update button modal flickers while an update is downloading because hovering over the button causes it to re-render repeatedly as update progress changes.

title: Agents window: sessions.changes.openSingleFileDiff ignored when workbench.editor.useModal is 'all'
document:
Agents window: sessions.changes.openSingleFileDiff ignored when workbench.editor.useModal is 'all'

When `sessions.changes.openSingleFileDiff` is enabled in the Agents window, clicking a file in the Changes view still opens the multi-file diff instead of a single-file diff — but only when `workbench.editor.useModal` is set to `'all'`.

### Steps to reproduce
1. In the Agents window, set `sessions.changes.openSingleFileDiff: true`.
2. Ensure `workbench.editor.useModal: "all"`.
3. Reload the Agents window.
4. Click a file in the Changes pane.

**Expected:** A single-file diff editor opens.
**Actual:** The multi-file diff editor opens (all changed files shown).

### Root cause
In `src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesView.ts` (`onDidOpen`, ~line 709): when `workbench.editor.useModal === 'all'`, the handler calls `_openFileItem(...)` and returns early, before reaching the `openSingleFileDiff` check (~line 717). The same early-return pattern also exists around line 1051.

### Suggested fix
Check `sessions.changes.openSingleFileDiff` before / within the `useModal === 'all'` branch so the single-file preference takes precedence.

Thanks to a colleague for the investigation.
canonical: When opening a changed file in the Agents window, the single-file diff preference is ignored if modal editors are set to open all files because the modal-editor path returns before the single-file diff check runs.

title: Agents window -- terminal pane missing `X`
document:
Agents window -- terminal pane missing `X`

Note the X should be to the right of the `...`. Confirmed with VB that his macOS version didn't have it, and aaron on windows didn't either.
canonical: The agents window terminal pane is missing the close button next to the options menu, likely due to a UI rendering or layout bug.

title: Subagent sessions should not be listed in the sessions list
document: Subagent sessions should not be listed in the sessions list
canonical: Subagent sessions are incorrectly appearing in the sessions list and should be hidden because they are not user-facing sessions.

title: Integrated Browser: file:// preview is blocked with 403 even when Workspace Trust is disabled (`security.workspace.trust.enabled: false`)
document:
Integrated Browser: file:// preview is blocked with 403 even when Workspace Trust is disabled (`security.workspace.trust.enabled: false`)

**Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?:** Yes

- **VS Code Version:** 1.124.0
- **OS Version:** macOS 14.7.7 (Sonoma), iMac (Late 2013, Intel Core i5, 24 GB RAM)
 - Note: this machine runs Sonoma via OpenCore Legacy Patcher. However, the behavior described below is reproducible from source-level logic that is platform-independent (see Root cause), so it is not specific to this hardware or to macOS.

## Summary

When previewing a standalone `.html` file in the Integrated Browser **without any folder/workspace open** (empty window), the preview is blocked with:

> `Forbidden. File does not reside within a trusted folder.`

This happens **even when Workspace Trust is fully disabled**, with the following user settings:

Setting `security.workspace.trust.enabled: false` is an explicit declaration that the user trusts all local content. The editor honors this (files open without any prompt), but the Integrated Browser's `file://` handler does not, which is inconsistent. Rendering a file in the sandboxed browser view is arguably a weaker operation than opening it in the editor with full extension/task capabilities, yet only the former is blocked.

Additionally, because the **Workspaces: Manage Workspace Trust** command is hidden when `security.workspace.trust.enabled` is `false` (its precondition requires the setting to be `true`), the user has **no discoverable way to fix the 403**: the only UI that could add a trusted folder is unreachable in exactly the configuration that triggers the problem.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Set `"security.workspace.trust.enabled": false` in user settings.
2. Open a new empty VS Code window (no folder/workspace).
3. Open any local `.html` file (`File > Open...`). It opens in the editor without any trust prompt, as expected.
4. Preview it in the Integrated Browser (e.g. **Show Preview** in the editor title bar, or navigate to the `file://` URL in the browser tab).
5. The browser renders: `Forbidden. File does not reside within a trusted folder.` (HTTP 403).
6. Try to add the folder as trusted: `Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P` → "Manage Workspace Trust" — the command does not appear, because Workspace Trust is disabled.

## Expected Behavior

With `security.workspace.trust.enabled: false`, `file://` navigation in the Integrated Browser should be allowed (the user has globally opted out of the trust model), or at minimum the empty-window trust settings (`security.workspace.trust.emptyWindow`, `untrustedFiles: "open"`) should be honored.

## Root Cause (from source)

The 403 is returned by the `file://` protocol handler in
`src/vs/platform/browserView/electron-main/browserSession.ts`:

`_trustedFileRoots` is populated by `sendTrustedFileRoots()` in
`src/vs/workbench/contrib/browserView/electron-browser/browserViewWorkbenchService.ts`, which only considers:

1. the current workspace folders (if `isWorkspaceTrusted()`), and
2. `workspaceTrustManagementService.getTrustedUris()` (the persisted "Trusted Folders & Workspaces" list).

The `security.workspace.trust.enabled` flag is never consulted in this computation. In an empty window there are no workspace folders (so #1 contributes nothing regardless of `emptyWindow: true`), and a user who has had trust disabled has an empty trusted-URI list (so #2 is empty). The result is an empty root set and an unconditional 403 for every `file://` URL.

So the two trust systems are disconnected: the workbench short-circuits to "everything trusted" when the feature is disabled, but the browserView trust-roots computation has no concept of that state.

## Workaround (confirmed)

1. Temporarily set `"security.workspace.trust.enabled": true` and reload.
2. Run **Workspaces: Manage Workspace Trust** → "Trusted Folders & Workspaces" → **Add Folder** → add a parent folder of the HTML files (e.g. the user home directory).
3. Set the setting back to `false` and reload.

The persisted trusted-URI list (`content.trust.model.key`, application storage) is loaded unconditionally by `getTrustedUris()`, so the preview keeps working afterwards. This confirms the mechanism, but it relies on undocumented behavior and requires toggling a security setting just to reach the relevant UI.

## Suggested Fix

In `sendTrustedFileRoots()`, when `workspaceTrustEnablementService.isWorkspaceTrustEnabled()` is `false`, either treat all local files as trusted (consistent with how the rest of the workbench behaves in that state), or honor the empty-window trust result for the file roots as well.
canonical: The integrated browser blocks local file previews from an empty window because its trusted-file root check ignores the global workspace trust disabled setting and only allows files inside explicitly trusted roots.

title: Remove upgrade buttons from Max UI when additional spend is met
document:
Remove upgrade buttons from Max UI when additional spend is met

Ref https://github.slack.com/archives/C0B3Y8JTQ3S/p1781062841683859 

It appears we are still rendering "Upgrade" CTAs for max users if we get 402 with a status code of `additional_spend_limit_reached` and we tell them to Upgrade. For MAX users there is no upgrade available. We should add a check for `canUpgrade` and if `false` direct them to https://github.com/settings/copilot/features
canonical: Max users who have reached the additional spend limit are incorrectly shown upgrade actions instead of being sent to the settings page, caused by missing handling of the no-upgrade-available state.

title: [major] `Codebase` stops GitHub Copilot Restore Checkpoint from working; history corrupted
document:
[major] `Codebase` stops GitHub Copilot Restore Checkpoint from working; history corrupted

In a request, somehow `Codebase` got attached to the request. Now GitHub Copilot says:

> To get more relevant Chat results, we need permission to read the contents of your repository on GitHub.

And stops for me to indicate **Grant**, **Not Now**, or **Never Ask Again**.

Unfortunately I tried to edit the prompt and resubmit, and it duplicated prompt and asked again to read the contents of my GitHub repository!

Worse, **Restore Checkpoint no longer works**. If I try to restore the checkpoint to the point _before_ this particular prompt, none of the prompts and responses are removed (although that previous prompt is copied to the request field, _as if_ the history had been restored to a checkpoint).

This may or may not be related to #319225.
canonical: A request accidentally marked as codebase-scope causes the assistant to keep prompting for repository access and breaks checkpoint restoration by failing to revert the conversation history properly.

title: Closer parity in right-click options for agent host sessions
document:
Closer parity in right-click options for agent host sessions

Folks raised today that there isn't the ability to delete agent host sessions. Here's a comparison of EH vs AH context menu options:

Everything from `Delete...` and later are missing from the AH menu. https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/320522 tracks deletion - could we also explore bringing over the other options?
canonical: Agent host sessions are missing the same right-click actions available for editor host sessions, including deletion and the later context menu options.

document:
Missing context window size picker in agent host sessions

@lostintangent and @cwebster-99 raised that there isn't currently the ability to use the context window size picker when using the agent host.
canonical: The agent host sessions do not currently provide the context window size picker, preventing users from adjusting the context window size.

title: Add documentation for BYOK Custom Endpoint
document:
Add documentation for BYOK Custom Endpoint

The chat language models json schema is not publicly documented well with the features
canonical: The public documentation is missing the custom endpoint option for BYOK chat language model JSON schema features, making it hard to configure supported models correctly.

title: Specify files for AI agents to ignore
document:
Specify files for AI agents to ignore

I would like to have the ability to exclude specific files from being seen by AI agents. I have lots of files that I'm not comfortable with AI seeing, and it has no need to see them. Any time the agent crawls through files or executes commands, it could acknowledge that the file exists, but that it just can't read it. The way I would do it is making a .aiignore file similar to how you can make a .gitignore file.
canonical: The bug is that AI agents can currently access all project files with no way to exclude sensitive files from being read, and the cause is the lack of an ignore mechanism for agent file access.

title: Voice input should mute mic routing to other apps (e.g. Teams) while active
document:
Voice input should mute mic routing to other apps (e.g. Teams) while active

When using Copilot voice in VS Code during a video call (Teams, Zoom, Meet, etc.), the microphone input is shared across all applications simultaneously. This means coworkers on the call can hear everything spoken to Copilot — prompts, code context, questions — which is disruptive and can be professionally awkward.

Requested behavior:
When Copilot voice is actively listening, it should take exclusive mic access, temporarily blocking other apps from receiving audio input.

Upon releasing the mic, normal routing should resume immediately.

Why this matters:
Voice-driven AI assistance is most useful during active development — which frequently overlaps with collaborative calls. Without mic isolation, users must manually mute themselves in the calling app before speaking to Copilot, which is error-prone and breaks conversation flow. This is both a usability issue and a professional privacy concern.
canonical: Copilot voice does not take exclusive access to the microphone while listening, so other apps can still hear the same input and expose private spoken prompts during calls.

title: Regression: spaces render too wide with a custom monospaced font
document:
Regression: spaces render too wide with a custom monospaced font

- VS Code Version: 1.124
- OS Version: win-11 25h2 x64

After upgrading VS Code, a custom monospaced font that has worked correctly for years no longer preserves alignment.

I use a custom monospaced font that contains Latin characters, Chinese/CJK characters, and symbols. The font is designed so that Latin characters, symbols, and spaces occupy 1 cell, while Chinese/CJK characters occupy exactly 2 Latin cells.

However, this regression does not require any CJK characters to reproduce. It can be reproduced with English text and spaces only.

I have confirmed that the main problem is that the space character is being rendered too wide. This alone is enough to break column alignment. Some English characters also appear bolder than before, which may or may not be related, but the incorrect space width is the core regression.

I also tried changing the setting that controls whitespace rendering between `svg` and `font`, but the problem remains.

## Expected behavior

Spaces should have the same advance width as Latin monospace characters in the selected font.

Usually, English text aligned with spaces should keep its columns:

This breaks layouts that rely on monospace alignment, even though the same font still works correctly in older VS Code versions.

Regression
This works correctly in VS Code 1.110.1.

It breaks after upgrading to a newer VS Code version. The version where I observed the regression was 1.124.0.

1. use monospaced font 
2. update vscode(user)
3. open vscode
canonical: VS Code has regressed so that spaces are measured or rendered wider than the monospace font’s character cell, breaking alignment even for plain English text.
