title: chat: deleting last user tool set should remove .toolsets.jsonc
document:
chat: deleting last user tool set should remove .toolsets.jsonc

## Problem
When deleting user-defined tool sets, removing the final remaining tool set leaves .toolsets.jsonc on disk (often as an empty or effectively empty file).

## Expected behavior
If the last user tool set is deleted, the extension should delete .toolsets.jsonc entirely instead of leaving an empty config file behind.

## Why this matters
- Keeps workspace config clean and intention-revealing.
- Matches user expectation that no tool sets means no tool-set file.
- Avoids edge cases in file watchers/parsers around empty config artifacts.

## Repro
1. Create .toolsets.jsonc with one user tool set.
2. Delete that tool set via the UI/action.
3. Observe file remains instead of being removed.

## Suggested fix
- On delete, if no user tool sets remain, remove .toolsets.jsonc.
- Respect FS trash capability checks and provide actionable error messages if removal fails.
canonical: Deleting the last user-defined tool set should remove the tool sets configuration file from disk instead of leaving an empty file behind.

title: Re-implement lazy loading of chat session items without using IAgentSessionsService in the sessions workbench
document:
Re-implement lazy loading of chat session items without using IAgentSessionsService in the sessions workbench

## Context

Commit c385fd3ddeb819e2b4d0563b4621990bafdbef6c (PR #312047, "feat(copilot): enable lazy loading for chat session items") introduced calls to `IAgentSessionsService.model.observeSession(...)` directly inside the **sessions workbench** (`src/vs/sessions`):

- `src/vs/sessions/contrib/sessions/browser/views/sessionsList.ts` (`renderSession`)
- `src/vs/sessions/services/sessions/browser/visibleSessions.ts` (`_getOrCreateVisibleSession`, originally added in `sessionsManagementService.setActiveSession`)

This violates the sessions layer architecture: `IAgentSessionsService` / the agent sessions model (`vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/**`) must **only** be consumed by the Copilot chat sessions provider (`contrib/providers/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts`), never by shared sessions-workbench code. The sessions layer is provider-agnostic and must not reach into a specific provider's model.

## What was done

The cross-layer `observeSession` triggers and the `IAgentSessionsService` dependency were **reverted** from `sessionsList.ts` and `visibleSessions.ts` (plus the now-stale test scaffolding). This removes the lazy-loading trigger that those calls provided.

## Follow-up needed

Re-implement lazy loading of chat session items (the on-demand `observeSession` trigger that populates expensive properties such as changes/badges) **the right way**, without importing `IAgentSessionsService` into the sessions workbench. Options to consider:

- Drive the trigger from inside the Copilot provider itself (it already legitimately depends on `IAgentSessionsService`), e.g. by reacting to a provider-agnostic visibility signal exposed by `ISessionsManagementService` / `ISession`.
- Or expose a provider-agnostic "session became visible/closed" signal on the sessions services that the Copilot provider subscribes to in order to call `observeSession` on its own sessions.

cc @DonJayamanne (author of PR #312047)
canonical: Lazy loading of chat session items stopped working because the sessions workbench no longer triggers the chat sessions model to observe visible sessions, so expensive session details are not populated on demand.

title: Explore an "efficiency mode" selection for chat
document:
Explore an "efficiency mode" selection for chat

In an effort to help users optimize their AI credits, we should explore adding an "efficiency" or "low cost" mode that can be toggled on for any model. It would enforce changes like: default reasoning effort, default context window, low reasoning for subagents, and add specifications to the system prompt to operate in low cost/efficiency mode to reduce tool calls and other optimization strategies. 

Prototype (building off @eli-w-king proposed config picker) 

Enabling efficiency mode would override the current selections for context window and thinking effort and restore the defaults. 

We would perhaps want to add some indication in the dropdown preview that efficiency mode is enabled (not reflected in the screenshot above). The CLI is also investigating a similar idea!
canonical: Selecting efficiency mode should override a model’s normal context and reasoning settings and apply low-cost defaults, but the current configuration picker does not support a toggle that changes these behavior defaults across chat and subagents.

title: Agents: Consider command naming or consolidation for navigating forward and back between sessions
document:
Agents: Consider command naming or consolidation for navigating forward and back between sessions

Testing #320316

The recency history and forward/back navigation are great. I wonder if we might consider slightly more detailed command names to help users discover and understand this functionality.

I think `Go Back` and `Go Forward` are concise options that work well as tooltips over the arrows in the UI (and similarly match how tools like browsers name these features). Though as standalone command names without the arrow UI context, I wonder if they could sound like they're referring to things other than just recent sessions, such as turns in a chat, or other previously opened interfaces (like changes or customizations).

I was going to suggest command phrasing like one of the following:
- `Agents: Go Back One Session` `Agents: Go Forward One Session`
- `Agents: Go Back To Session` `Agents: Go Forward To Session`
- `Agents: Go To Previous Session` `Agents: Go To Next Session`

Though as I continued to explore existing commands, I found we already have `Agents: Navigate to Previous Session` and `Agents: Navigate to Next Session`, which seem to function the same as `Go Back` and `Go Forward`. If `Agents: Navigate to Previous Session` and `Agents: Navigate to Next Session` do the same thing as the back and forward commands, should we consolidate into 2 instead of 4 commands?
canonical: The issue is that there are duplicate commands for moving between recent sessions, and the request is to clarify or consolidate them so the navigation actions have clearer, less ambiguous names.

title: ahp: auto-discovery mechanism
document:
ahp: auto-discovery mechanism

I have a service that creates ephemeral sandboxes (hosted in ACA https://sandboxes.azure.com/) and they run `code agent host …` on them.

Currently, I have to manually edit my `settings.json` to get them to appear in vscode. However, if I do a similar thing with `code tunnel` or ssh config, vscode automatically lists/shows any discoverable clients.

I'd like a generic way to have vscode discover my clients. One idea is to add a setting to vscode with a discover URL(s):

`chat.agentHost.autoDiscover: [ { endpoint: https://agents.example.com/agent, connectionToken: } ]`

`connectionToken` should be optional. endpoint can be polled. vscode should respect a www-authenticate challenge so auth can be done with my own IdP for OAuth similar to MCP spec.
canonical: VS Code does not currently have a generic auto-discovery mechanism for agent hosts, so clients created by external services are not automatically listed unless they are manually configured or exposed through an existing supported discovery path.

title: Add "Create from current selection" to Configure Tool Sets
document:
Add "Create from current selection" to Configure Tool Sets

## Summary

Today, "Configure Tool Sets..." lets users create a new tool sets file or open an existing user-defined tool set, but it does not help them capture the tools they have already chosen in an active chat session. This creates unnecessary friction when a user has already curated a useful tool selection in agent mode and wants to turn that selection into a reusable tool set.

We should add a "Create from current selection" flow that creates a new tool set definition pre-filled from the currently enabled tools and tool sets in the active chat input.

## Problem

The current flow is file-oriented, not selection-oriented:

- "Configure Tool Sets..." only offers:
 - create a new tool sets file
 - open an existing user tool set file
- The active chat already tracks the current enabled tool/tool set state
- The tools picker already exposes a "Configure Tool Sets..." entry, but that action loses the context of what the user currently has selected

This means users have to manually re-create a tool set after they have already done the work of selecting the right tools in chat.

## Proposal

Add a new action inside the "Configure Tool Sets..." flow:

- **"Create from current selection..."**

This action should:

- Detect the current tool selection from the active chat widget
- Create a new .toolsets.jsonc file, or allow appending to a chosen user tool sets file
- Insert a new tool set object with the ools property pre-filled from the current selection
- Open the file in the editor so the user can finish naming, describing, and optionally changing the icon

## Expected UX

### Entry point

The feature should be available from the existing "Configure Tool Sets..." command.

When invoked from the tools picker while a chat widget is active, the command should have access to the current tool selection immediately.

If invoked without suitable active chat context, we should either hide "Create from current selection", or show it but fall back gracefully with a clear message.

### Suggested flow

Inside "Configure Tool Sets...", offer:

- Create from current selection...
- Create new tool sets file...
- Existing user tool sets...

If the user picks "Create from current selection...":

1. Prompt for the target file name, or allow picking an existing user tool sets file
2. Prompt for the new tool set name
3. Create a tool set stub pre-filled from the current selection
4. Open the resulting file

### Generated content example

`jsonc
{
 "myToolSet": {
 "tools": [
 "read",
 "search",
 "github/issues"
 ],
 "description": "",
 "icon": "tools"
 }
}
`

## Implementation notes

### Source of truth

The current selection already exists as an enablement map in the active widget:
- src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/input/chatSelectedTools.ts
- src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/attachments/chatVariables.ts

We should reuse that state instead of recomputing from the picker UI.

### Serialization rules

- If a tool set is enabled, write the tool set reference name, not every child tool
- If an individual tool is enabled outside of an enabled tool set, write the individual tool reference name
- De-duplicate entries
- Use fully qualified reference names where appropriate to match prompt syntax

### Initial implementation

For the first version:
- Create a new .toolsets.jsonc file with a single pre-filled tool set object

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] "Configure Tool Sets..." includes a "Create from current selection..." option when there is an active chat tool selection
- [ ] Choosing that option creates a new tool set definition pre-filled from the active chat selection
- [ ] Enabled tool sets are preserved as tool set references rather than expanded
- [ ] Individually enabled tools are preserved
- [ ] Created content is valid .toolsets.jsonc and opens in the editor
- [ ] Existing "Create new tool sets file..." behavior remains unchanged
- [ ] If there is no active chat selection, the experience is graceful

## Why this helps

This makes tool sets much easier to discover and author from real usage. Users already build useful tool combinations interactively in chat. Turning that state into a reusable tool set should be one step, not a manual transcription exercise.
canonical: The bug is that the tool set configuration flow cannot create a reusable tool set from the currently active chat tool selection, forcing users to manually recreate the selected tools instead of preserving the existing enabled tools and tool set references.

title: macOS: Ctrl+F and Ctrl+P intercepted by workbench when typing in webview text input, breaking Cocoa text navigation
document:
macOS: Ctrl+F and Ctrl+P intercepted by workbench when typing in webview text input, breaking Cocoa text navigation

## Summary

On macOS, \`Ctrl+F\` (forward character) and \`Ctrl+P\` (previous line) are system-level Cocoa text navigation bindings active in every native text input — Terminal, Safari address bar, Spotlight, etc. When the cursor is in a **webview extension panel's text input**, these keys have no effect: the cursor does not move.

This is distinct from clipboard and iframe issues (e.g. #129178). It is not about Cmd-key shortcuts. It is specifically about \`Ctrl+F\`/\`Ctrl+P\` as **cursor-movement keys** (a macOS-native expectation) failing silently inside webview text inputs.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Install any extension that renders a chat/text input as a webview panel (Claude Code, Cline, Continue, OpenCode, etc.)
2. Open the panel and click into the text input
3. Type some text
4. Press \`Ctrl+F\` — **expected**: cursor moves forward one character; **actual**: nothing happens
5. Press \`Ctrl+P\` — **expected**: cursor moves to previous line; **actual**: nothing happens

Reproduces on macOS only. Does not reproduce on Windows/Linux (those OSes do not have Cocoa bindings).

## Environment

- macOS (Sequoia 15.x)
- VS Code 1.x (latest stable)

## Expected Behavior

When focus is inside a webview text input, \`Ctrl+F\` and \`Ctrl+P\` should be passed through to the webview so the Cocoa text system can handle them as cursor-movement commands, consistent with every other native macOS text input.

## Actual Behavior

The keys have no effect. The cursor does not move.

## Notes

- The suspected mechanism is that workbench keybindings (\`workbench.action.findInFiles\`, \`workbench.action.quickOpen\`) consume these keys before they reach the webview — but this is inferred, not confirmed.
- Multiple extensions have independently hit this and implemented JS-side \`preventDefault\` workarounds (Cline, Continue). This is evidence that the problem is systemic.
- Downstream issue reports: [anthropics/claude-code#66291](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/66291), [anomalyco/opencode#26519](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/26519)
- The root question is whether the workbench keybindings for \`Ctrl+F\`/\`Ctrl+P\` should have a \`when\` guard that excludes focus inside a webview text input — or whether the webview preload script should suppress these keys before they reach the workbench dispatcher.
canonical: Workbench keybindings are intercepting macOS control-key text navigation shortcuts before they reach webview text inputs, so cursor movement like forward character and previous line does not work.

title: Remote-SSH 0.107.1 breaks on macOS due to Unix socket path exceeding 104-char limit
document:
Remote-SSH 0.107.1 breaks on macOS due to Unix socket path exceeding 104-char limit

After updating Remote-SSH to 0.107.1, SSH remote connections fail with:
 listen EINVAL: invalid argument .sock
The IPC socket path generated is over 104 characters, which exceeds 
the macOS Unix socket path limit (UNIX_PATH_MAX = 104).
Example path generated:
/var/folders/6n/t4tspn250ndg0nrhsmqqvy2w0000gn/T/vscode-ssh-askpass-63e2a764c02090921c0643db5296287eac1cb1cd.sock
(110 chars)
**Workaround:**
Set TMPDIR to a shorter path:
 launchctl setenv TMPDIR /tmp
 pkill -9 -f "Visual Studio Code"
Then reopen VS Code.
**Root cause:**
The socket filename vscode-ssh-askpass-.sock (64 chars) combined 
with the macOS per-user temp dir /var/folders/...//T/ exceeds 104 chars.
Older versions used shorter socket names that stayed under the limit.

Remote OS version: Linux x64 4.18.0-553.123.1.el8_10.x86_64
canonical: Remote-SSH is generating an IPC Unix socket path that is too long for macOS, causing SSH remote connections to fail because the temporary socket name exceeds the platform’s path-length limit.

title: Copilot CLI: `create` tool hangs indefinitely for paths outside the workspace
document:
Copilot CLI: `create` tool hangs indefinitely for paths outside the workspace

## Bug

The Copilot CLI agent's `create` tool hangs indefinitely when creating files at paths **outside the workspace** (e.g., `/tmp/test.txt`). After a long wait, it returns `"The execution of this tool, or a previous tool was interrupted."` with no error details. Creating files **inside the workspace** works fine.

The `edit` tool (which only modifies existing files, typically within the workspace) is unaffected.

## Environment

- **VS Code:** 1.123.0
- **Copilot Chat Extension:** 0.51.0
- **OS:** macOS (Darwin, arm64)
- **Agent mode:** Copilot CLI (terminal-based agent)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a workspace in VS Code
2. Start a Copilot CLI agent session
3. Ask the agent to create a file at an out-of-workspace path, e.g., `/tmp/test-file.txt`
4. The `create` tool call hangs indefinitely — no visible approval dialog or notification appears
5. Eventually the session times out or the user cancels, returning: `"The execution of this tool, or a previous tool was interrupted."`

**Contrast with in-workspace path:**

6. Ask the agent to create a file inside the workspace, e.g., `./test-file.txt`
7. The `create` tool completes immediately and successfully

## Root Cause from Session Logs

The session event log (`events.jsonl`) reveals a **double permission request**. Here's the exact sequence for an out-of-workspace `create` call:

| Timestamp | Event | Detail |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| T+0ms | `tool.execution_start` | `create` tool begins for `/tmp/test.txt` |
| T+1ms | `hook.start` / `hook.end` | PreToolUse hook runs and completes instantly (no interference) |
| T+2ms | `permission.requested` (1st) | Write approval prompt — `kind: "write"`, `canOfferSessionApproval: true` |
| T+120ms | `permission.completed` → **approved** | User approves the first prompt |
| T+121ms | `permission.requested` (2nd) | **A second write approval prompt fires immediately** — identical content, identical `toolCallId` |
| *(hangs)* | *(waiting for approval)* | No UI appears for the second prompt — no dialog, no notification, no editor tab |
| T+~20min | `abort` (user_initiated) | User cancels after waiting |

For **in-workspace paths**, only a single `permission.requested` is emitted and the tool completes normally.

## What Was Ruled Out

| Hypothesis | Test | Result |
|-----------|------|--------|
| PreToolUse hook interference | Disabled all hooks (`~/.copilot/hooks/` emptied), reloaded window, retested | ❌ Same hang |
| Notification dialog stuck behind window | Set `"chat.notifyWindowOnConfirmation": false` | ❌ Same hang |
| Transient/intermittent failure | Reproduced 6+ times across 4 different sessions over 5 days | ❌ 100% reproducible for out-of-workspace paths |
| `edit` tool affected | `edit` works fine for existing files | ✅ Only `create` is affected |
| In-workspace paths affected | `create` inside workspace succeeds instantly | ✅ Only out-of-workspace paths hang |

## Expected Behavior

The `create` tool should either:
1. **Work** — issue a single permission prompt and proceed after approval, regardless of file path, or
2. **Fail fast with a clear error** — e.g., "Cannot create files outside the workspace" — instead of hanging silently

## Workaround

Use the `bash` tool with a heredoc or `cat` redirect instead of `create` for out-of-workspace files. This bypasses the file-write permission system entirely.
canonical: The create tool incorrectly requests write permission twice for files outside the workspace, causing the operation to wait indefinitely after the first approval.

title: Option to project from "open recent" in new window
document:
Option to project from "open recent" in new window

## Feature Request

### Issue
Currently, when navigating the menu workflow (**File -> Open Recent**), clicking a project automatically opens it in the current window, closing the active workspace. 

If a user wants to keep their current project open and open a recent project alongside it, they must manually open a completely new VS Code window first, navigate to **File -> Open Recent**, and select it from there. 

While holding `Ctrl/Cmd` + Left-Click exists as a hidden modifier to force a new window, this behavior is completely undiscoverable for mouse-heavy users. Furthermore, all three mouse buttons (Left, Right, and Scroll click) currently execute the exact same default behavior in this menu, representing an opportunity for a better user experience.

### Proposed Solution
Allow users to explicitly choose to open a recent project in a new window directly from the **File -> Open Recent** mouse workflow. 

**Described workflow:** 
File -> Open Recent -> {Action} -> Opens in current window OR new window.

Since native OS top-menus (rendered by Electron) heavily restrict right-click popups or scroll-click events, I propose a few potential architectural alternatives for the team to consider:

1. **Alternative 1 (Preferred for UI Discoverability):** Add a visual "Open in New Window" inline action button or arrow icon next to the project name in the menu list (similar to how extensions have inline action buttons).
2. **Alternative 2 (Power User Toggle):** Introduce a new global setting, such as `"window.openRecentInNewWindow": "default" | "always" | "never"`, allowing users to swap the default Left-Click behavior.
3. **Alternative 3 (Enhanced Mouse Hooks):** If Electron/OS limitations permit, map **Scroll-Click** on a recent item to trigger the "Open in New Window" behavior, maximizing the utility of the 3 mouse buttons.

### Additional Context
This change would greatly improve quality-of-life and multitasking speed for developers who jump between multiple repositories using the mouse menu.
canonical: Opening a project from the recent list always replaces the current workspace instead of offering a discoverable way to open the recent project in a new window.

title: Can't connect to WSL after window reload
document:
Can't connect to WSL after window reload

Testing #320340

After connecting to my WSL (Ubuntu), and then reloading VS Code, trying to reconnect fails (even though WSL is still running).

The "Reconnect" button seems to do nothing:

And going through the `WSL...` -> `Ubuntu` route fails with `Failed to connect to WSL distribution 'Ubuntu': Connection closed: wsl:Ubuntu`.

I seem to have to run `wsl --shutdown`, restart WSL, and reload VS Code to get it to work again.
canonical: Reloading the editor can leave the WSL remote connection stuck so reconnecting to the same distribution fails until WSL is fully restarted, likely because the existing remote session is not being cleaned up properly.

Error connecting to stopped WSL (Ubuntu)

title: Got "Language model unavailable" error when calling openSessionWithPrompt
document:
Got "Language model unavailable" error when calling openSessionWithPrompt

# Background
We bulit an extension which implements a custom type of session, called swarm session, based on chatSessionContentProvider. The extension has its own models registered via VS Code API. We allows one swarm session to send messages to another swarm session via tool. When sending a message, if we login the GitHub, we have no problem, but if we don't login, we will get the error "Language model unavailable", although our extension registers its models to VS Code.

# Steps to Reproduce
1. Precondition: Not login Github account.
2. Send a message from one swarm session to another swarm session via openSessionWithPrompt, like vscode.commands.executeCommand(`workbench.action.chat.openSessionWithPrompt.${SWARM_SESSION_SCHEME}`, { resource: targetSessionUri, prompt }). We would like to give a predefined model as well, but the command API does not support it and so the model will be provided later with the swarm custom ChatSessionContentProvider.
3. The call will go through the getModelForRequest and getDefaultlanguageModel. Since getDefaultLanguageModel can only return a model with COPILOT_VENDOR_ID (copilot) and I don't login GitHub, the getDefaultLanguageModel returns no model and then getModelForRequest throw the error "Language model unavailable". This error blocks our process of sending messages.

Could you please enable getDefaultlanguageModel to return a BYOK model, which is not from 'copilot', as a default model?
canonical: Calling openSessionWithPrompt can fail with a language model unavailable error because the default model selection only accepts Copilot models and does not fall back to a registered BYOK model when no GitHub account is signed in.

title: Always use main Tab Group when opening new Editors from Explorer
document:
Always use main Tab Group when opening new Editors from Explorer

In VSCode, you can open a folder into your session. Then you can click on a file in Explorer view on the left hand side, which opens a Preview. By double clicking a file in Explorer you can open multiple files, they become Editors in the session. Editors are organized as Tabs in to Tab Groups. You can split the layout by dragging an Editor to the side of a Tab Groups view. That will result in two Tab Groups that both can have multiple Tabs. I don't know if either Tab Group is considered as "main view" or if they are equal. The terms are a bit hazy to me, but I hope you can understand what I'm saying.

I usually work with a split layout of Tab Groups so that I have "main tabs" with most of the Editors and "side tabs" with one or two Editors in a narrower view. When I select a file in Explorer that I don't have open in an Editor, a Preview opens up into the view that was last active ("main tabs" or "side tabs"). I would prefer to always open new Editors and Preview into the "main tabs". This becomes more of an issue when I have third or fourth Tab Group open in another monitor and many Editors. When surfing through files with Explorer, VSCode focuses onto Editors and Tab Groups that I already have open, but if I then click on a file that's not yet opened, it opens quite often onto a "wrong" Tab Group, which gets annoying when going on from 9 to 5.

Copilot said there is currently no setting to control this behaviour. I would be most pleased to see this kind of setting. If for any technical reason you shun on the idea of designating a "Main Tab Group", then "the first one" would be sufficient.
canonical: Opening a file from the Explorer should always open in the primary tab group instead of the most recently active or otherwise arbitrary tab group.

title: VSCode seems it does not respect my profiles when starting some extensions
document:
VSCode seems it does not respect my profiles when starting some extensions

Hello there,

I recently posted a similar issue for new Agents window. In that task I explained that I disabled many features in the "Default" profile, including all AI, and the profile I am starting agent window is fully decorated for AI works. Yet, agents window tries to use "Default" profile to connect agents, and fails miserably to do so becuause of changes I made to "Default" profile.

The same is now happenning with Remote Explorer.

I also removed Remote Explorer from "Default" profile. And now I am not able to connect WSL containers, and vscode complains saying "Extension 'WSL' is required. And it says this after I started this connection from within Remote Explorer, just from another profile set for this purpose.

How can you recreate the issue? follow these steps:
1- Open "Default" profile and remove WSL, and maybe Remote Explorer(?!)
2- Create a new profile, from scratch or using default
3- switch to new profile, find and install WSL
4- open Remote explorer window and try to connect a WSL container (current or new windows, wont matter)
5- Now witness: VSCode switches to "Default" profile immediately before trying to connect, and it fails because there is no WSL in "Default" profile.
6- Bonus: open File menu, and then "Close Remote Connection". The profile stays in "Default".

One exception I saw so far is this:
If I enable WSL in "Default" profile, open a remote folder, and switch to another profile that has WSL enabled within that folder, then next time I try to connect from non-Default profile, it reopens the folder in that profile without a problem.

Everything I use in VSCode should be contained within the profile I am working in. The exception clearly shows that my profiles are respected in the workspace level, but not in global level. 

I hope you fix this issue and bring balance and peace to the use of profiles.

Connection to 'WSL: AgenticTraining' could not be established No remote extension installed to resolve wsl.
canonical: Starting remote or agent-related connections ignores the active profile and switches to the default profile, causing the connection to fail when the needed extension is only installed in another profile.

## GitHub Tags
#A11yTCS; #Visual Studio Code Client; #agency:plugin=a11y@company; #ES chat;#E2E_VisualStudioCodeClient_MAC_Dec2025;#DesktopApp; #FTP; #Mac; #Device; #A11ySev2;#A11yMAS;#WCAG2.1.1; #VoiceOver; #SCIM:5271834; #External; #External:VoiceOver; #ThirdParty; #ResolvedByTT;

## Issue Subcategory
Element not reachable via keyboard 

## User Experience
Keyboard-only users (including screen reader users and those with motor disabilities) cannot access or interact with the control at all. This creates a complete barrier to that functionality. 

## Repro Steps
Open VS code in MAC > Turn on Voice Over > Resources expandable control > Views and more actions control > Observe the issue: Keyboard navigation is blocked between “Resources” expandable control and “Views and more actions” using VO navigation (Ctrl+Option keys)

## Actual Result
When keyboard focus is placed on the Resources expandable control, navigating backward to the “Views and more actions” control using Control + Option + Left Arrow requires an excessive number of keystrokes.
 After reaching the “Views and more actions” control, the user is unable to navigate forward using Control + Option + Right Arrow, causing focus to get stuck at this control.

## Expected Result
Users should be able to navigate efficiently and consistently between interactive controls using Control + Option + Arrow keys.
 Focus should move logically in both forward and backward directions without requiring excessive keystrokes or getting stuck on any control.

## How to Fix
Use natively focusable elements (button, a href, input). For custom controls, add tabindex="0" and implement keyboard event handlers for activation. 

## Environment Details:
Application: VS Code

macOS: 15.3

## Attachment

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/67b3af0a-600b-493e-9d7b-9cc091ac080d
canonical: VoiceOver focus order is broken so keyboard navigation gets stuck between the Resources expandable control and the Views and more actions control because one or both controls are not exposed as properly focusable elements.

title: Linux Pasting file from Nautilus only shows filename or "/" instead of full path
document:
Linux Pasting file from Nautilus only shows filename or "/" instead of full path

- VS Code Version: 1.123.0 (also tested on 1.122.1)
- OS Version: Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS, GNOME Shell 46.0, X11 (`XDG_SESSION_TYPE=x11`)
- Hardware: x86_64

1. Open Nautilus (GNOME Files) file manager
2. Select any file and press `Ctrl+C` (or right-click → Copy)
3. Switch to VS Code editor and press `Ctrl+V`
4. Instead of the full file path (e.g., `/home/user/Documents/test.pdf`), only the filename (`test.pdf`) or just `/` is pasted

---

## Root Cause Analysis

When a file is copied in Nautilus, the system clipboard contains **three MIME types**:

| MIME Type | Content |
|---|---|
| `text/uri-list` | `file:///home/user/Documents/test.pdf` ✅ full path (URL-encoded) |
| `text/plain` | `test.pdf` ❌ filename only |
| `x-special/gnome-copied-files` | `copy\nfile:///home/user/Documents/test.pdf` (GNOME internal) |

**Old VS Code behavior**: Read `text/uri-list`, decoded the `file://` URI, and inserted the full path as text.

**Current VS Code behavior** (1.122.x / 1.123.x): Reads `text/plain`, which only contains the filename.

I verified this by checking the clipboard with `xclip`:

The `text/uri-list` data is present and correct, but VS Code ignores it.

## Attempted Fixes (none worked)

1. `"files.copyFilesAsText": true` — only controls copy behavior inside VS Code, not paste from external apps
2. `"editor.pasteAs.enabled": false` — disables the paste-as feature but VS Code still reads `text/plain`
3. `"editor.pasteAs.preferences": ["text.plain"]` — limits MIME type selection but doesn't change which format is read
4. Downgraded from 1.123.0 to 1.122.1 — behavior changed from `/` to filename, still not full path
5. `"editor.dropIntoEditor.enabled": false` — no effect on paste behavior

## Workaround

I created a small VS Code extension (`nautilus-clipboard-fix`) that:
1. Polls the clipboard every 300ms using `xclip -selection clipboard -t text/uri-list -o`
2. When a `file://` URI is detected, decodes it to a local path and caches it
3. Intercepts `Ctrl+V` — if a cached path exists, inserts it as text; otherwise falls back to normal paste

This confirms the issue is purely in VS Code's clipboard reading logic: the `text/uri-list` data is available on the clipboard, but VS Code's Electron clipboard API on Linux reads `text/plain` instead.

## Related Issues

- #170708 — Cannot paste full file paths after copying file from file explorer on Linux
- #147285 — Copy/paste file path from the file explorer is not working
- #155453 — File path not pasted from file manager in Linux

## Suggested Fix

When pasting from an external file manager on Linux, VS Code should:
1. Check if `text/uri-list` contains `file://` URIs
2. If yes, decode them to local paths and insert as text
3. Only fall back to `text/plain` if no `file://` URIs are found

This would restore the behavior that worked in older versions and matches what most Linux users expect.
canonical: VS Code on Linux pastes the filename or slash instead of the full file path when copying a file from the file manager because it reads the plain-text clipboard entry instead of the available file URI list.

title: Unable to use certain fonts in the editor (Consolas) since the last update
document:
Unable to use certain fonts in the editor (Consolas) since the last update

- VS Code Version: 1.123.0
- OS Version: Arch Linux

1. Be on Linux.
2. Copy `consola.ttf` from a Windows machine, to `/usr/local/share/fonts/WindowsFonts/consola.ttf`
3. Set `"editor.fontFamily": "Consolas",`
4. Observe that the font is not actually Consolas.

 E.g. by pressing Ctrl+Shift+P -> `Developer: Toggle Developer Tools`, then click the button in the top-left corner of devtools to select an element, then click on text in the editor. Then in the devtools, open the `Computed` tab, and at the bottom see `Rendered Fonts` -> `Family Name`.

 In my case it resolves to `Noto Sans Mono`, which seems to be a system-wide default.

This is a regression, it worked fine in the previous version.

Other fonts in `/usr/local/share/fonts/...` do work for me, it's this specific font that doesn't.

Could be related to: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/319988

`fc-match` does resolve this font correctly:
canonical: The editor no longer resolves a requested installed font correctly and falls back to a default monospace font instead of using that font.

`@endgame-champ`:

 - [ ] Note: The [cherry-pick workflow](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/actions/workflows/cherry-pick.yml) auto-generates cherry-pick PRs from `release/*` into main, those must also be merged. ([example](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/317220))

Feature Request: Support contextWindow for BYOK Models

# Problem

The current BYOK model configuration only exposes:

* Context Length: 1M
* Max Output Length: 384K

To fit the current schema, users must derive:

This is problematic because `maxInputTokens` is a derived value rather than an actual model capability. The original `contextWindow` information is lost, making some models difficult to describe accurately.

# Proposed Solution

Add support for:

title: Allow configuring a separate (cheaper) model for context compaction (/compact)
document:
Allow configuring a separate (cheaper) model for context compaction (/compact)

### Feature Request

When a chat conversation gets long, VS Code auto-compacts (summarizes) older messages to free up context window space. Users can also trigger this manually with `/compact`.

Problem: compaction currently uses the same model as the active chat session. So if you're on Claude Opus 4.8 or GPT-5.5, every `/compact` call burns through premium tokens at the same rate as a full chat response - even though summarization is a much simpler task.

This just feels like a waste. Compaction doesn't need to reason about code or generate complex logic - it's basically "condense this conversation." A lightweight model should handle it just fine.

### Suggested Solution

Let users configure a separate model for compaction. Other participants already have something like this - e.g. `chat.planAgent.defaultModel` lets you pick a model from a dropdown. Compaction should get the same treatment.

Something like:

Same pattern, just for compaction. Defaults to the chat model if not set, but lets you pick something cheaper if you want.

This would:
- Save credits/tokens for people on premium models
- Use faster, cheaper models for a task that doesn't need much brainpower
- Let power users stretch their monthly Copilot budget further

### Alternative Approaches

1. Auto-select cheapest - VS Code could just pick the cheapest available model from the same provider for compaction, no config needed.
2. Fixed lightweight default - Always use something like GPT-5 mini or Claude Haiku for compaction, with an option to override if someone wants to.

### Prior Art

Other tools already have similar requests:

- Claude Code CLI - Feature request for `compactModel` setting (anthropics/claude-code#12660)
- Codex CLI - Request for separate compaction model (openai/codex#13739)
- OpenClaude (VS Code extension) - `compactModel` config option request (Gitlawb/openclaude#1445)

VS Code already supports per-task model configuration in some places (title generation, intent detection, etc.), so compaction seems like a natural fit.

### Additional Context

The old `vscode-copilot-chat` repo (merged into main VS Code now) had this in PR #1846:

> Model selection: Always uses GPT-4.1 or the original request's endpoint, even for Simple mode.

So yeah - it's confirmed that compaction has zero model selection logic today. It just inherits whatever you picked for chat, which is exactly the issue.
canonical: Context compaction always uses the same model as the active chat instead of allowing a separate cheaper model, causing unnecessary use of expensive tokens for summarization.

title: Allow users to hide extension-contributed `viewsWelcome` content (welcome view)
document:
Allow users to hide extension-contributed `viewsWelcome` content (welcome view)

### Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No (it is caused by extension contributions)

## Summary

There is currently no user-facing way to hide or suppress the welcome content that extensions contribute via the `viewsWelcome` contribution point, short of disabling/uninstalling the extension entirely.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Install an extension that contributes `viewsWelcome` to a built-in view. For example, the **TypeSpec** extension contributes:
 
2. Open an empty window (no folder open).
3. The "Create TypeSpec Project" welcome content is rendered inside the built-in **Explorer** view, even for users who do not use TypeSpec.

## Problem

- The welcome content attaches to a **built-in view** (e.g. `explorer`), not to a contributed view. So the existing "hide a view" gesture from #35462 does **not** apply.
- Visibility is controlled solely by the extension author's `when` clause. The end user has no override.
- The only workarounds are disabling the extension or editing its `package.json` (reverts on update).

This is a distinct gap from #35462, which addressed hiding contributed **views/panels**, not `viewsWelcome` **content**.

## Proposed solutions (any one would help)

1. A context-menu action ("Hide welcome content") on contributed `viewsWelcome` items, persisted globally.
2. A setting to suppress `viewsWelcome` by extension id, e.g. `workbench.view.welcome.disabled: ["typespec.typespec-vscode"]`.
3. A general toggle to hide all extension-contributed welcome content on built-in views.

## Why core, not per-extension

If N extensions contribute welcome content, each would have to implement its own opt-out (most do not). Solving it once in VS Code covers all of them.
canonical: Users cannot hide extension-contributed welcome content shown in built-in views because only the extension’s own visibility condition controls it and there is no user override.

title: Windows 微软双拼输入法在EditContext API启用时只能输入拼音，无法转换为汉字
document:
Windows 微软双拼输入法在EditContext API启用时只能输入拼音，无法转换为汉字

### 问题描述
在Windows系统上使用微软拼音输入法的双拼模式时，当VSCode启用了"Editor: Edit Context"选项（默认启用），输入中文只能显示原始拼音字符，无法转换为汉字。关闭该选项后恢复正常。

### 复现步骤
1. 打开Windows系统设置，添加微软拼音输入法并切换到双拼模式
2. 打开VSCode 1.89及以上版本
3. 确保设置中"Editor: Edit Context"选项已勾选（默认状态）
4. 在编辑器中切换到微软双拼输入法，输入任意中文拼音

### 预期结果
输入拼音后应该显示汉字候选框，按空格可以上屏汉字

### 实际结果
只能输入原始拼音字符，没有汉字候选框，按空格直接上屏拼音

### 系统信息
- VSCode版本：（VSCode会自动填写）
- 操作系统版本：Windows 11 22H2 / Windows 10 22H2（根据你的实际情况修改）
- 微软拼音版本：（可以在微软拼音设置->关于中查看）

### 已尝试的解决方法
- 关闭硬件加速：无效
- 禁用msUseImeApi：无效
- 重启TextInputHost.exe：无效
- 启用微软拼音旧版兼容模式：无效
- 关闭EditContext API：有效

### 附加信息
这个问题只影响微软拼音的双拼模式，全拼模式正常。其他第三方输入法（如搜狗、百度）也正常。
canonical: Enabling the new text input API breaks Microsoft Pinyin in double-pinyin mode so the editor only receives raw keystrokes instead of IME composition and candidate selection, causing typed pinyin to be committed instead of converted to Chinese characters.

title: Agents Window Agent Harness with BYOK Support
document:
Agents Window Agent Harness with BYOK Support

Summary

Enable the VS Code Agents Window to support BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) models and custom agents, with a usable agent harness for local development and testing.

Problem
The Agents Window currently does not:

Show or allow selection of BYOK models
Recognize models configured via Chat: Manage Language Models
Support full testing of custom agents with external LLM providers

There are similar feature requests that have been marked as closed; however, the only related change appears to be the addition of the local CLI in VS Code Insiders, which does not provide a working solution for using BYOK models with custom agents in the Agents Window.

Why This Matters
Without this, it is not possible to fully build, test, or validate custom agents using real model behavior outside of the Chat experience.
canonical: The Agents Window does not support selecting or using bring-your-own-key models, so custom agents cannot be fully tested there with external language model providers.

title: For port forwarding in WSL, use `localhost` rather than an internet address or `192.168`.

Can't turn on Chat Session Sync (`chat.sessionSync.enabled`)

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 1.0.209
- VS Code Version: 1.123.0
- OS Version: Windows 11 Pro (10.0.26200)

1. Open Command Palette
2. Type and select "open user settings"
3. Type `chat.sessionSync.enabled` in the search field
4. Observe the setting's state (Local Index is enabled)

Actual Behavior:

title: GitHub Copilot diff missing from edits
document:
GitHub Copilot diff missing from edits

Suddenly the edits to a document by Claude Opus 4.8 are not showing a diff correctly. The model edited sections throughout the document. However:

* The entire document shows green, as if the document was blank before.
* If I select "Toggle Diff Editor for Chats Edits", it shows entire green on the right, and a blank document on the left—again as if the document was blank before.
* However if I look at the conversation and click on the "Edited [foo.md +37 -41]" notification in the middle of the response, the correct diff is shown!
* Likewise if I go to the Source Control view on the left and double-click that file, again a correct diff is shown.

Thus I can see the diff of changes in the literal "things change" and "Git difference" windows, but in the actual "what this round modified" I cannot see the individual edits. The consequence is that **this prevents me from accepting or rejecting each document edit individually**.

Perhaps because there were edits dispersed throughout the documents, the change diff algorithm thought there was one big addition? But even that doesn't make sense, because in that case should have have shown the entire document in red as deleted. But it's showing an empty document as the previous state.

I don't know how to reproduce this, but I'll watch for patterns.
canonical: Chat edits are being recorded as if the original document was empty, causing the diff view to show the whole file as newly added instead of showing the actual individual changes.

title: Navigate to previous/next session in the sessions list
document:
Navigate to previous/next session in the sessions list

## Feature Request

Provide keyboard navigation to move through the sessions list one session at a time, opening the adjacent session relative to the active one.

Previously the Agents window only offered:
- `Ctrl/Cmd+1..9` — jump to the Nth visible session
- Go Back / Go Forward — **chronological history**, not list order

There was no way to step **up/down** through the list in display order.

## Solution

Added two actions in the Agents window:

- **Navigate to Previous Session** (`sessionsViewPane.navigatePreviousSession`)
- **Navigate to Next Session** (`sessionsViewPane.navigateNextSession`)

They move through the **visible** sessions list in display order (respecting grouping, filtering, and collapsed sections), open the session adjacent to the active one, and clamp at the list edges.

### Keybindings

| | macOS | Windows / Linux |
|---|-------|-----------------|
| Previous | `Cmd+Alt+Left` or `Alt+Up` | `Ctrl+PageUp` or `Alt+Up` |
| Next | `Cmd+Alt+Right` or `Alt+Down` | `Ctrl+PageDown` or `Alt+Down` |

These mirror core "Previous/Next Editor" and browser tab switching. All bindings are gated on the Agents window and outside the editor area so the editor's "Move Line Up/Down" stays intact, while navigation still works from the chat input.

## Verification Steps

1. Open the Agents window with several sessions in the list.
2. Open a session in the middle of the list.
3. Press `Ctrl+PageDown` (macOS: `Cmd+Alt+Right`) → the **next** session in the list opens and is selected.
4. Press `Ctrl+PageUp` (macOS: `Cmd+Alt+Left`) → the **previous** session opens.
5. Repeat using `Alt+Down` / `Alt+Up` — same behavior.
6. Navigate to the **last** session, press Next again → selection stays on the last session (clamps, no wraparound).
7. Navigate to the **first** session, press Previous again → selection stays on the first session.
8. Confirm grouping/filtering is respected: collapse a section or apply a filter, then navigate — only **visible** sessions are traversed in the order shown.
9. Focus the **chat input** and press `Alt+Up`/`Alt+Down` → session navigation still works (the composer has no "Move Line" behavior).
10. Open a file in the modal **editor area** and press `Alt+Up`/`Alt+Down` → the editor's **Move Line Up/Down** runs (session navigation does not trigger).
11. Verify both commands appear in the Command Palette as "Navigate to Previous Session" / "Navigate to Next Session".

Associated PR: #320330
canonical: The Agents window lacked keyboard commands to move to the previous or next visible session in the session list order, causing users to jump only by history or by direct selection instead of stepping through adjacent sessions.

title: Github Copilot: Sudden disappearance of Codex model and full monthly AI credit consumption in one evening
document:
Github Copilot: Sudden disappearance of Codex model and full monthly AI credit consumption in one evening

I have been using GitHub Copilot regularly since August, and during all these months I have never once used up my full monthly AI credit quota — even when working on more complex tasks and intentionally using the Codex model, which has always been my preferred option.

However, today something very unusual happened:

The Codex model suddenly disappeared from the list of available models in Copilot.

In a single evening, my entire monthly AI credit limit was consumed, despite the fact that I barely used Copilot at all.

According to my usage report, multiple models were invoked automatically (e.g., Auto: GPT‑5 mini, Auto: GPT‑5.4 mini, Gemini 3.1 Pro, etc.).

To make matters worse, Copilot did not even complete the task I asked it to do today, despite consuming all credits.

I have never experienced anything like this in all the months of using Copilot.

The sudden disappearance of Codex and the unexpected background consumption of my entire quota strongly suggest a malfunction or unintended behavior.

I would like to request:

An explanation of why Codex disappeared from my available models.

An investigation into why Copilot consumed my entire monthly quota in one evening.

A restoration of the credits consumed on this day, as they were not used intentionally.

Guidance on how to prevent Copilot from consuming credits in the background without user interaction.
canonical: Copilot suddenly stopped offering Codex and appears to have switched to automatic model usage that rapidly consumed the monthly AI quota in the background without completing the requested task.

title: Add setting to open single-file diff from the Changes view
document:
Add setting to open single-file diff from the Changes view

Implemented via #319701.

## Feature

Adds the `sessions.changes.openSingleFileDiff` setting (boolean, default `false`, preview). When enabled, clicking a file in the **Changes** view of the Agents window opens a focused single-file diff editor instead of the multi-file diff editor.

## Verification steps

1. Open the Agents window with a session that has changes (added / modified / deleted files) in the **Changes** view.
2. With the setting **off** (default), click a file in the Changes view.
 - ✅ The multi-file diff editor opens (existing behavior).
3. Enable `sessions.changes.openSingleFileDiff`.
4. Click an **added**, a **modified**, and a **deleted** file in the Changes view.
 - ✅ Each opens a focused **single-file diff** editor handling the respective case.
5. With the setting enabled, check the inline toolbar on a changed file.
 - ✅ The redundant "Open Changes" alt action is hidden (single diff is already the default click).
6. Toggle the setting off again and confirm the alt action reappears live (no reload).
canonical: Clicking a changed file in the Changes view should open a single-file diff editor when the new setting is enabled, but currently it opens the multi-file diff instead because the click action is still wired to the old behavior.

title: Add editor part chevron to toggle the secondary side bar in the Agents window
document:
Add editor part chevron to toggle the secondary side bar in the Agents window

Implemented via #319987.

## Feature

Adds a chevron toggle to the editor part title bar (right of the tabs) in the Agents window to quickly widen the editor by collapsing the secondary side bar (auxiliary bar).

## Verification steps

1. Open the Agents window and open a file so the editor is shown **inside the window** (not as a modal) with the secondary side bar visible.
2. In the editor title bar (right of the tabs), click the **chevron-right** ("Push Editor Right") action.
 - ✅ The secondary side bar is hidden and the editor widens.
3. The same slot now shows a **chevron-left** ("Show Secondary Side Bar") action. Click it.
 - ✅ The secondary side bar is restored.
4. Verify the chevron direction flips correctly based on auxiliary bar visibility.
5. Verify the action is only shown for the Agents window editor (main editor group, grid mode) and not when the editor is modal.
canonical: The Agents window editor is missing a toggle chevron in its title bar to collapse and restore the secondary side bar, so users cannot quickly widen the editor when the auxiliary bar is visible.

title: Session header should show animated progress indicator like the sessions list
document:
Session header should show animated progress indicator like the sessions list

Implemented via #319995.

## Enhancement

The session header showed a static codicon for in-progress / needs-input sessions, while the sessions list showed an animated pixel spinner. The header now renders the same animated progress indicator as the list, with reduced-motion support.

## Verification steps

1. Open the Agents window and start a session so it is **in progress**.
2. Observe the status indicator in the **session header** (top of the session view).
 - ✅ It shows the animated pixel spinner (grid variant), matching the spinner shown in the **sessions list** for the same session.
3. Trigger a state where the session **needs input** (e.g. a confirmation/approval).
 - ✅ The header shows the animated ring-variant spinner, matching the list.
4. Enable OS "reduce motion" (or `workbench.reduceMotion`) and repeat.
 - ✅ The header falls back to the static codicon (no animation) and updates live when the reduced-motion setting changes.
canonical: The session header used a static status icon instead of the animated progress indicator shown in the sessions list for in-progress or needs-input sessions, likely because the header was not reusing the same spinner rendering logic as the list.

title: Hidden secondary side bar should stay hidden when switching back to a session
document:
Hidden secondary side bar should stay hidden when switching back to a session

Fixed via #320075.

## Bug

In the Agents window, when a user hides the secondary side bar (auxiliary bar) for a session, switching away and back to that session would re-reveal the side bar, overriding the session's saved "hidden" state. This happened because restoring the session's editor working set reveals the editor part programmatically, which re-triggered the "editor implies auxiliary bar" invariant.

## Verification steps

1. Open the Agents window with at least two sessions, each having a file/editor open (e.g. open a file from files view so the editor + secondary side bar both show).
2. In session A, **hide** the secondary side bar (auxiliary bar).
3. Switch to session B, then switch **back** to session A.
 - ✅ The secondary side bar should remain **hidden** in session A.
4. In session B, keep the secondary side bar **visible**, switch to A and back to B.
 - ✅ The secondary side bar should remain **visible** in session B.
5. Open a file from files view in any session.
 - ✅ The editor and the secondary side bar should both be shown (invariant preserved for genuine editor reveals).
canonical: Switching back to a session should preserve its saved hidden state for the secondary side bar instead of re-showing it when the session’s editor view is restored.

title: Test: Sessions recency history — picker & back/forward navigation
document:
Test: Sessions recency history — picker & back/forward navigation

Refs: #320315

- [x] anyOS @ulugbekna
- [x] anyOS @bamurtaugh

Complexity: 3

Authors: @sandy081

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23320316%0A%0A&assignees=sandy081)

---

The Agents window now maintains a single source of truth for session recency (most-recently-opened first), persisted across reloads, and uses it to power the **Sessions Picker** and **Back/Forward navigation**. Read state and the status icon are now consistent across the sessions list, the sessions picker, and the session header.

## Test steps

### 1. Recently opened ordering
- Open a few sessions in sequence (A, then B, then C).
- Open the Sessions Picker (`Ctrl+R` / `Cmd+R`).
- Verify the **recently opened** group lists them most-recently-opened first (C, B, A), and the **other sessions** group lists never-opened sessions sorted by last update.

### 2. De-duplication
- Use a **Copilot CLI session started from Copilot Chat** (required to have multiple chats within a single session).
- Open two different chats within that same session.
- Re-open the picker and verify the session appears **only once** in "recently opened".

### 3. Active session pre-selected
- With a session active, open the picker and verify that session is the highlighted/selected item.

### 4. Search across title and folder
- Type part of a session **title** → it filters.
- Type part of a **folder/workspace** name → matching sessions still appear (matches on the second row).

### 5. Keyboard
- `Enter` opens the selected session and closes the picker.
- `Cmd/Ctrl+Enter` (or `Alt+Enter`) opens the session **to the side** (new grid slot next to the active session).
- `Right Arrow` opens the session in the **background** and the picker **stays open** so you can keep navigating.
- `Escape` dismisses the picker.

### 6. Back/Forward navigation
- Commands: **Sessions: Go Back** and **Sessions: Go Forward** (also available as the back/forward arrows in the session header).
- Open three sessions in order: **A**, then **B**, then **C** (C is now active).
- **Go Back** once → lands on **B**. **Go Back** again → lands on **A**.
- **Go Forward** once → back to **B**.
- From **B**, now open a *different*, older session **D** (i.e. go somewhere new instead of forward).
- **Go Back** → should land on **B** (where you just were), and continuing **Go Back** should still reach **A** and **C** — i.e. opening **D** did **not** discard **C** the way browser history would. Order is most-recently-visited first (Alt+Tab-style, no truncation).

### 7. Persistence across reload
- Reload the window (Developer: Reload Window).
- Open the picker and verify "recently opened" still reflects prior recency; Back navigation still works.
canonical: The sessions picker and back/forward navigation are inconsistent because session recency is not preserved as a single most-recently-used history, causing duplicate or misordered entries and incorrect navigation after opening other sessions or reloading.

title: Sessions: unified recency history powering the sessions picker and navigation
document:
Sessions: unified recency history powering the sessions picker and navigation

Implemented via #319991.

## Feature

The Agents window now maintains a single source of truth for session recency (most-recently-opened first), persisted across reloads, and uses it to power the **Sessions Picker** and **Back/Forward navigation**. Read state and the status icon are now consistent across the sessions list, the sessions picker, and the session header.

### What changed
- New `SessionsRecencyHistory`: MRU-ordered, de-duplicated `(session, chat)` history, persisted per workspace.
- **Sessions Picker** (`Ctrl/Cmd+R` in the Agents window): two groups — **recently opened** and **other sessions**; search across title and folder; pre-selects the active session; supports open-to-side and background-open keyboard modifiers.
- **Back/Forward navigation** rebuilt on the shared history (MRU/Alt+Tab semantics, survives reloads).
- Session header read-state/status icon now matches the sessions list (no more "read in list, unread in header/picker").
- Removed the hover on the command-center title widget.

## Verification steps

1. **Recently opened ordering**
 - Open a few sessions in sequence (A, then B, then C).
 - Open the Sessions Picker (`Ctrl+R` / `Cmd+R`).
 - Verify the **recently opened** group lists them most-recently-opened first (C, B, A), and the **other sessions** group lists never-opened sessions sorted by last update.

2. **De-duplication**
 - Use a **Copilot CLI session started from Copilot Chat** (required to have multiple chats within a single session).
 - Open two different chats within that same session.
 - Re-open the picker and verify the session appears **only once** in "recently opened".

3. **Active session pre-selected**
 - With a session active, open the picker and verify that session is the highlighted/selected item.

4. **Search across title and folder**
 - Type part of a session **title** → it filters.
 - Type part of a **folder/workspace** name → matching sessions still appear (matches on the second row).

5. **Keyboard**
 - `Enter` opens the selected session and closes the picker.
 - `Cmd/Ctrl+Enter` (or `Alt+Enter`) opens the session **to the side** (new grid slot next to the active session).
 - `Right Arrow` opens the session in the **background** and the picker **stays open** so you can keep navigating.
 - `Escape` dismisses the picker.

6. **Back/Forward navigation**
 - Commands: **Sessions: Go Back** and **Sessions: Go Forward** (also available as the back/forward arrows in the session header
 - Open three sessions in order: **A**, then **B**, then **C** (C is now active).
 - **Go Back** once → lands on **B**. **Go Back** again → lands on **A**.
 - **Go Forward** once → back to **B**.
 - From **B**, now open a *different*, older session **D** (i.e. go somewhere new instead of forward).
 - **Go Back** → should land on **B** (where you just were), and continuing **Go Back** should still reach **A** and **C** — i.e. opening **D** did **not** discard **C** the way browser history would. Order is most-recently-visited first (Alt+Tab-style, no truncation).

7. **Persistence across reload**
 - Reload the window (Developer: Reload Window).
 - Open the picker and verify "recently opened" still reflects prior recency; Back navigation still works.
canonical: The sessions picker and back/forward navigation were using inconsistent or non-persistent session recency state, causing recently opened sessions and navigation history to be ordered incorrectly, duplicated, or lost across reloads and across different views.

title: Restore sessions on reload in the Agents window
document:
Restore sessions on reload in the Agents window

## Feature: Restore sessions on reload (Agents window)

When the Agents window is reloaded or reopened, the previously visible agent sessions and their state are restored automatically, so the user lands back where they left off.

### What gets restored
- **Visible sessions grid** — which sessions were open, their left-to-right order, the active session, and sticky/pinned sessions.
- **Per-session layout** — auxiliary bar visibility, active view container, and editor working sets (open editors per session).
- **Sessions list UI state** — pinned and read status of sessions.

### How it works
- On startup, `Workbench.restore()` calls `SessionsViewService.restoreVisibleSessions()` after `LifecyclePhase.Restored`.
- `SessionsViewService` persists visible session states (`agentSessions.activeSessionStates`, workspace-scoped) via `onWillSaveState()` and reloads them via `_loadSessionStates()`. The grid is rebuilt atomically in a single transaction (`VisibleSessions.restoreGrid()`), waiting for providers to surface persisted session resources (30s timeout).
- `LayoutController` persists/loads per-session layout (`sessions.layoutState`).
- `SessionsListModelService` persists pinned/read status.

### Key files
- `src/vs/sessions/browser/workbench.ts` — `Workbench.restore()`
- `src/vs/sessions/browser/sessionsViewService.ts` — `restoreVisibleSessions()`, `_loadSessionStates()`, `_saveSessionStates()`
- `src/vs/sessions/services/sessions/browser/visibleSessions.ts` — `VisibleSessions.restoreGrid()`
- `src/vs/sessions/contrib/layout/browser/sessionLayoutController.ts` — `LayoutController`
- `src/vs/sessions/services/sessions/browser/sessionsListModelService.ts` — `SessionsListModelService`

## Verification steps

Verify progressively, from the simplest case to the most complex. After each step, **Developer: Reload Window** and confirm the expected restore.

### 1. Single session (no editors)
1. Open the Agents window and open a single agent session.
2. Reload the window.
3. **Expected:** The session reappears and is active.

### 2. Single session with editors
1. With a single session open, open a couple of editors in it
2. Reload the window.
3. **Expected:** The session reappears with its editors restored

### 3. Multiple sessions (no editors)
1. Open multiple agent sessions side by side. Reorder them, make one sticky/pinned, and select a specific session as active.
2. Reload the window.
3. **Expected:** All sessions reappear in the same left-to-right order, the previously active session is active, and sticky/pinned sessions remain pinned.

### 4. Multiple sessions with editors
1. With multiple sessions open, open files/diffs in one session
2. Reload the window.
3. **Expected:** All sessions reappear with order, active session, sticky pins, files/diff editor in active session are restored.
canonical: The Agents window does not reliably restore previously open sessions, their order, active selection, pins, and per-session layout and editor state after a reload or reopen, likely because the persisted visible-session and layout state is not being saved or reapplied correctly during startup.

title: test: advanced autopilot in local agent harness
document:
test: advanced autopilot in local agent harness

- [x] anyOS @jukasper
- [x] anyOS @hediet
- [x] anyOS @TylerLeonhardt

Complexity: 3

Authors: @justschen

[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23320313%0A%0A&assignees=justschen)

---

This iteration, we added **Advanced Autopilot** (aka "goal mode", but with the intention to add more stuff, such as a true LLM delegated tool approval loop). In standard Autopilot, the agent decides it's finished by calling `task_complete`. With Advanced Autopilot enabled, `task_complete` is **ignored** as a stop signal. Instead, after every Autopilot turn, we run a classifier (a utility model) that looks at a chat transcript and decides whether the request has actually been satisfied:

- **YES** → the goal is met, so the loop stops.
- **NO** → the agent keeps working, using the evaluator's reason as guidance for the next turn.
- **IMPOSSIBLE** → the goal can never be satisfied in this session (self-contradictory, missing capability, exhausted approaches), so the loop stops instead of churning forever.

While Autopilot continues, the progress spinner surfaces the evaluator's reason (e.g. _"Autopilot: continuing — tests are still failing"_) so you can see *why* it kept going. There are safety caps so a stalled loop can't run away.

This is implemented in the local agent harness (default Copilot agent + the Agents app). **DOES NOTE WORK YET WITH CLI OR LOCAL AGENT HOST**

## Settings
- `chat.autopilot.advanced.enabled` — turns on Advanced Autopilot. **Off by default** (tagged `experimental`). This only takes effect when the request is running in the **Autopilot** permission level.

## Testing
1. Set `chat.autopilot.advanced.enabled` to `true`.
2. Put the session into the **Autopilot (Preview)** permission level or set `chat.permissions.default` to `autopilot` and start a new session.
3. Give the agent a multi-step task where it's tempting to stop early, e.g. _"Add a function `foo`, then run the unit tests, and fix anything that fails."_
4. Observe that even after the model believes it is done (where it would normally call `task_complete` and stop), Advanced Autopilot keeps going if the evaluator decides the original request isn't fully satisfied.
5. Watch the progress spinner — when it continues, it should show the evaluator's short reason (e.g. _"Autopilot: continuing — …"_). When it has no reason it falls back to _"Autopilot: verifying task is done…"_.
6. Confirm it **stops** cleanly once the original request is genuinely satisfied (the evaluator returns YES).
7. Test the **impossible** path: give it a self-contradictory or unachievable request (e.g. _"Make this test both pass and fail at the same time"_). Autopilot should give up rather than looping forever.
8. Confirm there's no runaway loop — after repeated non-productive turns it should hit the safety cap and stop on its own. (The overall run is still bounded by the normal tool-call limit.)
9. As a control, turn `chat.autopilot.advanced.enabled` back **off**, stay in Autopilot, and verify the old behavior: the agent stops when it calls `task_complete`.

## Notes
- Advanced Autopilot only changes the *completion decision*; it does not change tool auto-approval. You still need to be in the Autopilot permission level for tools to be auto-approved.
- Let me know if you see the loop stop too early (goal clearly not met) or keep going after the goal is obviously satisfied — those are the most interesting failure modes for the evaluator.
- Also flag any odd spinner/reason rendering or performance issues.
canonical: Advanced Autopilot can fail to stop when the task is actually complete or stop too early because the evaluator’s completion decision is being applied incorrectly in the local agent harness.

title: Retain selected session type in the agents window across window reload
document:
Retain selected session type in the agents window across window reload

## Feature

Retain the previously selected **session type** in the agents window across window reloads. Previously, reloading the window always reverted the session type to **Copilot CLI from the Copilot Chat provider**, regardless of what the user had last used (e.g. the Local Agent Host's `copilotcli`).

Implemented in #320084.

## What changed

- The session type picker now persists `{ providerId, sessionTypeId }` on **every** effective change of the active session's type (not just on manual dropdown picks), so an auto-selected/defaulted type survives reload. `providerId` disambiguates providers that advertise the same session type id.
- `NewChatWidget` no longer pre-validates the stored pick against the folder's currently-available types (which discarded a valid preference whose provider had not yet registered). It now creates the draft immediately with the best available provider and **upgrades it in place** once the preferred provider registers (driven by `onDidChangeSessionTypes`), with no timeout — agent hosts can connect arbitrarily late.

## Verification steps

1. Open the agents window and start a new session using the **Local Agent Host → Copilot CLI** session type (the agent host, not the Copilot Chat provider).
2. Reload the window (`Developer: Reload Window`).
3. ✅ Verify the new-session view still shows **Copilot CLI from the Local Agent Host** — not Copilot CLI from the Copilot Chat provider.
4. Manually switch the session type to a different one (e.g. Copilot Chat), reload again.
5. ✅ Verify the last manually-selected type is retained after reload.
6. Select other harnesses offered by the **Copilot Chat provider** (e.g. the various session types it advertises), reloading the window after each selection.
7. ✅ Verify each selected Copilot Chat provider harness is retained across reload.
canonical: The agents window lost the previously selected session type on reload because the chosen provider and session type were not persisted correctly and the stored selection was rejected before all providers had registered.

I think it would be much more useful if VS Code allowed users to define both foreground and background colors, and also reference colors from the active theme, for example:

Alternatively, VS Code could support relative color adjustments based on the current theme colors, such as:

This would make whitespace characters clearly visible regardless of the selected theme, without requiring users to manually configure colors for each theme they use.

A good example is shown in the screenshot:

It would also be nice if more control characters were displayed.
canonical: Whitespace characters are hard to see on many themes because their appearance is too limited, with only a fixed foreground color available instead of customizable foreground, background, and theme-aware colors.

title: [Remote-SSH] Activity Bar icons invisible after connecting to remote host (regression in 1.123.0)
document:
[Remote-SSH] Activity Bar icons invisible after connecting to remote host (regression in 1.123.0)

## Description
After updating to VS Code 1.123.0, the Activity Bar icons (Explorer, Search, Extensions, etc.) become completely invisible (black) after connecting to a remote host via Remote-SSH. The icons are visible before connecting, and become invisible immediately after the SSH connection is established.

Downgrading to 1.122.1 resolves the issue completely.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open VS Code 1.123.0 on Windows
2. Connect to a remote host via Remote-SSH
3. Observe that Activity Bar icons are invisible (black squares)

## Expected Behavior
Activity Bar icons should be visible when connected to a remote host via Remote-SSH.

## Actual Behavior
Activity Bar icons are invisible (appear black) after Remote-SSH connection is established.

## Environment
- VS Code Version: 1.123.0 (regression), 1.122.1 (works fine)
- OS: Windows 10/11 (x64)
- Remote OS: Linux (remote host IP: 101.34.245.228)
- Theme: Dark+ (also reproduced with other themes)

## Additional Info
- Issue only occurs after Remote-SSH connection; local window is unaffected
- Tested modifying `argv.json` with various GPU-related flags (`disable-color-correct-rendering`, `disable-GPU`, `disable-hardware-acceleration`) — none resolved the issue in 1.123.0
- Moving Activity Bar to right side (`View: Move Primary Side Bar Right`) did not help
- Only workaround: downgrade to 1.122.1

## Screenshots
Happy to provide screenshots if needed.
canonical: Remote-SSH in the newer release causes Activity Bar icons to render invisibly after connecting because the remote window applies a broken icon coloring or rendering change.

title: Bug: fetch_webpage tool returns 'Tool not found' error
document:
Bug: fetch_webpage tool returns 'Tool not found' error

### Bug: `fetch_webpage` tool returns "Tool not found" error

**VS Code Version:** 1.123.0 (arm64) 
**OS:** macOS 
**Date:** 2026-06-07 

### Description

The `fetch_webpage` tool is listed in the Copilot Chat agent's available tool set but calling it always returns:

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Copilot Chat in VS Code (agent mode)
2. Ask the agent to fetch a webpage, e.g.: "用 fetch 搜索一下杭州天气" / "Use fetch_webpage to look up weather for Hangzhou"
3. The agent attempts to call `fetch_webpage` with valid parameters
4. Observe the error: `ERROR while calling tool: Tool not found`

### Expected Behavior

The `fetch_webpage` tool should successfully fetch the webpage content.

### Actual Behavior

Returns `ERROR while calling tool: Tool not found` even though the tool is documented in the agent's available tools and the call parameters are valid.

### Call Example That Failed

### Workaround

Using `curl` in the terminal via `run_in_terminal` works fine for the same URLs.
canonical: The agent advertises a webpage-fetching tool but invoking it fails because the tool is missing or not properly registered in the available tool set.

title: [Bug] Claude/AI Integration: File truncation during Edit/Write operation
document:
[Bug] Claude/AI Integration: File truncation during Edit/Write operation

Environment:

Application: Claude for Desktop

App Version: []

Issue Summary:
I encountered a critical file truncation issue resulting in severe data loss while using the AI Edit tool on the Claude Desktop app. The AI modified a large HTML file (dashboard_출력.html), but the write stream was interrupted prematurely due to process timeouts.

Assigned a strict system prompt prohibiting the use of Edit tools for large files to prevent truncation.

Triggered the AI to modify the large HTML file.

The AI bypassed the prompt constraint and executed the Edit tool.

The operation failed mid stream, resulting in the file being permanently truncated at 2,687,459 bytes.

The file ends abruptly, missing critical closing tags and severing multiple core functions.

Evidence and Logs:

File Size at truncation: 2,687,459 bytes

System Logs showing the exact failure mechanism:
[error] [MCP] Could not connect to MCP server Filesystem Error: UtilityProcess spawn timeout
[warn] MaxListenersExceededWarning: Possible EventEmitter memory leak detected
[LOCAL SESSION] Subagent parent not found possible out of order processing

These logs clearly indicate that the local filesystem connection crashed (spawn timeout) and process synchronization failed during the large file edit, which directly caused the truncation.

Request:
Please implement strict rollback mechanisms for failed write streams and address the memory leak and timeout issues when handling large files via the MCP Filesystem server on the Desktop application.
canonical: A large file edit through the filesystem integration can fail mid-write due to timeout or synchronization problems, leaving the file permanently truncated instead of rolling back the incomplete change.
